Externals' **invisible + lock** posture: U5 only POSTS; coins already super-only-read (externals can't see them, spec 160 U3) and the **spend lock** lands in U6b. **pgTAP 106** (19 asserts: catalog + secdef; money posture; gate super-only [director·pm·visitor 42501]; not-settled→P0001; pool 10000, ht_cut 0.2, weights senior 4/mid 2, external_factor 2 → HT 2000 (cut only, no double-dip), senior 4000, mid 2000 [share follows them — now on another project], external 2000 [flat level-blind], ungraded 0; recorded ht_coins 2000·dc_distributed 8000·dc_count 3; total minted = pool 10000; idempotent re-distribute→P0001; unknown→P0001). **Verify:** lint·typecheck·**db:test 106 files/2073/0**·db:types·test 188/1268 — all green. **U5 spec detail authored first.** **NEXT = U6a Nova shop** (shop_items + redeem + 'shop_redemption' coin_source) → U6b saver-bonus + vesting + confiscation. LESSON: reuse `post_coins` from a definer needs the SAME (super-only) gate, else 42501 cascade.

## Spec 161 U4b - project settlement engine: settle_project (ADR 0060 §3 model-b) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Migration `20260768000000_settlement_engine.sql` (sequenced **above** the concurrent spec 146 U3 equipment block `20260767*`). ADR 0060 §3 model-b: WPs bank profit as they complete; the **whole project settles ONCE at close** → `coin_pool = Σ banked WP profit × coin_multiplier` (U4a dial). Produces the pool **number** + a **frozen snapshot**; distribution = U5. **DECISION — bank-at-SETTLEMENT with a frozen snapshot (not a completion trigger):** a completion trigger fires under whoever flips the WP status (usually a site_admin on photo-approval) and would call the super/director-gated `wp_profit`/`wp_labor_sell` → **42501**, forcing an ungated refactor of shipped U3/U3b + failing the completion tx. Instead `settle_project` is an explicit super/director action **at close** that calls `wp_profit` **in its designed caller context** (gate passes) and FREEZES each completed WP's profit into `wp_profit_bank` — so post-settlement corrections can't move settled coins (U5 reads the frozen bank, never live `wp_profit`). Trade-off accepted: a correction between WP-complete and project-close IS reflected (correct); defect-rework-before-close handled naturally (a WP in 'rework' isn't 'complete' → excluded). **`settle_project(p_project) returns table(coin_pool, banked_profit_total, coin_multiplier, wp_banked_count, wp_skipped_null_budget_count, equipment_costed)`** SECURITY DEFINER · super+director null-safe `is distinct from` (no PM ref → **90/91 untouched**) → 42501; project exists → P0001; **closed-only** (`status in ('completed','archived')`) → P0001; **idempotent** (a `project_settlements` row exists → P0001 'already settled'). Loops WPs `status='complete'`, calls `wp_profit`; **budget-NULL (profit NULL) → SKIP + COUNT** (`wp_skipped_null_budget_count`, never silently 0); else **freezes** a `wp_profit_bank` row + sums; `coin_pool = total × multiplier`; audits (generic `update`). **`project_settlements`** (project_id PK = idempotency key; coin_multiplier/banked_profit_total/coin_pool/counts/equipment_costed/settled_by) + **`wp_profit_bank`** (frozen 6 wp_profit components per WP, unique per WP) — both **zero authenticated grant** (money). **Equipment gap CLOSED** by the concurrent spec 146 U3 (`wp_profit` now folds in `wp_equipment_sell`) → `equipment_costed` aggregates **true**; pool no longer provisional.

**pgTAP 104** (20 asserts: catalog project_settlements+PK / wp_profit_bank / settle_project secdef; money posture both tables; gate pm·sa·visitor→42501; closed project w/ WP-1 budget 5000+senior-full labor 800→profit 4200, WP-2 4000→4000, WP-3 complete-no-budget→SKIPPED, WP-4 in_progress→ignored, multiplier 2.0 → banked 8200·pool 16400·banked_count 2·skipped 1·equipment_costed true·2 bank rows; director settles a 2nd closed project; idempotent re-settle→P0001; active project→P0001; unknown→P0001). **Verify:** lint·typecheck·**db:test 105 files/2054/0**·db:types·**test [vitest]** — all green. **U4b spec detail authored first.** **CONCURRENCY EVENT:** the spec 146 U3 equipment session was building in the SAME tree + remote DB — collided on migration version 20260766000000 + pgTAP 104, and its `wp_profit_with_equipment` REPLACE changed the foundation. Operator confirmed (AskUserQuestion) the equipment session finished → I renumbered above it (768), repaired the orphan remote `20260766000000` (`supabase migration repair --status reverted` — my botched first-push left a phantom version whose create-table tx rolled back), and built ON the equipment-aware `wp_profit`. **First-RED bug:** seeded projects as 'completed' THEN inserted WPs → spec-145 trigger `P0002 cannot add a WP to a closed project`; fix = seed active, add WPs, then close. **NEXT = U5 distribution** (HT cut + level-weight split → coin_postings via post_coins). LESSON: `wp_profit%rowtype` fails (it's a fn, not a relation) → use `record`; a live concurrent session in one tree/DB = the [[concurrent-session-hazard]] (check list_sessions, renumber, migration-repair the orphan).

## Spec 146 U3 - equipment usage logs: check-out/check-in → per-WP charge → wp_profit (ADR 0055 + ADR 0060 §2) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20** (commits ba8a52d feat · 115d6ab director-fix · 6ed8af0 types; migrations 20260767000000–000400). Closes the spec 161 U3b equipment gap (`equipment_cost=0`/`equipment_costed=false`): attribute equipment to a WP via a **check-out/check-in usage log**, derive a **per-WP equipment charge live**, wire into `wp_profit`. **Verify:** lint·typecheck·test **188/1268**·db:push (5 migrations)·db:types (app+worker)·**db:test 105 files (104 passed; my 105=41/41, 102/90/91/86 all green)** — the **1 failure is `104-settlement-engine.test.sql`, the CONCURRENT session's in-progress file, NOT mine** (its closed-project seed bug; not in any of my commits). **Operator picks (this session, via AskUserQuestion):** (1) granularity = **check-out → check-in SPAN** (one row/checkout, billed whole days on site × daily rate), NOT row-per-day; (2) the WP is charged the **CHARGE-OUT daily rate computed LIVE** (transfer price, symmetric with DC labor @ SELL; PRC keeps the margin over the batch cost — Case A + ADR 0060 §2), NOT a GL-allocated batch-cost share. **This REVISES the roadmap-table U3/U4 sketch** (was per-day log + frozen `wp_equipment_costs`): the live `wp_profit` reuses live reads (`wp_labor_sell`), never the frozen snapshot, so the frozen `wp_equipment_costs`/`freeze_wp_equipment_cost` (old U4) is deferred to budget-vs-spend + a WP-dimensioned equipment GL posting (later seam). **No new GL account this unit** — the charge-out is a number the GL doesn't hold (GL holds batch COST at batch grain), so computing it live from the usage logs is the transfer-price layer, NOT a second costing path (exactly like `wp_labor_sell` vs the GL's labor COST).

**Builds:** `equipment_usage_logs` (append-only, supersede; `item_id`/`work_package_id` WP-grain/`checked_out_on`/`checked_in_on` null=open/`daily_rate_snapshot` MONEY zero-grant/`superseded_by`/`correction_reason` nullable — NO `reason-iff-superseded` CHECK since check-in is the first supersede; CHECK in≥out; column-scoped SELECT omitting the rate, like `labor_logs`; append-only block trigger) · `check_out_equipment(item,wp,date)` (gate site_admin/pm/procurement/super; per-item advisory lock; item must be priced + WP not complete + no existing open checkout for the item → P0001) · `check_in_equipment(log,date)` (closes the open row via a superseding closed successor; in<out → P0001; re-check-in of closed/superseded → P0001) · `wp_equipment_sell(p_wp)` (mirror `wp_labor_sell`: super+director gate null-safe, Σ over CURRENT rows of `greatest((coalesce(checked_in_on,current_date)−checked_out_on)+1,0) × daily_rate_snapshot` — whole days inclusive, open accrues to today, no internal/external branch) · `wp_profit` REPLACE (same signature; `equipment_cost = wp_equipment_sell`, `equipment_costed = true`). pgTAP **file 105** (104 taken by a concurrent Spec 161 U4b settlement-engine session — renumbered; my migrations likewise 20260766→20260767 around their pushed settlement_engine) + update `102-wp-profit` (`equipment_costed` false→true). Spec 146 U3 detail authored first.

**ADR 0058 fix (migration 20260767000400):** the check_out/check_in RPC gates + the `equipment_usage_logs` read policy initially named `project_manager` WITHOUT `project_director` → **pgTAP 90 + 91 (the see-all-PM completeness invariants) caught it** on the first GREEN db:test. Forward-fix migration adds `project_director` to both gates + the policy (DROP+CREATE — no CREATE OR REPLACE POLICY). Lesson re-confirmed: **any new gate/policy naming project_manager must also name project_director** ([[spec152-project-director-role]]). **Concurrent-session reconciliation:** the half-applied push (table created but unrecorded after the 20260766 collision) was repaired via `supabase migration repair --status applied 20260767000000` then push-the-rest — NOT a re-create (verified table+fn+trigger+policy+grants all present first via `supabase db query`).

## Spec 161 U4a - settlement multiplier dial: nova_dials (ADR 0060 §3 + decision a) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Migration `20260765000000_settlement_multiplier_dial.sql`. Third unit this session (operator chose "build w/ seeded default" via AskUserQuestion). ADR 0060 §3: coin pool = Σ banked WP profits × the **project multiplier** ("the most important undefined lever"). Per **build-decision (a)** (every dial = editable seeded data, tune anytime) the multiplier ships as a **seeded editable dial**, not a constant — the settlement engine (U4b) reads it. **`nova_dials`** = the one **key/value home for every economic dial** (the literal ADR-decision-a shape; HT cut % + level weights [U5] + saver-bonus rate [U6] land here as seeded rows in their units): `dial_key text PK`, `value numeric(20,4) ≥0`, updated_by/at. **MONEY/economics posture: zero authenticated grant** (operator reads via admin client behind requireRole; U4b engine reads via definer; setter is sole writer). **Seeded `('coin_multiplier', 1.0)`** — placeholder (1 baht banked profit → 1 coin point; coins=abstract points no peg, decision b); **operator MUST calibrate vs utilization before go-live** (standing markup-% open dial — flagged, not blocking: mechanism ships, number tuned). **`set_nova_dial(p_key,p_value)`** SECURITY DEFINER **super_admin-only** (operator economics, anti-favoritism §5, like sell-rate dials; null-safe `is distinct from`; **no PM ref → 90/91 untouched**) → 42501; value≥0 (P0001); **update-only on a SEEDED key** (typo'd key → P0001, no phantom dials); audits (generic `update` action, target nova_dials, payload key+old+new — no enum-add). **U4a = the dial ONLY**; the settlement math (Σ wp_profit × multiplier → pool) + banking-at-completion = **U4b**, mirrors U1-dials-before-U3-read.

**pgTAP 103** (13 asserts: catalog table+dial_key PK+value col+set_nova_dial secdef; seed coin_multiplier=1.0; money posture authenticated-no-SELECT; super tunes to 1.5 + reads back + audited once; negative→P0001; unknown key→P0001; pm·visitor→42501). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **188/1268**·db:push·**db:test 103 files/1993/0** (was 1980 → +13)·db:types (nova_dials + set_nova_dial added) — all green. **U4a spec detail authored first.** **NEXT = U4b settlement engine** (Σ wp_profit over project WPs × coin_multiplier → coin pool; banking profit at WP completion; null-budget WP handling) → U5 distribution (HT cut + level weights → `coin_postings`) → U6 shop. **U4b/go-live STILL wants operator UTILIZATION DATA** to calibrate coin_multiplier (seeded 1.0 is a placeholder). LESSON: pnpm must run from the INNER nested repo (`prc-ops/prc-ops`) — bash cwd reset to outer mid-session, ERR_PNPM_NO_IMPORTER_MANIFEST_FOUND; cd inner or `pnpm -C prc-ops`.

## Spec 161 U3b - WP profit assembly: wp_profit, materials from GL (ADR 0060 §2 + ADR 0057) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Migration `20260764000000_wp_profit.sql`. Assembles the §2 read `profit = budget − labor_sell − materials − equipment`, **exposing every term** (returns table). **`wp_profit(p_wp uuid) returns table(budget, labor_sell, materials_cost, equipment_cost, equipment_costed, profit)`** SECURITY DEFINER·stable·pinned search_path. **budget** = wp_economics.budget (NULL until PD sets → profit NULL). **labor_sell** = `wp_labor_sell(p_wp)` REUSED (definer-to-definer: caller's role resolves so its identical super/director gate passes — no re-impl). **materials_cost DERIVED FROM THE GL** (ADR 0057, NOT a re-sum of purchase_requests): `Σ(debit−credit)` on `journal_lines` for the WP on WIP account **1400**, restricted to PURCHASE-sourced entries via `coalesce(orig.source_table, e.source_table)='purchase_requests'` (`left join journal_entries orig on orig.id=e.reversal_of`) — **reversal-safe** (a reversal carries source_table 'journal_reversal' but copies work_package_id + swaps debit/credit, so an auto-corrected purchase nets via reversal_of→original's source; verified `post_purchase_to_gl` reverse-and-reposts). **Labor also debits 1400 but EXCLUDED** (source wp_labor_costs); VAT (1300) + equipment (no WP dim) naturally excluded. **profit = budget−labor_sell−materials−equipment, NULL if budget NULL.** Gate **super_admin+project_director** (no PM ref → 90/91 untouched), null-safe `is distinct from` (NULL-role denied) → 42501; unknown WP → P0001; execute revoke-public/grant-authenticated; read → no audit/enum.

**EQUIPMENT GAP (operator-confirmed via AskUserQuestion 2026-06-20 — chose "build profit w/o equipment, flag it"):** investigation (Explore agent + verified by hand) found `post_rental_batch_to_gl` ([20260743000100:176-178]) posts a rental batch to WIP 1400 at **batch grain with NO work_package_id (nor project_id)** — equipment cost is **not WP-dimensioned in the GL**, can't be derived per-WP today. Splitting a batch across a project's WPs needs a **business allocation rule** = a follow-up spec touching the equipment poster / ADR 0055 (operator decision). U3b does NOT improvise it (scope rule + "discovering a missing requirement mid-unit isn't license to expand"): **equipment_cost=0 + equipment_costed=false** so the omission is LOUD, never folded silently. U4 minting is blocked anyway → no payout rides on the partial figure. Profit is OVERSTATED by missing equipment until that follow-up.

**pgTAP 102** (18 asserts: catalog has_function+secdef; gate super+director read / pm·sa·visitor 42501; WP-A all 6 components [budget 5000·labor_sell 800·materials 1000 (labor 1400 entry EXCLUDED)·equipment 0·equipment_costed false·profit 3200]; WP-B reversal-safe materials [2000−2000+1500=1500, profit 2500]; WP-C budget-null → budget NULL + profit NULL; unknown WP P0001). Journal rows seeded directly (accounting_period 2099-12 + journal_entries/lines, gl_accounts by code) to isolate from the poster machinery. Verify: lint·typecheck·test **188/1268**·db:push·**db:test 102 files/1980/0** (was 1962 → +18)·db:types (`wp_profit` added) — all green. **U3b spec detail authored first.** **NEXT = equipment-per-WP follow-up spec** (allocation rule, gated on operator basis) + **U4 settlement × project multiplier** (gated on utilization data for markup-%/multiplier). LESSONS: reversal entries carry source_table='journal_reversal' (not the original's) but COPY work_package_id + swap debit/credit → net via reversal_of→orig.source_table; labor + materials BOTH hit WIP 1400 → must isolate by source, not account alone.

## Spec 161 U3 - WP labor priced at SELL: wp_labor_sell (ADR 0060 §2) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Migration `20260763000000_wp_labor_sell.sql`. The **novel core** of the §2 profit engine — `WP profit = budget − (equipment + DC labor @ SELL + materials)` — built as **only** the SELL-priced DC-labor term (the one number no existing fn produces; `freeze_wp_labor_cost` sums the same logs at COST). **`wp_labor_sell(p_wp uuid) returns numeric`** SECURITY DEFINER · `stable` · pinned search_path. Sums over **CURRENT** labor_logs (anti-join + tombstone filter, ADR 0009 — mirrors the cost engine) where `worker_type_snapshot='dc'` (own labor = payroll overhead, NOT transfer-priced → excluded, as it's absent from the §2 formula), valued at the worker's per-level sell rate: `(full→1, half→0.5) × (internal_sell unless wp_economics.is_external → external_sell)`; `coalesce(…,0)`. **is_external** = `coalesce((select is_external from wp_economics where wp=p_wp), false)` (no row = internal, U2 posture). **Ungraded DC → 0** — the inner join to `sell_rate_table` drops a NULL-level worker, so an ungraded DC can never silently inflate profit; **decision: contribute 0, no side-effect flag** (pure read; surfacing the ungraded-DC-day count = U3b's profit struct). **Level = live grade** (labor_logs has no level snapshot; level-at-time = later refinement, noted). **Gate super_admin + project_director only** (no project_manager ref → ADR 0058 pgTAP 90/91 untouched); **null-safe `is distinct from` form** denies NULL-role/service-role (rls-self-check-coalesce trap) → 42501; unknown WP → P0001 (typo errors, never a misleading 0). Reads zero-grant money tables (definer bypasses RLS); **invoked under the caller's authed session** (real super/director JWT so current_user_role() resolves), never the admin client — like freeze. Execute lockdown: `revoke all from public; grant execute to authenticated`. Pure read → **no audit, no enum-add**. **pgTAP 101** (12 asserts: has_function+secdef; gate super+director pass / pm·site_admin·visitor 42501; internal WP senior-full 800 + mid-half 350 = **1150**; same crew external = **1375** and `>` internal; edge WP = **290** [only junior current-half counts: ungraded→0, own-with-level excluded, superseded-old excluded, tombstone excluded]; unknown WP P0001). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **188/1268**·db:push·**db:test 101 files/1962/0** (was 1950 → +12)·db:types (`wp_labor_sell` added) — all green. **Spec U3 detail authored first** (like U1/U2/U2b). **NEXT = U3b** full profit assembly (subtract equipment [spec 141/146] + materials [purchasing] from `wp_economics.budget`, **reconcile to GL** ADR 0057 — derive from, not a second costing path) → U4 settlement × project multiplier → U5 coin distribution → U6 shop. **Open dial (flag, not blocking):** markup % still wants real **utilization data** for a solvent default — rates are editable/seeded, so the mechanism is built; operator should sanity-check sell rates vs actual billable-vs-idle DC-days before go-live.

## Spec 162 U1 - Nova operator surface (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Operator: "can we start showing something on nova?" → the **first visible Nova surface**. Everything before was backend (ledger 160 U2, dials 161) — invisible. **`/nova` operator console** (`requireRole(["super_admin"])` — coins are super_admin-read + externals-invisible ADR 0060 §4 + gift-first ADR 0061, so worker-facing Nova waits). **No DB change** (reuses `coin_postings`/`coin_balance`/`post_coins`). Reads via RLS server client (super_admin sees all postings): active workers + coin_postings (newest first) → derives per-worker **balances** (group+sum, never stored) + the recent **ledger**. **`NovaAwardForm`** (client): worker + source picker (`coin_source`, default behavior_bonus) + amount + reason → **`awardCoins`** server action (super_admin re-check → `post_coins` RPC, maps errors Thai, revalidates). Coins shown as "เหรียญ" (points, no baht peg). `COIN_SOURCE_LABEL` SSOT in `src/lib/nova/coin-source.ts` (form + ledger). **Settings hub:** the HELD "Nova" coming-soon row → real `/nova` link for super_admin (home = บัญชี section); other roles keep coming-soon. **/nova uses DetailHeader** (back chip → /settings; classified as STATIC_DETAIL drill-down). **Anti-favoritism note (ADR 0060 §5):** a manual award is discretionary = the operator's INTERIM recognition tool; the automatic earn-rules (161 U5 settlement, U6 saver bonus) are the pillar-clean path. **TDD:** `nova-award-form.test.tsx` (3: submit-gate worker+amount>0+reason; awards right args + resets + router.refresh; error inline). **Updated 2 structural guards** for the new route/folder: `feature-components-structure` (+nova domain), `nav-back-affordance` (+nova STATIC_DETAIL). Verify: lint·typecheck·**test 188 files/1268** — all green (no DB). **Verified-by-checklist** (page auth-gated super_admin+LINE → preview /login on cloud PC; form test carries the contract; operator on-device = acceptance — the operator can now award a coin + watch the balance/ledger populate). **Scope OUT:** worker-facing Nova (gift-first/Internal-invite later); automatic earn-rules (161 U5/U6); leaderboards (toxic — ADR 0060 private/rate-based). **First Nova pixels are live.**

## Spec 161 U2b - HT (Head Technician) assignment (ADR 0060) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Migration `20260762000000_project_ht.sql`. ADR 0060 §1: the **HT is a promoted DC, one per site, exclusive WP owner; the PM assigns**. `projects.ht_worker_id uuid null references workers(id)` — the single column **IS** the one-per-project rule. **Not money** (role designation) → added to the projects **per-column** SELECT grant (maintenance rule from 20260626000200 — projects grant is per-column, not table-wide; a new non-money column must be granted or the app can't read it). **`assign_project_ht(p_project,p_worker)`** SECURITY DEFINER: gate **pm+director+super** (PM assigns §1; references project_manager → director included per ADR 0058) → 42501; project exists (P0001); worker must be an **active DC** (`worker_type='dc'` AND `active` — "a promoted DC") → P0001; sets ht_worker_id (overwriting — last-wins); audits (generic `update` action, target projects, target_id project). Role-only (not membership-scoped) for v1, like U2. **pgTAP 100** (14 asserts: catalog col+type+FK; secdef; pm assigns active-DC / director overwrites last-wins; site_admin+visitor 42501; own-worker / inactive-DC / unknown-project / unknown-worker P0001; current HT = last assigned; 2 assigns audited). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **188/1268**·db:push·**db:test 100 files/1950/0**·db:types — all green. **LESSON (re-confirmed): pgTAP UUIDs are HEX-ONLY** — first run errored 22P02 on `ow…owowowow` (o/w not hex); fixed to `0a…0a0a0a0a`. **WP-profit-center SETUP now complete: budget (U2) + internal/external (U2) + HT (U2b).** **NEXT = U3 WP profit engine** (reads sell_rate_table + wp_economics.budget + labor@sell + equipment → profit) — **BLOCKED on operator UTILIZATION DATA for markup % calibration** (ADR 0060 open dial).

## Spec 161 U2 - WP economic identity: budget + internal/external (ADR 0060) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Second economics unit. Migration `20260761000000_wp_economics.sql`. The WP's profit-engine inputs, isolated so **budget keeps the MONEY posture** (the profit denominator, hidden from non-HT DCs) instead of leaking through a `work_packages` column grant — mirrors `wp_labor_costs`. **`wp_economics`** (one row per WP, upserted): `work_package_id` PK+FK (on delete cascade), `budget numeric(20,4)` null (≥0 when set), `is_external boolean not null default false`, updated_by/at. **Zero authenticated grant** (RLS on, revoke all — pm/director/super read via admin client; SECURITY DEFINER setters are the only writers). **`set_wp_budget(wp,budget)`** = **project_director + super only** (the PD sets budget, ADR 0060 §1; anti-favoritism root = benchmark-to-scope) → **no project_manager ref, so pgTAP 90/91 not triggered**; validates WP-exists + budget≥0 (P0001); upserts budget preserving is_external; audits. **`set_wp_external(wp,bool)`** = **pm + director + super** (PM classifies; **references project_manager → director included** per ADR 0058); upserts is_external preserving budget; audits. Both audit via generic `update` action (target wp_economics, target_id=wp), no enum-add. Gates **role-only** (not membership-scoped) for v1 — budget gate is see-all anyway; PM-membership on set_wp_external = later refinement. Nothing computes profit from these yet (U3). **pgTAP 99** (19 asserts: catalog table+PK+FK+cols; money posture no-auth-SELECT; set_wp_budget director+super set / pm+visitor 42501 / unknown-WP+negative P0001; set_wp_external pm sets / site_admin+visitor 42501 / unknown P0001; **upsert preserves the other column** — budget=120000 + is_external=true coexist; 3 sets audited). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **187/1264**·db:push·**db:test 99 files/1936/0**·db:types — all green. **NEXT = U2b** (HT assignment: `projects.ht_worker_id`, one per project, PM-assigned) then **U3 WP profit engine** (reads sell_rate_table + wp_economics + labor + equipment → profit; **needs operator UTILIZATION DATA for markup % calibration** — ADR 0060 open dial). **Lesson: money column on an existing table with a table-wide grant would leak — isolate WP money in a separate zero-grant table (wp_labor_costs pattern).**

## Spec 161 U1 - profit-sharing economics: per-level rate foundation (ADR 0060) (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** **ADR 0060 ACCEPTED this session** (was design-only) — operator approved the build with 3 anchors: **(a) editable dials seeded with defaults** (tune live, not hardcoded — satisfies "tune before build"); **(b) coin value = abstract points, per-item shop pricing, NO baht peg** (coins accrue as raw points; WP P&L + sell rates are baht, the separate reward layer settles from banked baht profit); **(c) narrow confiscation, vested coins are the worker's to keep** (resolves ADR 0060 §6 saver-bonus collision toward trust; ADR 0061 invariant). Spec 161 = the economics arc (U1 rate dials · U2 WP profit center+budget+is_external+HT · U3 WP profit engine · U4 project settlement×multiplier · U5 coin distribution→`coin_postings` · U6 Nova shop+saver bonus+vesting). **U1 (THIS):** migration `20260760000000_sell_rate_foundation.sql`. `worker_level` **enum** (senior/mid/junior/apprentice; ADR 0060 §1); `workers.level` nullable col (category not money → `grant select(level)` authenticated); **`sell_rate_table`** (level PK, baht `cost_band`/`internal_sell`/`external_sell` numeric(20,4), updated_by/at, nonneg check) — **MONEY posture: zero authenticated grant** (day_rate posture — operator reads via admin client behind requireRole super_admin), **SEEDED** 4 rows (senior 650/800/950 · mid 550/700/850 · junior 450/580/720 · apprentice 380/480/600 — illustrative, operator retunes). **`set_worker_level(worker,level)`** SECURITY DEFINER super_admin (grading=operator economics, anti-favoritism §5; `is distinct from` null-safe gate; reuses `worker_change` audit kind=level_change). **`set_sell_rate(level,cost,internal,external)`** SECURITY DEFINER super_admin, nonneg (P0001), updates row + audits (action `update`, target sell_rate_table, payload old/new — generic action, no enum-add). **No `project_manager` ref → pgTAP 90/91 not triggered.** Nothing READS the rates yet (U3). **pgTAP 98** (19 asserts: enum labels; workers.level col+type; table+PK; seed count=4 + senior value; money posture no-authenticated-SELECT; set_worker_level sets+audits+gate super/pm/visitor/unknown-worker; set_sell_rate updates+audits+gate+negative). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **187/1264**·db:push·**db:test 98 files/1917/0**·db:types — all green. **Lessons applied:** super_admin-only gate avoids the 90/91 invariant; `is distinct from` null-safe (rls-self-check-coalesce); zero-grant money table read-as-owner in pgTAP (can't SELECT as authenticated). **NEXT = U2** (WP budget + is_external + HT assignment). Markup % calibration still needs operator utilization data (ADR 0060 open dial).

## Spec 160 - worker-ecosystem foundation Stage 0 (ADR 0061) (2026-06-20)

Status: **U1 + U2 + U3 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20. STAGE 0 COMPLETE** (person + ledger + portal-home; economics deferred). Under ADR 0061 — the foundation spine before any economics; also the long-promised `DC → Project` correction (closes the original "site admins can't find the DCs" thread; DC belongs to a project, not a crew, per [[prc-ops-pay-model]]). Units: **U1 DC-as-person + project assignment** (add `workers.project_id` nullable FK + append-only `worker_project_moves` stream mirroring equipment_movements + `assign_worker_to_project` RPC pm/super + **drop the `workers_dc_has_contractor` force-tie** so a DC needs no contractor; additive/reversible; pgTAP 95) · U2 event-sourced coin-ledger skeleton (append-only `coin_postings`, derived balance, pluggable sources — no economics) · U3 portal as the worker's home (extend ADR 0051). **Build order (ADR 0061 decided): Stage 0 first; coin dials + economy deferred.**

**U1 detail (SHIPPED 2026-06-20):** migrations `20260756000000_worker_project_assignment.sql` (+ fix-forward `20260757000000_worker_assignment_director_gate.sql`). Adds `workers.project_id uuid null references projects(id)` (current assignment; granted to authenticated like the other non-rate columns; NO backfill — data-only follow-up); **DROPS** CHECK `workers_dc_has_contractor` (a DC may now have a null contractor; `contractor_id` column + `workers_own_has_no_contractor` kept); append-only `worker_project_moves` (`worker_id`/`project_id` nullable=unassigned/`moved_at`/`moved_by`/`reason`, RLS on, **zero write grant — RPC-only**, no update/delete, definer-owner insert bypasses); `assign_worker_to_project(p_worker,p_project,p_reason)` SECURITY DEFINER pinned search_path, gate **pm/super/director** → else 42501, sets `project_id` + appends a move row + an `audit_log` row (reuses `worker_change` action, `payload.kind='project_move'`) in one tx; worker-not-found → P0001. **No economics** (spine only). **pgTAP 95** (25 asserts: catalog col+FK; moves table append-only no UPDATE/DELETE/INSERT priv; CHECK dropped+kept; relaxed-CHECK lives_ok; RPC secdef; pm sets+appends+audits; sa/visitor 42501; super second-move history→2). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **187 files/1258**·db:push·**db:test 95 files/1860/0**·db:types — all green. **LESSONS:** (1) **project_director rides along** — ADR 0058 global invariants (pgTAP 90 "no PM-gated RPC without project_director" + 91 "no RLS policy names project_manager without project_director") broke because the spec's "pm/super" shorthand omitted director; every new PM gate/policy MUST add `project_director` — caught by db:test, fixed forward (20260757). (2) **Relaxing a CHECK breaks the test that pinned it** — pgTAP 29 asserted `throws_ok(dc-without-contractor, 23514)`; rewired to a catalog assertion that the CHECK is now dropped (no row added → preserves the later `count(workers)=4`). (3) timestamp pick: latest applied migration was `20260755000000` (specs 155-157), not the `20260752000000` an audit-action grep surfaced — `migration list` before choosing.

**U2 detail (SHIPPED 2026-06-20):** migration `20260758000000_create_coin_ledger.sql`. Realizes ADR 0061 invariants 2+3 — the event-sourced coin-ledger spine, **NO economics** (sources named not valued; no value/earn-rules/settlement/shop/vesting/dials). `coin_source` **enum** (`profit_share`/`savers_bonus`/`behavior_bonus`; pluggable via future `add value`); append-only **`coin_postings`** (`worker_id` FK / `source` / `amount numeric(20,4)` **signed** — clawback = negative posting, not an edit / `reason` nonblank≤500 / `occurred_at` / `created_by` / `created_at`; checks amount≠0 + reason; index worker+occurred_at). **Money/authority posture:** RLS on, `revoke all`, **zero write grant (RPC-only)**, SELECT policy **super_admin-only** (worker-sees-own waits for U3 portal); **self-auditing** like equipment_movements → no audit_log row, no audit_action. **`coin_balance(worker) numeric`** = `coalesce(sum(amount),0)` — **SECURITY INVOKER** so it respects the read policy (non-operator sees 0, no leak); **balance always derived, never stored**. **`post_coins(worker,source,amount,reason,occurred_at)`** SECURITY DEFINER pinned search_path, gate **`current_user_role() is distinct from 'super_admin'`** (null-safe — the rls-self-check-coalesce trap; a single `<>` would open on NULL) → 42501; validates worker-exists/amount≠0/reason (P0001); inserts with `created_by=auth.uid()`. **No `project_manager` in any gate/policy → ADR 0058 invariants (pgTAP 90/91) not triggered** (lesson applied from U1 — super_admin-only is intentional). **pgTAP 96** (28 asserts: catalog table+cols+FK+enum labels; append-only no U/D/INSERT priv; secdef + coin_balance exists; super posts→balance 100→150; negative clawback nets→120; ledger grew to 3; zero-amount/blank-reason/unknown-worker P0001; pm/sa/visitor 42501; super reads / non-operator reads 0 via RLS). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **187/1264**·db:push·**db:test 96 files/1888/0**·db:types — all green.

**U3 detail (SHIPPED 2026-06-20):** migration `20260759000000_crew_assignments_portal.sql` (read-only RPC, no table/grant change). Realizes ADR 0061 invariant 5/6 (portal = worker's home) as the **first Stage-0 step**: the external DC portal (`/portal`, ADR 0051) crew list now shows **each member's current project** (U1 `workers.project_id`) — record, not coin. **`get_my_crew_assignments()`** SECURITY DEFINER, returns `(worker_id,name,active,project_id,project_code,project_name)` for the caller's OWN crew (`workers.contractor_id = current_user_contractor_id()`, ADR 0051 binding), left-joined to `projects` — the definer join lets an external contractor read **only their assigned projects' names** past the staff-scoped projects RLS; unbound/staff caller (NULL binding) → zero rows; `revoke public/anon, grant execute authenticated` (get_my_dc_payments posture). **`loadPortalData` swaps the crew RLS query for the RPC** (fan size + concurrency unchanged); portal renders project under each crew name or "ยังไม่ได้กำหนดโครงการ" (graceful pre-backfill, like the picker). **COINS DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from the portal** — ADR 0060 §4 makes external DCs' coins **invisible** (unlock only on Internal invite) + ADR 0061 **gift-first** (first experience = record, not the opaque coin); `coin_postings` stays super_admin-only. **pgTAP 97** (10 asserts: secdef; contractor A sees only own crew + project code/name resolved; never B's crew; B sees own + null project when unassigned; unbound/staff → 0 rows). **vitest** load-portal-data (crew fan member = RPC, assignment shape). Verify: lint·typecheck·test **187/1264**·db:push·**db:test 97 files/1898/0**·db:types — all green. **Verified-by-checklist** (portal auth-gated → preview /login on cloud PC; pgTAP carries RPC security; operator on-device = acceptance).

**STAGE 0 COMPLETE (U1+U2+U3).** **Open / next (own units):** **backfill existing DCs' `project_id`** (data-only — BLOCKED on operator's DC→project mapping; lights up both the spec 158 U2 picker's in-project section AND the U3 portal crew-project line). **Worker-sees-own-coins** on the portal — deferred to the Internal-invite / policy-evolution step (ADR 0060 §4 + invariant 6); not built. **Economics** (coin value/earn-rules/settlement/shop/vesting, ADR 0060 dials) — the next major arc, all deferred per ADR 0061 build order until the dials are tuned with the operator. Spec 158 U3 (SA-adds-DC-on-spot; operator sign-off first).

## Spec 159 U1 - subcontractor vs DC terminology consistency (2026-06-20)

Status: COMPLETE (pending full-suite green + commit). Operator: "DC setting is confusing — ผู้รับเหมา/DC, ทีมงาน." Root cause: **"ผู้รับเหมา" overloaded 3 ways** — (1) general WP contractor [LEAVE, operator], (2) subcontractor `category=contractor` [firm pays its own crew], (3) DC `category=dc` [PRC pays directly] mislabeled ผู้รับเหมา on several surfaces. **Taxonomy already exists** (`contractor_category` + `contractor_subtype` enums; Contacts tabs render DC บริษัท/ประจำ/ชั่วคราว) — NO schema change. **U1:** SSOT `SUBCONTRACTOR_LABEL = "ผู้รับเหมาช่วง"` in labels.ts; rewired the 4 subcontractor surfaces to it (contacts-tabs TAB*LABEL.contractors + CONTRACTOR_FIELDS name + addLabel; contacts/crews title+h1; contacts/[type]/[id] TYPE_CONFIG label; settings crews door label+hint — kills the `ผู้รับเหมา/DC` slash-merge → `ผู้รับเหมาช่วง / DC`). DC tab/subtypes untouched (already consistent). **CAUGHT: "ผู้รับเหมา" is a SUBSTRING of "ผู้รับเหมาช่วง"** → consistency guards assert presence-of-ช่วง / absence-of-exact-merged-string, never absence-of-ผู้รับเหมา. **TDD:** source-guard `contact-subcontractor-label.test.ts` (SSOT value; 4 surfaces reference the constant; settings no longer has `ผู้รับเหมา/DC`). First test wrongly asserted the literal — but SSOT means surfaces hold the \_constant name*, so the guard checks constant usage (the stronger anti-drift check).

**Open questions (OUT — own units):** **U2 DC-side de-ผู้รับเหมา** — labor-cost-view (`dc: "ผู้รับเหมา"`, `ผู้รับเหมา (DC)`), /payroll ("ค่าแรงผู้รับเหมา"), the DC-worker **parent picker** (worker-roster-manager + workers/actions + group-workers UNKNOWN label), the DC **portal** ("พอร์ทัลผู้รับเหมา"), the role label `contractor: "ผู้รับเหมา (DC)"` all still call a DC "ผู้รับเหมา"; needs the operator's chosen DC-crew word (e.g. ทีม DC / สังกัด DC) first. General-WP "ผู้รับเหมา" stays generic (operator).

## Spec 158 U2 - project-scope the labor picker (2026-06-20)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-20.** Verify: lint · typecheck · full test **187 files / 1264 / 0** green (**no DB change** — reads the spec 160 U1 `workers.project_id` column). Cashes in [[spec160-worker-ecosystem-foundation]] U1: the WP labor picker now surfaces the DCs assigned to THIS WP's project first, instead of a flat company-wide roster — the direct payoff of the original "site admins can't find the DCs" pain. **Prioritize, don't hide (no-regression bridge):** project workers render under **"ในโครงการนี้"**, everyone else under **"ทีมงานอื่น"** (that second heading only when in-project is non-empty; else the roster renders plain = today). U1 search still spans all; selection stays keyed by worker id (survives filter + partition). **No backfill yet** → `project_id` null for existing DCs → in-project empty at first, picker looks like today, fills as PMs run `assign_worker_to_project`. **Pure** `partitionRosterByProject(roster, inProjectIds: Set): {inProject, others}` in `group-workers.ts` (splits a GroupedRoster by id membership; a mixed contractor group appears in both partitions with only its members; group order/names preserved; empty groups dropped per side — reuses GroupedRoster, no new type). **`fetch-zone-data.ts`** gains a `projectId` param + selects `workers.project_id` (not money — covered by the U1 column grant) + returns `projectWorkerIds: string[]`. **`labor-log-zone.tsx`** gains optional `projectWorkerIds` prop (default `[]` → existing tests/look unchanged); extracted a `RosterGroups` sub-component so both partitions share one render; partitions the **already-filtered** view (search ∘ partition compose). Callers: PM WP page (via `loadWorkPackageDetail` — threads `wp.project_id`; `WorkPackageDetailData.labor` gains `projectWorkerIds`, fallback `[]`, concurrency-test mock updated) + SA review page (passes `wp.project_id`). **TDD:** `labor-group-workers.test.ts` (+3 partition cases) + `labor-log-zone.test.tsx` (+3: in-project ahead of others via `compareDocumentPosition`; no heading when empty; in-project tick submits). **Verified-by-checklist** (the WP page is auth-gated → browser preview only renders /login on the cloud PC with no creds; the 6 component/pure tests carry the contract; operator on-device = acceptance). **Deferred (own units):** U3 SA-adds-DC-on-spot (role+RLS+create-RPC, must not expose `day_rate`; operator sign-off first); backfill existing DCs' `project_id` (data-only, spec 160 follow-up); destination = spec 130 DC self-report.

## Spec 158 U1 - DC labor picker search/filter (bridge) (2026-06-20)

Status: COMPLETE (pending commit). Verify: lint + typecheck + full test **186 files / 1252 / 0** green (no DB change). Operator feedback "site admins can't find the DCs" → root cause: the WP `LaborLogZone` renders a global unscoped roster (every active `workers` row company-wide, name-ordered, no project filter, no search) → the needed DC is buried. Direction (operator): "designed for us; eventually self-governance" — destination is DC self-report (spec 130 portal + `labor_logs.self_logged`; see `self-governance-doctrine` memory). This is an explicit **bridge**. **U1 only:** a pure `filterRoster(roster, query)` (substring match on worker OR contractor name, Thai-aware, contractor match keeps the whole group, empty groups dropped) + a search input in `labor-log-zone.tsx` (placeholder SSOT `ค้นหาทีมงาน`; filtering display-only, selection persists across filtering). No schema / role / money change. **TDD:** extend `labor-group-workers.test.ts` (filterRoster cases) + `labor-log-zone.test.tsx` (search hides non-matches; pre-filter selection still submitted). **Deferred (own units):** U2 project-scope the picker (workers have no project link — scope by contractor / prior labor_logs / explicit link?); U3 SA-adds-DC-on-spot (role+RLS+create-RPC, must not expose `day_rate` money); destination = spec 130 self-report.

## Spec 157 - delete an empty work package (Tier 1) (2026-06-20)

Status: COMPLETE (pending suite green + commit). ADR 0059 §3. First delete path for WPs. `delete_work_package(p_work_package_id)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC: gate pm/super/**director** + `can_see_wp()`; **refuses (P0001) if ANY child exists** (photo_logs / labor_logs / approvals / purchase_requests / work_package_members / dependencies) else deletes; writes an audit_log row (event wp_deleted + code/name). Server action `delete-actions.ts` maps P0001 → "cancel instead" message; `WpDeleteControl` (ConfirmDialog, no window.confirm) in the manager-only block, navigates to /projects/[id] on success (the WP is gone → router.refresh would 404). **TDD:** pgTAP `94` (15 asserts: catalog, FK-blocks-delete, pm empty-delete+gone+audit, super+director see-all, photo/labor/PR → P0001 + survives, sa-member+visitor role-deny, non-member pm membership-deny) + vitest `wp-delete-control` (confirm→delete→navigate; history→inline error+no-nav).

**KEY CORRECTION — the FK premise was wrong.** The investigation + ADR 0059 + spec 157 claimed `labor_logs.work_package_id` was a **bare column with no FK** (orphan risk) needing an "adjacent FK migration." **It already HAS an FK** (`references work_packages(id)`, NO ACTION → blocks WP delete with 23503; migration 20260619000300 line 22). So spec 157 dropped the FK migration — **one migration only** (the RPC). ADR 0059 + spec 157 amended in place with correction notes. The empty-guard is still essential anyway: without it, deleting a WP would CASCADE-delete its purchase_requests / members / dependencies (silent data loss) and abort on the append-only photo_logs/approvals triggers. LESSON: verify FK existence by reading the table migration, not a summary — `references` on the column line is the FK.

**Verify:** db:push (prod) + db:test (file 94) + db:types + lint + typecheck + full test — green (below).

**Open questions (OUT):** **Tier 2 cancel/restore** for WPs WITH history (`cancelled` status enum value + exclusion from lenses/progress — own spec, own enum migration); cascade/bulk delete; un-delete (gone is gone — that's why Tier 2 exists). **ADR 0059 §Consequences "labor_logs FK debt" is now void** (FK exists).

## Spec 156 - edit a work package's name (2026-06-20)

Status: COMPLETE (pending full-suite green + commit). ADR 0059 §2. `name`/`code` were create-only; this makes **name** editable (code stays immutable — cross-surface business key, deferred). Added `set_work_package_name(p_work_package_id, p_name)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC: gate pm/super/**project_director** + `can_see_wp()` membership; name trimmed, non-empty, `<= 200` chars (the exact create_work_package bound) → else 22023; no audit. Server action `name-actions.ts` + inline `WpNameControl` (single-line field + Save, toasted) in the manager-only management block beside priority/deliverable/schedule. **TDD:** pgTAP `93` (11 asserts: catalog, pm-member rename+trim, blank/over-long 22023, super+director see-all, sa-member+visitor role-deny 42501, non-member pm membership-deny 42501) + vitest `wp-name-control` (renders, Save gated on trimmed change, calls action with trimmed value, blank disables Save). **Deviations from spec doc (deliberate):** (1) **inline editor in the management block, NOT a header sheet** — consistent with the sibling controls + lower-risk; header keeps the read-only nameplate. (2) unknown WP → 42501 (membership gate first), not false. No default-param issue this time (both args always non-null). **Verify:** db:push (prod) + db:test (file 93) + db:types + lint + typecheck + full test — green (see below).

**Open questions (OUT):** edit `code` (uniqueness re-validation 23505 + cross-surface audit; pending operator sign-off, ADR 0059 §2); unified multi-field WP-settings page (not needed — per-field controls suffice).

## Spec 155 - bind a work package to a deliverable (2026-06-20)

Status: COMPLETE (pending full-suite green + commit). ADR 0059 §1. Closes the gap: `work_packages.deliverable_id` was settable ONLY by the manual seed script — no post-create write path, so in-app WPs were permanently ungrouped. Added `set_work_package_deliverable(p_work_package_id, p_deliverable_id)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC: gate pm/super/**project_director** + `can_see_wp()` membership (ADR 0056/0058, the reopen precedent); NULL = ungroup; a non-null deliverable must share the WP's project (cross-project/unknown → 22023); no audit (benign metadata). Server action `deliverable-actions.ts` + a manager-only `WpDeliverableControl` native-select on the WP-detail page (ยังไม่จัดกลุ่ม + each งวดงาน); loader gained `deliverable_id`; the project's deliverables ride alongside the loader (planner-gated, no waterfall). **TDD:** pgTAP `92` (13 asserts: catalog, pm-member bind/clear, super + director see-all, cross-project/unknown 22023, sa-member + visitor role-deny 42501, non-member pm membership-deny 42501) + vitest `wp-deliverable-control.test.tsx` (renders ungrouped+each, binds chosen id, clears to null). **Verify:** db:push (prod) + db:test **92 files / 1809 / 0** + db:types + lint + typecheck green.

**Two deviations from the spec doc (both deliberate):** (1) unknown WP → **42501** (membership gate runs first; can_see_wp is false for a missing WP — we don't disclose existence to non-members), NOT the doc's "false". (2) Took **two migrations** — `20260753000000` (RPC) then `20260753000100` (CREATE OR REPLACE adding `p_deliverable_id uuid default null`) — because supabase typegen marks a no-default arg as required non-null `string`, so the server action couldn't pass null; the default makes it optional → the house omit-to-clear idiom (set_work_package_schedule). **Lesson: a nullable RPC arg the app must pass NULL/omit needs `default null` so typegen marks it optional — author it in the first migration.** Transient `cli_login_postgres` SASL circuit-breaker hit when db:push ran concurrent with db:test — retry alone clears it; don't run two supabase CLI ops at once.

**Open questions (OUT):** `create_deliverable` (in-app งวดงาน creation — until it ships, binding is inert on in-app-created projects); deliverable at CREATE time (extend create_work_package + add-WP-sheet picker); bulk re-bind/drag UI.

## Spec 154 - coordinator read-only project view (2026-06-20)

Status: COMPLETE — shipped + pushed to prod (commit 609a0ab, origin/main 2026-06-19/20). Origin spec 143 / ADR 0056. `project_coordinator` is in PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES (reads /projects + /projects/[id]) but NOT SITE_STAFF_ROLES, so on /projects/[id] it falls through to the SA/PM main branch which links every WP row to the WP detail + renders a calendar chip — both gate `requireRole(SITE_STAFF_ROLES)`, deny coordinator → redirect → /projects (dead links / bounce). Fix = stop rendering links it can't follow, NOT widen any gate (ADR 0056 keeps WP detail + schedule SITE_STAFF-only). One predicate `canOpenWp = SITE_STAFF_ROLES.includes(ctx.role)`; WorklistRow + WorkPackageList gain optional `canOpen` (default true → existing call sites unchanged); when false the row is a non-interactive container (no Link/href/hover/focus/chevron, list-enter animation kept). Calendar chip gated on canOpenWp. Coordinator keeps the manager-grade lens list, just non-clickable; NOT routed into the procurement branch. TDD: worklist-row.test.tsx (canOpen false → no link, name/status still render; true/omitted → one WP-detail link) + work-package-list.test.tsx (canOpen false → zero row links; default → links).

**Open questions (OUT of scope, not built):** (1) Fix B = coordinator read-only WP DETAIL + schedule — contradicts ADR 0056, needs an ADR amendment first. (2) WP-detail/schedule requireRole gates stay coordinator-excluded (correct). (3) Read-only row styling beyond removing tap affordance.

## Spec 153 - desktop hub-strip coverage (HubNav on /settings + /dashboard) (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE — shipped + pushed to prod (commits fdc9855 fix + 1ea5a63 feat, on origin/main 2026-06-19). Gap: the desktop `HubNav` strip renders on only `/projects`, `/review`, `/requests`; `/settings` + `/dashboard` are primary-tab hubs with NO HubNav, and the bottom bar is `sm:hidden` → both are desktop dead-ends. Fix = render HubNav on the full hub set via a single role selector `hubNavForRole(role)` in hub-nav.tsx (mirrors `tabsForRole`), + new `ACCOUNTING_HUB_NAV` (mirrors `ACCOUNTING_TABS`: บัญชี /accounting · ตั้งค่า /settings). `/portal` stays the documented exception (own header + logout). TDD: `hubNavForRole` mapping in hub-nav.test.tsx (each served role → its set; accounting → ACCOUNTING_HUB_NAV; visitor → null) + a source guard in nav-back-affordance.test.ts (settings/dashboard/review/projects/requests page.tsx contain "HubNav"; portal does not).

**Open questions (OUT of scope, not built):** (1) `/accounting` dual-nature — it is BOTH a primary tab (in ACCOUNTING_TABS / ACCOUNTING_HUB_NAV) AND a DetailHeader back-chip page (STATIC_DETAIL in nav-back-affordance, back → /settings). Contradiction left as-is. (2) Unify BottomTabBar + HubNav into one source; migrate the 3 existing inline HubNav role-selects (/projects, /review, /requests) to `hubNavForRole`. (3) Label drift phone-tab vs desktop-strip (รอตรวจ vs รายการรอตรวจ).

## Spec 151 U1 - lazy client bundles: defer offline-queue runner (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (U1), build-verified; **first-load delta NOT numerically verifiable in this env** (see caveat). Bundle investigation: total client JS ~542kb gz; the 125kb gz chunk = supabase-js (browser client) + zod. zod is imported ONLY by `env.ts`/`env.server.ts` (env validation at import); the browser client by 9 client components — 8 route-scoped/tap-gated, but **`UploadQueueRunner` is mounted in the root `layout.tsx`**, so it drags that 125kb chunk onto EVERY page's first paint. Fix: `src/components/features/photos/upload-queue-runner-lazy.tsx` — a `'use client'` wrapper that `next/dynamic`-imports the runner with `ssr:false`; layout renders the wrapper. Safe: the runner is a background drainer for LEFTOVER offline uploads (renders null unless queued; replay idempotent ADR 0039; in-page phase-uploader unaffected), so loading it a beat after hydration is harmless. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm build` all green (exit 0). **CAVEAT — couldn't prove the win numerically:** Turbopack's prod build emits no per-route First Load JS table, and there's no runnable app here (no auth/prod URL), so the ~110kb first-paint reduction (124kb deferred − ~14kb split overhead; total bundle ticked 542→556kb because the chunk still EXISTS, just lazy) rests on the `dynamic(ssr:false)` framework guarantee, not a measurement. **Confirm on laptop:** preview + Network tab (the supabase chunk should load AFTER first paint, not block it), or add `@next/bundle-analyzer`. **Next (later, all need a runnable app to verify):** dynamic-import the 8 tap-gated uploader/sheet components per-route; Tier-2 in-page Suspense; middleware getUser; infra.

## Spec 150 U1 - streaming: route-level loading coverage (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (U1). Bigger-lever follow-up to 147/148 (measure-first concluded: DB 462→197ms + bundle 542kb gz / 169 baseline / 125 zod+supabase chunk already quantified; operator chose to skip prod-only TTFB measurement and proceed to fixes). `loading.tsx` is route-level streaming (React Suspense) — instant skeleton on client nav while the server renders. 11 routes had one; **13 dynamic data-backed routes had none → blank-until-rendered on tap.** Added an identical one-line `loading.tsx` (re-exports the existing shared `PageSkeleton`, the exact idiom of the 11 existing files — no new logic/component) to: dashboard, equipment, profile, settings, workers, contacts/{crews,customers,vendors}, projects/[projectId]/{schedule,settings}, requests/[requestId], requests/orders/[poId], requests/orders/[poId]/deliveries/[deliveryId]. Skipped static/public/redirect routes. **No new test** — presentational re-export of the already-shipped (untested-by-repo-precedent) `PageSkeleton`; the skeleton is the reused unit. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm build` (exit 0, all loading.tsx compiled; Turbopack doesn't list them in the route table — expected). Researched Next 16 streaming docs first (CLAUDE.md rule) — loading.tsx behavior confirmed. **Spec number:** streaming is 150 (149 taken by the concurrent accounting-GL spec). **Next (later units):** Tier 2 = in-page `<Suspense>` on hero pages (WP/project detail) keeping real chrome + streaming only the body via the 147 loaders; then lazy client bundles (`next/dynamic` the 125kb zod+supabase form chunk). OUT: middleware getUser, infra.

## Spec 148 - data-fetch parallelization round 2 (ranks 5-8) (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (U1-U4). Same waterfall fix as spec 147, on the four lower-traffic detail pages. All four = extracted server-only loader (root → `Promise.all` fan → dependent tail) + RED-first concurrency test (in-flight-counter stub). Behavior-preserving; same queries/columns/results.

- **U1 PO detail** (`requests/orders/[poId]`) → `src/lib/purchasing/load-po-detail.ts` (`loadPurchaseOrderDetail`). Fan: po ∥ members ∥ deliveries; tail: wp-by-id ∥ per-line amounts (both need members). Amounts stay admin-client + back-office-only (spec 106), moved into the loader. Test `load-po-detail.test.ts` (3; admin client mocked, concurrency ≥3).
- **U2 delivery detail** (`requests/orders/[poId]/deliveries/[deliveryId]`) → `src/lib/purchasing/load-delivery-detail.ts` (`loadDeliveryDetail`). Full fan of 5 (po ∥ delivery ∥ members ∥ deliveryRows ∥ proofRows, all param-keyed) → proof signed-URL tail; งวด view + proof grouping computed in the loader. Test (2; concurrency ≥5).
- **U3 schedule** (`projects/[projectId]/schedule`) → `src/lib/projects/load-schedule.ts` (`loadProjectSchedule`). Fan: project ∥ work_packages ∥ deliverables (projectId-keyed) → deps + critical-path tail. Test (2; concurrency ≥3).
- **U4 projects hub** (`projects`) → `src/lib/projects/load-hub.ts` (`loadProjectsHub`). projects → fan [row client names ∥ PM-only suggested-code rpc ∥ clients]. Smallest payoff. Test (3; concurrency ≥3 for PM). Removed the duplicate PM-block from the page.

Each page drops its now-unused fetch imports + destructures the loader bundle; pure derivations stay. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (174 files / 1158 tests) all green. **Sweep 147+148 = all 8 ranked detail-page waterfalls fixed.** Remaining levers are larger/structural — NOT data-fetch waterfalls: `<Suspense>` streaming, middleware `getUser` per-nav, client bundle/hydration, infra (Supabase Pro / cold-start). See the bigger-lever plan (next).

## Spec 147 U4 - DC-portal data-fetch parallelization (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE. **Final unit of the waterfall-fix sweep (rank 4).** The DC self-service portal (`portal`) ran 5 fully-serial reads then own-docs + bank-present (profile -> consents -> crew -> payments-rpc -> pending-bank-change -> own-docs -> bank-present-rpc), all RLS-self-scoped. Collapsed to one Promise.all fan (the 6 independent reads) -> dependent tail (own-docs read only when a profile row exists). No root entity to fetch first; the portal renders empties, so no notFound. **TDD:** `tests/unit/load-portal-data.test.ts` (RED first — module-missing) -> 3 cases: concurrency (max in-flight >=6; serial peaks at 1; stub supports `supabase.rpc`), assembled shape, own-docs skipped when profile is null (asserted via a `vi.hoisted` mock not-called). **Impl:** `src/lib/portal/load-portal-data.ts` (`loadPortalData(supabase)`, inferred return, mirrors U1-U3). **Behavior-preserving:** same queries/columns/results; all packet/consent/payment derivations stay in the page as pure logic. Page drops the `getOwnContractorDocuments` import (now in the loader) + adds `loadPortalData`. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (170 files / 1148 tests) all green.

**Sweep summary (spec 147 ranks 1-4 DONE):** four detail pages converted from serial-await waterfalls to root -> Promise.all fan -> dependent tail, each via an extracted server-only loader with a RED-first concurrency test (in-flight-counter stub; passes only when the fan overlaps). Measured (U1, prod/Singapore/warm): 462ms -> 197ms, 57%. Pattern + idiom now established (`load-detail` / `load-request-detail` / `load-portal-data`). **Follow-up (NOT this spec):** ranks 5-8 (orders/[poId], deliveries/[deliveryId], schedule, projects hub) = same fix, smaller payoff — new spec when wanted. Separate larger levers untouched: `<Suspense>` streaming (perceived latency), middleware `getUser` per nav, client bundle/hydration (81 client comps), infra (Supabase Pro / cold-start).

## Spec 147 U3 - request-detail data-fetch parallelization (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE. Third unit of the waterfall-fix sweep (rank 3, worst ratio — ~8 fully-serial hops). The request-detail page (`requests/[requestId]`) ran request -> wp -> requester name -> attachments -> signed URLs -> poRow -> po-docs -> signed URLs -> suppliers, all in series. Collapsed to root (page fetches request, keeps isValidUuid + notFound) -> one Promise.all fan (wp, display name, request attachments, poRow, po source-docs, suppliers) -> dependent tail Promise.all (the two signed-URL mints need their attachment rows). **TDD:** `tests/unit/load-request-detail.test.ts` (RED first — module-missing) -> 5 cases: concurrency (max in-flight >=5; serial peaks at 1), assembled shape, requester-name fallback (name -> email -> em-dash), suppliers gated to back-office+approved, poRow=null + no po-docs when the request has no PO. **Impl:** `src/lib/purchasing/load-request-detail.ts` (`loadRequestDetail(supabase, request, { isBackOffice })`, mirrors U1/U2). Return type inferred (not an explicit interface) to sidestep the `*_attachments_current` view-row typing. **Behavior-preserving:** same queries/columns/results; attachment splits (confirmations/invoice/reference x image/pdf/link) stay in the page as pure filters. Page drops 5 now-unused imports (SupplierOption type, mintSignedUrlsForAttachments, mintSignedUrls, PO_ATTACHMENTS_BUCKET, fetchDisplayNames); isBackOffice moved above the loader call. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (169 files / 1145 tests) all green. **Next:** U4 = DC portal (`portal`, 5 fully-serial reads, RLS-scoped to self — profile/consents/crew/payments-rpc/pendingChange independent + own-docs/bank-present tail). Last unit of the sweep; ranks 5-8 (orders/deliveries/schedule/projects-hub) = follow-up spec; `<Suspense>` streaming separate.

## Spec 147 U2 - project-detail data-fetch parallelization (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE. Second unit of the waterfall-fix sweep (rank 2). The project-detail page (`projects/[projectId]`) ran ~6-7 serial levels (project -> client+members -> names -> work_packages -> deliverables -> deps -> onboarding+sources -> templates). Collapsed to root (page fetches project, keeps the procurement early-return + notFound) -> one Promise.all fan (clients, project_members, work_packages, deliverables, + PM-only onboarding rpc / copy-from sources / wp_templates count) -> dependent tail Promise.all (display names need the lead+member ids; critical-path dependency rows need the wp ids). **TDD:** `tests/unit/load-project-detail.test.ts` (RED first — module-missing) -> 3 cases: concurrency (max in-flight >=6; serial peaks at 1; stub extended for `supabase.rpc` + head/count selects), assembled shape (PM), PM-only reads skipped for non-PM. **Impl:** `src/lib/projects/load-detail.ts` (`loadProjectDetail(supabase, project, isPmRole)`, mirrors U1). onboarding typed via `Database["public"]["Functions"]["project_onboarding_status"]["Returns"][number]` (avoids a lib->app import; structurally == the page's OnboardingStatus, so the page assignment stays cast-free). **Behavior-preserving:** same queries/columns/results, procurement branch untouched. Page drops 3 now-unused imports (fetchDisplayNames, criticalWorkPackageIds, the OnboardingStatus type) and destructures the loader bundle; presentational derivations (typeLabel, WP/deliverable mapping) stay. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (168 files / 1140 tests) all green. **Next:** U3 = request detail (`requests/[requestId]`, ~8 serial hops, worst ratio — root request then wp/names/attachments/poRow/poDocs/suppliers all independent + signed-URL tails). OUT: U4 portal, ranks 5-8 follow-up; `<Suspense>` streaming separate.

## Spec 152 U3 - project_director role: table write RLS (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (U3) — **FEATURE COMPLETE**. Final unit: adds `project_director` to every RLS policy whose role list literally names `project_manager`. **Why it matters:** some core PM actions are direct-table writes gated by RLS, NOT RPCs — most importantly **approving a work package** (an INSERT into `approvals`), so without U3 a director could not approve. Also covers deliverables/reports/work*packages/photo_logs/photo_markups/purchase_requests writes, project_members/work_package_members manage, and the master-table READS (clients/contractors/suppliers/service_providers/equipment\*/workers) that list PM directly. **Method: reconstructed the 48 policies from the LIVE catalog (`pg_policies`), appended `'project_director'::user_role` to each role `ARRAY[...]` (Postgres reverse-compiles `in()` → `= ANY(ARRAY[])`, and has no CREATE OR REPLACE POLICY, so DROP+CREATE).** Migration `20260752000000_project_director_write_rls.sql` — **48 policies / 58 role lists** widened (UPDATE policies carry PM in both USING + WITH CHECK). **NOT touched:** project-scoped SELECTs (gate on `can_see*\*`→ see-all for director since U1, never name PM literally); every super_admin-alone operator policy (no PM literal → no match). **Test (RED→GREEN):** pgTAP`91`— completeness catalog (per-field: no policy names PM without director; 48 before → 0 after), behavioural lives_ok (director inserts an approval + updates a work_package directly on an UNINVOLVED WP — see-all + role), negative control (visitor still denied → 42501). No TS change. db:push applied to prod; db:test 91 files / 0 fail. **project_director is now a complete see-all project_manager: sees everything, every RPC action, every direct-table write.** Built in isolated worktree off`main`@#77.

## Spec 152 U2 - project_director role: RPC action gates (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (U2). Adds `project_director` to every SECURITY DEFINER RPC whose `current_user_role()` gate already admits `project_manager` — so a director performs every PM action (create project/WP, record purchase, post journal, retention, WHT, equipment, contractor, etc.) across all projects. **Method: sourced the LIVE definitions via `pg_get_functiondef` (the DB is authoritative — no hand-copying 47+ bodies), appended `'project_director'` to each role list with a regex transform, emitted one migration `20260751000000_project_director_rpc_gates.sql` of CREATE OR REPLACE.** **55 functions / 55 gates** (investigator estimated 47; the live filter found 55 — more complete). Shape breakdown: 32× `not in ('project_manager','super_admin')`, 9× +site*admin, 6×/3× +procurement, 2× +accounting (gl_trial_balance/gl_reconciliation), 3× `in (...)` allow-forms (record/revoke_contractor_consent, add_contact_document). **Deliberately untouched:** `can_see_project` (its membership branch lists PM but director passes via the see-all branch, U1); `set_accounting_period_status`'s SECONDARY super-only sub-gate (`not v_is_super` for reopening a closed period — a director still cannot reopen closed periods); the GL posters/plumbing (`post*\*\_to_gl`, `drain_gl_posting`, `resolve_posting_period`) which are service-role/de-gated; every `= 'super_admin'`-alone operator gate. **Test (RED→GREEN):** pgTAP `90-project-director-rpc-gates.test.sql`— (1) completeness catalog assertion: ZERO PM-gated RPCs lack project_director (was 55 before push → RED confirmed; 0 after → GREEN); (2-4) behavioural lives_ok: director may create_project / create_work_package / set_work_package_notes (covers the 2-role, see-all+WP, and 3-role-with-site_admin shapes); (5) negative control: a visitor is still denied (gate didn't fall open). **No TS change** (RPC gates are server-side; U1 did the page gates). db:push applied to prod; db:test 90 files / 1792 asserts / 0 fail. Built in isolated worktree off`main`@#76 (concurrent 147-151 session still in main tree). **Next:** U3 = table write RLS policies (direct `.insert/.update` paths; most writes already go through these RPCs so U3 is the completeness tail). **Director is now FUNCTIONAL as a see-all PM for every RPC-backed action.**

## Spec 152 U1 - project_director role: identity + visibility + nav (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (U1). New role "similar to super_admin" (operator request). ADR 0058: scope = **executive-director tier** = `project_director` behaves as `project_manager` everywhere, EXCEPT visibility is see-all (like super/coordinator) instead of membership-scoped; NOT granted operator-only surfaces (user/role mgmt, OperatorHub, notification internals). No role-group SQL helper exists, so the build = add the enum value + add `project_director` next to every `project_manager` gate. **U1 shipped:** enum migration `20260750000000` (own migration, ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE) + `can_see_project` see-all branch `20260750000100` (create-or-replace; can_see_wp/can_see_photo_log inherit by delegation); `USER_ROLE_LABEL.project_director = "Project Director"` (operator declined a Thai name — i18n-labels test exempts this one label); `roleHome`→/review; PM_TABS; all PM-tier TS arrays (PM_ROLES, SITE_STAFF_ROLES, BACK_OFFICE_ROLES×2, PURCHASING_ROLES, EQUIPMENT_MOVE_ROLES, PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES, ACCOUNTING_ROLES, BACKOFFICE_BACKDATE_ROLES) + page allowlists/manager checks across 10 page files. **Tests:** pgTAP `89-project-director-role.test.sql` (director sees an uninvolved project + WP + photo_log; uninvolved PM sees nothing — proves see-all differentiator) + vitest role-home/role-sets/i18n-labels updated. db:push applied to prod DB (enum additive, zero live users have the role); db:test 89 green (suite 89 files / 1787 asserts / 0 fail); lint+typecheck+test green. **Built in an isolated worktree** off clean HEAD — a concurrent session's uncommitted specs 147/148/150/151 had rewritten 4 of the detail pages I gate, so this branch re-applies only the role edits to clean bases (no cross-contamination). **Next:** U2 = RPC action gates (add project_director to every SECURITY DEFINER RPC `current_user_role() in (…)` allowlist containing project_manager — until then a director sees PM write buttons but the RPC raises 42501; no live user yet). U3 = table write RLS policies. **Open:** role-group helper (`PM_ROLES.includes`) consolidation deferred (drift surface flagged); director excluded from PM notification recipients (ADR 0058 §3) — revisit if operator wants it.

## Spec 147 U1 - WP-detail data-fetch parallelization (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE. First unit of the waterfall-fix sweep (ranks 1-4). The WP-detail page (`projects/[projectId]/work-packages/[workPackageId]`) ran ~10 Supabase reads in a serial waterfall; the child reads depend only on the work package, not each other. **Measured on prod** (free tier, Singapore region, app co-located with DB): warm hop ~55-65ms; the chain ran **462ms serial -> 197ms parallel** (Promise.all the independent fan), 57% / ~264ms saved — real page worse (+ signed-URL Storage + labor zone + RLS eval). My first "~100-200ms/hop" guess was refuted; warm hops are cheaper, but the parallel win (~halves DB time) is real and bigger in absolute terms on the full page. **TDD:** `tests/unit/load-work-package-detail.test.ts` (RED first — module-missing) -> 4 cases: concurrency (in-flight-counter stub; max >=5, a serial waterfall peaks at 1), assembled shape, wp=null on project mismatch, planner-gated reads. **Impl:** extracted `src/lib/work-packages/load-detail.ts` (`loadWorkPackageDetail`, mirrors `fetchLaborZoneData` spec 46) — root wp fetch -> one Promise.all fan (contractors, approvals, requests, planner-pair, labor, photos, defect) -> dependent tail Promise.all (displayNames needs approvals+requests; signedUrls needs photos). **Behavior-preserving:** same queries/columns/results, only scheduling changes. Page calls the loader + keeps its presentational derivations; dropped 4 now-unused imports. Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (167 files / 1137 tests) all green. A throwaway timing harness (`scripts/measure-wp-hops.ts`, service-role via authed CLI) confirmed the delta, then deleted. **Next:** U2 = project detail (`projects/[projectId]`), same pattern (~9 serial hops). OUT: U3 request detail, U4 portal, ranks 5-8 (orders/deliveries/schedule/projects-hub) = follow-up; `<Suspense>` streaming + middleware getUser + client bundle/hydration + infra (Pro/cold-start) all separate, untouched.

## Spec 146 U2 - equipment project allocations (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE. ADR 0055 decisions 4/8. Second P2 unit; data layer, no UI, money domain. Commits where a rented set's monthly cost lands: a rental batch attached to a PROJECT for a period. **Model resolved:** allocation = the MONEY commitment (batch → project, admin-only); physical item→project custody stays on `equipment_movements` (field-visible, spec 141 U3). Under Case A (independent per-item daily charge-out, not a pass-through of the monthly cost) the allocation needs NO per-item membership/quantity — whole-batch-to-project; batch-item composition is not modeled + not needed (recorded seam). **TDD:** `tests/unit/allocation.test.ts` (8 cases, RED first) → `validateAllocation` (period order, ISO strings, no Date parse; date logic mirrors validate-rental-batch, kept separate per scope — shared iso-date helper = recorded seam). **Migrations (3, additive):** `20260737000000` audit-action `equipment_allocation_create` (own migration); `20260737000100` `equipment_project_allocations` (batch_id/project_id FKs, period, CHECK ends_on≥starts_on, zero-grant money table, no-delete, project+batch indexes); `20260737000200` `create_equipment_project_allocation(batch,project,starts,ends?,note?)→uuid` SECURITY DEFINER, gate pm/super/**procurement**, FK-existence + period guards (P0001), created_by pin, audited. **pgTAP file 78 (18 asserts):** catalog/PK/RLS/zero-policy, period CHECK, zero-grant read+write denied, RPC gate (site_admin AND visitor → 42501; procurement ALLOWED; pm happy), P0001 bad-batch/bad-project/end<start, created_by pin, 2 audit rows, anon denied. **Both** audit_action enum pins updated again (03 + 18 — the lesson held, no miss this time). types regenerated (db:types dual-write). Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (1137 unit) + `pnpm db:push` + `pnpm db:test` (78 files / 1568 asserts / 0 failures) all green. **Next:** P2 U3 = `equipment_usage_logs` (per-item/WP/day at daily_rate_snapshot, mirror labor_logs). OUT: usage logs (U3), freeze+wp_equipment_costs (U4), UI (U5), owner portal (U6); allocation edit/end (`ends_on` set at create only); bulk/partial split (reconciliation seam).

## Spec 146 U1 - equipment rental money spine (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE. ADR 0055 decision 5. The first P2 (rental money) unit; data layer, no UI, zero field/site_admin exposure. **TDD:** `tests/unit/equipment-daily-rate.test.ts` + `tests/unit/rental-batch.test.ts` (16 cases, RED first) → `validateEquipmentDailyRate` + `validateRentalBatch` pure validators (Thai, mirror the DB CHECKs; dates compared as ISO strings, no Date parsing). **Migrations (4, additive only):** `20260736000000` audit-action enum values `equipment_rate_change` + `equipment_batch_create` (own migration, enum-add isolation); `20260736000100` `equipment_items.daily_rate numeric(12,2)` MONEY (CHECK ≥0; anti-grant by omission from the spec-141 column-scoped grants — admin-only out of the box, like acquisition_cost); `20260736000200` `equipment_rental_batches` (owner_id/monthly_rate/period header, zero-grant money table like wp_labor_costs, CHECKs monthly_rate≥0 + ends_on≥starts_on, no-delete); `20260736000300` the two money RPCs. **RPCs (SECURITY DEFINER, mirror set_worker_day_rate):** `set_equipment_daily_rate(id,rate)` + `create_equipment_rental_batch(owner,rate,starts,ends?,note?)→uuid`; gate = pm/super/**procurement** — a DELIBERATE divergence from labor's pm/super-only (procurement is equipment back office, ADR 0055 decision 6); created_by pinned to auth.uid(); each write audited. **pgTAP file 77 (29 asserts):** catalog/PK/RLS/zero-policy, daily_rate anti-grant + negative CHECK, batches zero-grant + both CHECKs, RPC gates (site_admin AND visitor → 42501; procurement ALLOWED), P0001 existence/shape guards, created_by pin, exact audit-row counts, anon denied. **Both** audit_action enum_has_labels pins updated (+2): file 03 AND file 18-appsheet-writer-purchasing (the spec-27 "grep ALL enum pins" lesson — file 18 was missed on the first db:test run and caught it). Numbered 77 not 67 (67 already taken by spec 142's project-onboarding pgTAP). types regenerated (db:types, no hand-extend needed). Verification: lint + typecheck + `pnpm test` (full unit suite) + `pnpm db:push` + `pnpm db:test` all green. **Next:** P2 U2 = `equipment_project_allocations` (batch↔project set membership). Open Qs (ADR 0055, before U6): batch payment mechanism (cash vs intercompany), owner access identity.

OUT (per spec, surfaced not built): allocations (U2), usage logs (U3), freeze_wp_equipment_cost + wp_equipment_costs (U4), budget-vs-spend join + rate/batch UI (U5), owner portal (U6).

## Pre-existing fix - spec 145 pgTAP plan count (2026-06-19)

Status: COMPLETE (separate from spec 146, found while running the green gate). `supabase/tests/database/76-lock-completed-project.test.sql` declared `plan(11)` but contains only 10 assertion statements — it has been failing `pnpm db:test` (1 failure) since spec 145 shipped (efc9c72, 2026-06-18); CI never caught it (CI runs lint/typecheck/test only, not db:test). Minimal correct fix: `plan(11)` → `plan(10)` to match the 10 real, all-passing assertions (no fabricated coverage added). Open question: whether spec 145 intended an 11th assertion (e.g. a positive "clone into an active project works" or "WP-DONE is now rework") — if so, add it in a follow-up; the lock behavior itself is fully covered by the 10.

## Spec 16 P2 UI - reference attachments (2026-06-11)

Status: COMPLETE (DB shipped under spec 23). New action addPurchaseRequestAttachment (per-kind validation, server-rebuilt path, purpose=reference). ## super_admin navigation fix (2026-06-11)

Status: COMPLETE. Operator report: 'superadmin has a weird navigation'. Root cause: roleHome() only knew site_admin/project_manager, so super_admin fell into the /coming-soon default — post-login redirect (LINE callback, /login, /), the /profile back link, and the bare /requests back link all bounced a fully-privileged role to the 'tools not ready' page. Fix: super_admin → /pm (consistent with the tab bar giving super the PM set, spec 19). Test-first: tests/unit/role-home.test.ts pins all served + unserved roles. 279 unit tests green.

## Incident: AppSheet EMAXCONNSESSION (2026-06-11)

Operator screenshot: AppSheet app failed to load — 'max clients reached in session mode, pool_size: 48' on projects + users reads. Diagnosis: AppSheet data source pointed at the SESSION pooler (5432, per go-live checklist Step 2 wording); Supavisor session pools are per-DB-user with a 48-client cap, and AppSheet's parallel sync burst exhausted appsheet_writer's pool. pg_stat_activity showed ZERO held appsheet_writer backends at diagnosis time — burst, not a leak; nothing to terminate; no prod change made. Fix: operator repoints AppSheet to the TRANSACTION pooler (6543). Checklist Step 2 now documents the port split (5432 = smoke ritual only). Raising session pool_size is not viable (already near the compute tier's max_connections). Open question: if AppSheet misbehaves on transaction mode (prepared-statement quirks), revisit — fallback is a dedicated session pool budget discussion.

Root cause: navigation gap, not RLS — the PM journey went project list -> reports directly; WPs only reached PMs via the รอตรวจ queue. Fix: the reports page nav strip gains a รายการงาน link to /sa/projects/{id} (already PM-authorized; already the WP review screen's spec-12 back-target). Verified-by-checklist (page link; no pure seam). 279 tests green. Open question: option 2 (a real /pm/projects/{id} hub with WP list + reports tab) if the operator wants PM-styled WP browsing later.
