Operator chose (2026-06-17) a MANUAL proof-of-delivery uploader near-term ("upload first", before U2b). `purchase_order_attachments` (spec 125) is deliberately single-purpose, so a distinct proof category needs schema. **Migration** `20260713000000_po_attachment_purpose.sql`: new `purchase_order_attachment_purpose` enum (`source_document` default + `proof_of_delivery`) — a FRESH `create type` (not `alter type add value`), so its labels are usable in the same migration (the 20260622000100 hazard doesn't apply); `purpose` column added NOT NULL DEFAULT `source_document` (existing rows + the create-PO source-doc INSERT path keep working with no code change); column-scoped `grant insert (purpose)` so app sessions can set it (ADR 0038); the existing back-office INSERT policy is unchanged (it doesn't constrain the purpose column — proof upload is the same back-office/site gate); `purchase_order_attachments_current` view recreated to carry `purpose` (security_invoker + ADR 0009/0015 anti-join preserved). **pgTAP** file 50 extended 23→29 (enum labels, table+view column, default-to-source_document behavioral check, proof_of_delivery insert + view surfaces it) — runs post-apply. **App unchanged** (typecheck/lint/963 unit stay green from f716605). **Next after apply:** `db:types` regen (will add `purpose` to the generated types app+worker), then **U4a-ii** = `ProofOfDeliveryUploader` on the PO detail (mirror InvoiceUploader, writes purpose='proof_of_delivery' to po-attachments) + `addProofOfDeliveryAttachment` action + a หลักฐานการรับของ display section. The Lalamove auto-POD (U4b, blocked on creds) fans into the SAME purpose.

## Spec 134 U4a-ii - Manual proof-of-delivery uploader (UI) (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-17. **App-only** (the U4a-i migration `20260713000000_po_attachment_purpose` was merged + `db:push`ed to prod this session; `db:test` 59 files / 1256 assertions / 0 failures; `db:types` regenerated app+worker, committed e670b7b). typecheck · lint · **963 unit** · all green. Completes the operator-chosen ("upload first") manual proof-of-delivery slot. **Action** `addProofOfDeliveryAttachment` (requests/actions.ts) — mirrors `addPurchaseOrderAttachment` but stamps `purpose='proof_of_delivery'`; same po-attachments bucket + path + back-office INSERT policy + idempotent 23505 replay. **Component** `ProofOfDeliveryUploader` (mirrors InvoiceUploader: prepare/downscale or raw PDF → direct upload to po-attachments at {po_id}/{att}.{ext} → action → refresh). **PO detail (U1 page)** gains a "หลักฐานการรับของ" section: reads `purchase_order_attachments_current` filtered `purpose='proof_of_delivery'`, signs URLs (private bucket, service-role), renders image lightbox group + PDF iframes + the uploader (shown to all PO-detail viewers = the insert-policy role set; RLS re-enforces). **Leak fix:** the ticket-detail source-doc query (`เอกสารใบสั่งซื้อ`) now filters `purpose='source_document'` so proof rows never show there. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page + storage → no preview; matches how InvoiceUploader/addPurchaseOrderAttachment shipped; pgTAP file 50 covers the schema). **U4a feature-complete.** Lalamove auto-POD (U4b, blocked on creds) will fan into this same `proof_of_delivery` purpose. **Next:** U2b (desktop grid PO grouping); U3 (within-ticket split, ADR-gated).

## Spec 134 U2b - Desktop grid PO grouping (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-17. **App-only.** typecheck · lint · **963 unit** · all green. Finishes U2 (U2a was the phone cards): the desktop ProcurementGrid กำลังจัดส่ง band now renders a **PO header row** before each bundled order's members, linking to the PO detail (U1). **Page:** the in_transit gridGroups items are pre-ordered via `groupByPurchaseOrder` so each PO's members are contiguous (PO groups first-appearance, then loose) — `flattenRecordOrder`/prev-next drawer nav follows this visual order with no extra change; `purchase_order_id` added to the `ProcurementGridRecord` mapping; a serializable `poFacts` record (poNumber/supplier/derived status/lineCount, reusing the phone roll-up) passed to the grid. **Grid:** `PoHeaderRow` (Package icon · PO-#### · supplier · status pill · N รายการ · chevron; one `<Link>` to /requests/orders/[poId], NOT a drawer-opening record); inserted in `BandRows` only for the in_transit band when a row's `purchase_order_id` differs from the previous row's (contiguity guaranteed by the pre-order). Other bands + selection/checkbox (to_order only) untouched. **Fixtures:** `purchase_order_id: null` added to the three `ProcurementGridRecord` factories (grid-preview page + procurement-grid-health + phone-po-basket tests). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page → no preview; the pure `groupByPurchaseOrder` + roll-up carry the tests; /grid-preview fixtures carry no PO so headers don't render there). **U2 feature-complete (phone + desktop).** **Next:** U3 (within-ticket split, ADR-gated); U4b (Lalamove auto-POD, blocked on creds).

## Spec 134 U3 - Within-ticket partial delivery via split-on-receipt (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0052 accepted). Migration `20260714000000_purchase_request_partial_split` merged + `db:push`ed (dry-run clean); `db:test` **60 files / 1280 assertions / 0 failures** (new file 60 = 24); `db:types` regenerated app+worker (commit 7291dd8). The 1–2% within-ticket case. **Schema:** `split_from_request_id` lineage column (RPC-only by the column-scoped grant posture, ADR 0038) + `split_purchase_request_on_receipt(p_request_id, p_received_qty, p_received_by?, p_delivery_note?, p_delivered_amount?)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Splits an in-transit PO member into a delivered portion (the ORIGINAL, reduced — sets delivered_at so the existing derive trigger flips it to `delivered` + the audit trigger logs delivery) + a REMAINING child (new row, on_route, same PO, `split_from_request_id`=original, pr_number from the sequence default). Guards: back-office, in-transit member (purchased/on_route), `0<received<ordered`, delivered-amount range. **Amount (ADR 0052 §4, operator: proportional default + buyer-editable):** omit `p_delivered_amount` → `round(amount×received/ordered,2)`; supply → buyer value; remainder = original − delivered (family sum = original exactly, per-WP spend preserved). One split audit row (action 'update', ADR 0027/0031 no-new-enum precedent). **UI (U3-ii):** `splitPurchaseRequestOnReceipt` action (relays the RPC) + `PartialReceiveControl` (a "รับบางส่วน" expander on each in-transit PO-detail line — qty + editable proportional-prefill amount [back office only] + note); after submit the PO badge becomes `partially_received` via the roll-up (no new derive/display logic — the split yields ordinary tickets). **Verified-by-checklist** for the UI (auth-gated; the RPC carries the pgTAP). **Spec 134 partial-delivery story (U1 detail · U2 a+b grouping · U3 split · U4a proof) is COMPLETE on prod.** Only U4b (Lalamove auto-POD) remains, blocked on sandbox creds.

## Spec 134 U5 - PO-level receive flow + demote within-ticket partial (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0053). Migration `20260715000000_receive_po_lines` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **61 files / 1290 / 0** (new file 61 = 10); db:types reconciled (1f50e47). **Reframes the receiving UX to the operator's real delivery distribution** (A ~85% whole PO · B ~14% whole-ticket subset waiting on restock · C ~1% within-ticket made-to-order) — the photo-per-ticket model made A/B tedious while U3's รับบางส่วน was the loud control; now inverted. **Migration:** `receive_po_lines(p_request_ids uuid[], p_received_by?, p_delivery_note?)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC — back-office gate, in-transit-only (`status in purchased/on_route`), all-or-nothing; sets delivered_at+received_by+delivery_note → the existing derive trigger flips status to delivered + the audit trigger logs each line (standard `purchase_request_delivery` rows). No new column; spec-24 photo path + the roll-up unchanged. **UI:** `receivePoLines` action; `PoReceiveSection` (the PO detail's prominent "รับของ" checklist — every in-transit line **ticked by default** = Case A one tap; untick the waiting lines = Case B subset, the rest stay on_route → PO `partially_received` via roll-up); the within-ticket split (U3) **demoted** to a quiet "แบ่งรับบางส่วน" link per line via a new `subtle`/`triggerLabel` prop on `PartialReceiveControl` (Case C). The static "รายการในใบสั่งซื้อ" list stays as read-only PO contents. **Delivery cost** ("sometimes paid, sometimes free") deferred to U4b/courier (operator). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; RPC carries the pgTAP). **Spec 134 now: U1·U2·U3·U4a·U5 on prod; only U4b (Lalamove) parked on creds.**

## Spec 134 U6 - PO progress stepper + delivering (in_transit) roll-up state (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17. **App-only** (pure helper + labels + a presentational component; no schema). typecheck · lint · **969 unit** (+6) · green. Closes two operator-reported gaps on the PO detail: (1) no progress tracker; (2) delivery felt missing — the PO jumped ordered → received because the roll-up folded `on_route` into "ordered". **Roll-up (amends ADR 0044 §5):** `PurchaseOrderStatus` gains `in_transit` (กำลังจัดส่ง); `derivePurchaseOrderStatus` returns it when ≥1 active member is `on_route` and none delivered yet (between `ordered` = all purchased/none shipped, and `partially_received`). Label `กำลังจัดส่ง` + pill SKY (the per-ticket on_route hue; partially_received also SKY, label disambiguates). **Stepper:** new pure `purchaseOrderStageStates(status)` → 3 stages [ordered, in_transit, received] each done/current/pending (+ partial flag on received for partially_received); `PurchaseOrderTracker` (server-safe, mirrors the spec-22 per-ticket tracker geometry) renders สั่งซื้อ → จัดส่ง → รับของ on the PO detail summary card. So the delivering stage is always visible as a milestone (even when shipment-recording is skipped, ADR 0027) and shows live (sky/กำลังจัดส่ง) once `record_shipment` fires. TDD: derive on_route cases flipped ordered→in_transit + stageStates tests first; i18n + status-colors PO_STATES extended. List surfaces (phone PO card, desktop PO header) inherit the new label/pill automatically. **Verified-by-checklist** for the page render (auth-gated; pure helpers carry the tests).

## Spec 134 U7-ii - PO delivery breakdown UI (งวดส่ง) (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17. **App-only** (U7-i migration `20260716000000_delivery_batch_id` already merged + db:push'd this session: column + receive_po_lines/split stamping; db:test **61 files / 1293 / 0**; db:types 3e13abd). Answers the operator's "what if partial delivery branches the tracker?" — chosen render = breakdown list under the stepper (not a literal fork-tree), batches grouped by `delivery_batch_id`. **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/delivery-batches.ts` `groupDeliveryBatches(lines)` → `{batches[] (by delivery_batch_id, fallback delivered_at, oldest first), pending {count, earliestEta} | null}`, excludes rejected/cancelled (6 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `PoDeliveryBreakdown` (server-safe): "การจัดส่ง" — งวดที่ N · count · รับแล้ว <date> ✓ rows + a ค้างส่ง · count · คาด <eta> ⏳ row (lucide Check/Clock). **PO detail:** members select += `delivery_batch_id`; rendered under `PurchaseOrderTracker` **only when the PO forked** (`batches.length > 1 || pending`), so the 85% one-delivery PO keeps just the linear stepper. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; helper carries the tests). **Spec 134 = U1·U2·U3·U4a·U5·U6·U7 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove auto-POD + per-delivery cost) parked on creds — `delivery_batch_id` is its bridge (a courier order = one batch).

## Spec 134 U8 - Receiving is a site action (drop procurement from the receive gate) (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17. Operator insight: the purchase team is off-site and only knows the planned delivery (ETA); actual receipt must be confirmed by people on site. Discovery: the spec-23/ADR-0028 delivery-confirmation PHOTO path ALREADY gated to (site_admin, project_manager, super_admin) — procurement was never allowed there; the U5 `receive_po_lines` + U3 `split_purchase_request_on_receipt` RPCs were the outliers (allowed back-office incl procurement). **Migration** `20260717000000_receive_site_only` CREATE OR REPLACE both RPCs with the role gate narrowed to the site set (bodies otherwise identical → same signatures → db:types a no-op, confirmed zero drift). **UI:** PO detail computes `canReceive = SITE_STAFF_ROLES.includes(ctx.role)`; `PoReceiveSection` (and the nested รับบางส่วน split) render only when canReceive — procurement still sees the PO, tracker, delivery breakdown + lines read-only, no รับของ controls. **pgTAP:** file 61 (+1) + file 60 (+1) assert procurement is refused (42501) — **61 files / 1295 / 0**. typecheck · lint · **975 unit** green. The proof-of-delivery uploader (U4a) stays back-office for now (it's evidence, not the receive-status action) — possible follow-up if proof should also be site-only. **Spec 134 = U1·U2·U3·U4a·U5·U6·U7·U8 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove auto-POD + per-delivery cost) parked on creds.

## Spec 134 U9 - Consolidated การจัดส่ง (Delivery) section on the PO detail (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17. **App-only refactor** (no schema). typecheck · lint · **975 unit** · green. Fixes the operator's "on a PO screen there's nothing about Delivery, only หลักฐานการรับของ" — delivery was scattered (ETA labeled กำหนดรับของ in the summary; the จัดส่ง stepper dot; the การจัดส่ง batch breakdown hidden unless forked; the proof section MISLABELED หลักฐานการรับของ though it's the `proof_of_delivery` doc procurement attaches). Per the settled model (procurement owns delivery, site owns รับของ), consolidated all delivery into ONE **การจัดส่ง** block. **New** `PoDeliverySection` (server-safe): heading การจัดส่ง → ETA/status line (กำหนดส่ง <date> / ส่งครบแล้ว / รอกำหนดส่ง) → the งวดส่ง **branch breakdown** when the PO forked (งวดที่ N · count · รับแล้ว <date> ✓ + ค้างส่ง · count · คาด <eta> ⏳) → **หลักฐานการจัดส่ง** (the proof_of_delivery gallery + uploader, procurement attaches). **Page:** removed the summary ETA line, the standalone breakdown render, and the inline หลักฐานการรับของ section; renders `<PoDeliverySection>` after the tracker, before the รับของ checklist; deleted the now-orphaned `po-delivery-breakdown.tsx` (inlined). Order: summary+tracker → **การจัดส่ง** (delivery home: ETA · branches · proof) → **รับของ** (site receive action, U8-gated) → line list. `รับของ` stays the site's action; การจัดส่ง is procurement's delivery view. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; groupDeliveryBatches carries the tests). **Spec 134 = U1–U9 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove) parked on creds.

## Spec 135 U1 - First-class deliveries: data layer + auto-create + backfill (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migrations `20260718000000_purchase_order_deliveries` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **62 files / 1309 / 0** (new file 62 = 14, incl the backfill invariant on real data); db:types reconciled (61eb1cf); app typecheck clean (additive — no app code touches deliveries yet). **Additive by design** — leaves U7 `delivery_batch_id` + the receive/split RPCs untouched so the U9 reads keep working until U2. **Table** `purchase_order_deliveries` (po_id, eta, note, cost [money/back-office], carrier [future Lalamove], created_by) + RLS (back-office SELECT mirroring purchase_orders; NO direct write — RPC-only, ADR 0038) + `purchase_requests.delivery_id` FK (column-scope grant excludes it → RPC-only). **Auto-create:** `create_purchase_order` (CREATE OR REPLACE of the 20260701000300 body + the delivery block; same 5-arg signature) now inserts the default delivery (= whole PO) and stamps every member line's `delivery_id` → every PO has ≥1 delivery, the 85% never touch it. **Backfill** (DO block): one delivery per (PO, delivery_batch_id) group + the default/remainder; pgTAP proves no PO-member line is left without a delivery. **Verified-by-pgTAP** (no app/UI change in U1). **Next:** U2 = การจัดส่ง section renders the deliveries list (switch the app off delivery_batch_id → delivery_id); U3 procurement split-delivery UI; U4 per-delivery proof. Spec 134 U5/U7/U9 will re-point at deliveries; delivery_batch_id retired in a later migration once the app is off it.

## Spec 135 U2 - การจัดส่ง renders the deliveries list; split inherits its delivery (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migration `20260719000000_split_inherits_delivery` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **62 files / 1310 / 0** (file 60 now 27 — the inherit assertion; backfill invariant still 0). The PO detail now reads the real `purchase_order_deliveries` (U1) instead of the U7 receipt-batch grouping. **App:** pure `buildDeliveriesView(deliveries, lines)` → per-delivery {ordinal งวดที่ N (by created_at), eta, derived status (reuse derivePurchaseOrderStatus over the delivery's lines), active lineCount, latest receivedAt} (5 unit tests, TDD); `PoDeliverySection` renders ONE delivery (the 85% default) as a calm status line and MULTIPLE as the งวดส่ง list (งวดที่ N · status pill · eta · count · receipt ✓). Page reads `purchase_order_deliveries` + groups members by `delivery_id` (was delivery_batch_id); **deleted** `groupDeliveryBatches` + its test (replaced). **Correctness migration:** the within-ticket split (U3) left its remainder child with `delivery_id` NULL after U1 (orphan); now the child INHERITS `v_orig.delivery_id` (ADR 0054 §7) — file 60 proves it. cost deferred to U3 (money). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page; helper + RPC carry tests). delivery_batch_id is now app-dead (still written vestigially by receive/split; retired in a later migration). **Next:** U3 procurement split-delivery UI (sheet: select lines + eta + cost) + RPC; U4 per-delivery proof.

## Spec 135 U3 - Procurement split-delivery UI + guarded RPC (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migrations `20260720000000_split_po_delivery` + fix-forward `20260720000100_split_po_delivery_fix_lock` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **63 files / 1326 / 0** (new file 63 = 16); db:types zero drift (CREATE OR REPLACE, same signature). lint · typecheck · **979 unit** (incl. 5 new TDD) · green. **Caught by pgTAP:** the first RPC used `count(*) … FOR UPDATE`, which Postgres rejects (0A000) — every real split errored; fix-forward locks the selected rows in a separate `PERFORM … FOR UPDATE` then counts.

## Spec 135 U4 - Per-delivery proof of delivery (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migration `20260721000000_po_attachment_delivery_id` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **63 files / 1331 / 0** (file 50 now 34, +5: delivery_id col + view col + FK violation + back-office insert + view surfaces); db:types reconciled (the two delivery_id FK Relationships entries, app+worker). lint · typecheck · **984 unit** (+5 new TDD) · green. **Closes the spec 135 deliveries arc.** A proof-of-delivery (signed note / received-goods photo) now belongs to the **delivery (งวด)** it documents, not the whole PO. **Migration** (mirrors the 20260713000000 purpose migration): `purchase_order_attachments.delivery_id` nullable FK → `purchase_order_deliveries(id)` + index + `grant insert(delivery_id)` (ADR 0038 column-scope) + recreate `purchase_order_attachments_current` to carry it. **NO backfill** — the table is append-only (the block-write trigger rejects UPDATE), so legacy proof (`delivery_id` NULL) surfaces under the PO's **default** (earliest, งวดที่ 1) delivery via the app helper, not a data migration. **App:** `addProofOfDeliveryAttachment` now takes a `deliveryId`, **validated to belong to the PO** (server-side read under RLS) before insert, and stamps it; `ProofOfDeliveryUploader` carries `deliveryId`; pure `groupProofByDelivery(docs, defaultDeliveryId)` (5 unit tests, TDD) maps each doc to its delivery (NULL → default); `PoDeliverySection` renders proof **per delivery** — one block for the 85% single-delivery default (unchanged feel), a per-งวด block (heading "งวดที่ N · หลักฐานการจัดส่ง" + its own uploader) when the PO is split. Page query adds `delivery_id`, groups, passes `proofByDelivery`. The delivery-belongs-to-PO RLS with-check left as a possible follow-up (action enforces it; the INSERT policy is unchanged, mirroring the purpose migration's minimalism). **Verified-by-checklist** for the UI (auth-gated; helper + pgTAP carry tests). **Spec 135 = U1·U2·U3·U4 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove auto-POD + per-delivery cost) parked on sandbox creds — the delivery entity + per-delivery proof/cost are its join point.

## Spec 135 U5 - Delivery detail page; การจัดส่ง = one action (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). **App/nav only — no migration** (reuses U4's `delivery_id`); no db:push. lint · typecheck · **988 unit** (+3 new TDD) · green. **Operator UX:** the การจัดส่ง section had **two** buttons (สร้างงวดจัดส่ง + แนบหลักฐาน per delivery, multiplied per งวด after U4). Consolidated to one. **New route** `src/app/requests/orders/[poId]/deliveries/[deliveryId]/page.tsx` — a delivery detail page (PURCHASING_ROLES gate, uuid-guard + notFound, delivery must belong to the PO): งวด summary (derived status pill via buildDeliveriesView · eta · cost [back-office display gate, money] · note), its member lines (link to the PR), and **หลักฐานการจัดส่ง** (the U4 gallery + uploader, scoped to this delivery; legacy NULL proof under the default งวด via groupProofByDelivery). **`PoDeliverySection` redesign:** every delivery row is now a **Link** to its detail page (chevron affordance; single + multi alike), the inline proof block is **removed**, only the สร้างงวดจัดส่ง button stays → one action. **PO detail page:** dropped the proof query + mintSignedUrls + groupProofByDelivery + the proof props (proof now lives on the delivery page). **New nav SSOT** `src/lib/nav/order-paths.ts` — `poDetailHref` / `deliveryDetailHref` (3 unit tests, TDD; mirrors project-paths). **Extracted** `DeliveryProofBlock` to its own component (off po-delivery-section, used by the detail page). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pages; nav helper carries the test). **Spec 135 = U1·U2·U3·U4·U5 on prod;** only U4b parked on creds.

## Spec 135 U6 - Delivery dispatch + branching tracker + date fix (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migration `20260722000000_dispatch_po_delivery` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **64 files / 1341 / 0** (new file 64 = 10); db:types zero drift. lint · typecheck · **988 unit** · green. **From the operator's device test (3 findings):** (a) **date overflow** — the split-sheet eta `<input type="date">` overflowed on iOS; added `min-w-0 appearance-none` (the purchase-record-form pattern). (b) **stuck at สั่งซื้อแล้ว** — `on_route` (→ in_transit / กำลังจัดส่ง) was only reachable via the per-ticket `record_shipment` (buried on the PR detail); the operator at the delivery level had no path. **New RPC `dispatch_purchase_order_delivery(p_delivery_id)`** (back-office gate, mirrors record_shipment: sets the งวด's `purchased` lines' `shipped_at` → the existing on_route derive + audit trigger chain flips them, **no new trigger**; returns the count, re-dispatch = 0-row no-op) + `dispatchPurchaseOrderDelivery` action + `DeliveryDispatchControl` "บันทึกการจัดส่ง" button on the delivery detail page (back-office, shown when the งวด has purchased lines). (c) **tracker didn't branch** — `PoDeliveriesTracker` renders a per-งวด `PurchaseOrderTracker` (each its own สั่งซื้อ→จัดส่ง→รับของ from its derived status, left-ruled) on the PO detail when `deliveries.length > 1`; single-delivery keeps the one linear tracker. **Verified-by-checklist** for button/tracker/date (auth-gated; RPC carries the pgTAP). **Spec 135 = U1·U2·U3·U4·U5·U6 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove auto-dispatch — fills the same on_route transition automatically) parked on creds. Procurement plans a PO's deliveries (งวดส่ง): the explicit split for the multi-delivery 15% — the U1 default delivery keeps the 85% one-tap. **Migration `20260720000000_split_po_delivery`:** new `split_purchase_order_delivery(p_purchase_order_id, p_request_ids uuid[], p_eta?, p_note?, p_cost?)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC (the only writer of `purchase_order_deliveries` + `delivery_id` besides create_purchase_order, ADR 0038). Creates a NEW delivery carrying eta/note/cost and repoints the selected lines' `delivery_id`. **Gate:** back office (project_manager/procurement/super_admin) — **NOT site_admin** (a delivery is procurement's PLAN, not a รับของ receive action, U8). **Guards:** every selected id is a distinct in-transit (`purchased`/`on_route`) member of the PO (one count check catches non-member, delivered/rejected/cancelled, and dup ids); no **source delivery emptied** — each touched delivery keeps ≥1 active line (a delivered line counts, so it keeps a source alive after all its in-transit lines move); cost ≥ 0. One split audit row (action `update`, target `purchase_order_deliveries`, payload `transition:['delivery_split']` — ADR 0027/0031 no-new-enum precedent). **App:** `splitPurchaseOrderDelivery` action (relays the RPC); pure `deliverySplitWouldEmptySource(selected, activeCountByDelivery)` (5 unit tests, TDD) mirrors the non-empty guard for a clear inline message; `SplitDeliveryControl` client component — a "สร้างงวดจัดส่ง" trigger → BottomSheet (spec 78): line checkboxes + กำหนดส่ง (date) + ค่าจัดส่ง (money) + หมายเหตุ → submit. `PoDeliverySection` renders the trigger only when back office AND some delivery still holds ≥2 active lines (a split is possible); page passes `splittableLines` (in-transit members) + `activeCountByDelivery`. **Verified-by-checklist** for the UI (auth-gated; RPC carries the pgTAP). cost now lands (the U2 deferral). **UX refinement (2026-06-17, operator):** the split sheet opens with **all lines pre-ticked** (most deliveries carry the whole order — untick only the items going to another งวด), re-seeded on every open; a proactive "ต้องเหลืออย่างน้อย 1 รายการไว้อีกงวด" hint replaces the silently-disabled submit when all are still ticked (can't move the whole source out). **Next:** U4 per-delivery proof (`purchase_order_attachments.delivery_id`; scope the POD uploader/gallery to a delivery).

## Spec 136 - Create a purchase request on the WP page only (+ PM self-approve) (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17. **App-only, no migration** (self-approval already works at the data layer); no db:push. lint · typecheck · **988 unit** · green. **Operator:** "the สร้างคำขอซื้อ section on the คำขอซื้อ (/requests) tab should be removed — we don't allow users to make a request there anyway." **Removed** the สร้างคำขอซื้อ section from `/requests` + the now-dead `?wp=` machinery (pinned-WP resolution, the spec-12 contextual back-bar, the `?wp=`-preserving filter-chip hrefs, the unused imports). `/requests` is purely the worklist now. **Catch found via grep:** the PM **review** WP page (`/review/work-packages/[id]`) created a request by a cross-tab "สร้างคำขอซื้อ →" link INTO that section (the only live entry); the SA project WP page already creates inline (spec 29). So **moved PM creation inline** — replaced the link with the shared `PurchaseRequestForm` in a `<details>` (mirrors the project WP page). `/requests?wp=` is fully dead now. **PM self-approve (operator ask):** `PurchaseRequestForm` gains `canSelfApprove` (PM/super) → the submit becomes **"สร้างและอนุมัติ"**, which after create (+ attachment flush) chains `decidePurchaseRequest(approved)` so a PM-raised request is approved in one tap (a PM is the approver). SA keeps the plain "ส่งคำขอซื้อ". **No migration:** `decidePurchaseRequest` is a user-session UPDATE gated only by `status='requested'` — no requester≠approver guard, so self-approval already works (approved_by + the audit trigger record the actor). Confirmation reads "บันทึกและอนุมัติแล้ว"; if the approve leg fails the request stays pending (approvable from the worklist) and an inline note says so. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pages; the form-render unit test still passes). Acceptance owed: raise a request from a WP as SA → pending; as PM → "สร้างและอนุมัติ" → approved; /requests shows no create section.

## Spec 137 - Action-state bands + view filter on the site /requests worklist (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod — 2026-06-17. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **995 unit** (+7 new TDD) · green. **Operator:** site people need more than ทั้งหมด/ของฉัน, the flat sort "is not intuitive", and "at the very least be able to filter out items received." (I recommended + built; aligns with the locked SA action-state lens, [[worklist-priority-alignment]].) The non-procurement `/requests` view (SA/PM/super) was a flat list (pending by priority, then everything newest-first) + a ทั้งหมด/ของฉัน toggle. **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/request-bands.ts` (7 unit tests, TDD): `requestBand(status)` → action-state band; `groupRequestsByBand(rows, view)` → ordered non-empty bands **รออนุมัติ** (requested) → **อนุมัติแล้ว รอสั่งซื้อ** (approved) → **กำลังจัดส่ง** (purchased/on_route) → **เสร็จแล้ว** (delivered/site_purchased) → **ไม่อนุมัติ/ยกเลิก** (rejected/cancelled), active bands sorted priority-then-oldest (`comparePendingRequests`), done/closed newest-first; `RequestView` active(default)/done/all with `VIEW_BANDS` — **active HIDES received+closed** (the operator's minimum). **Page:** `?view=` (default active); the non-procurement filter row is now a **view segmented control** (กำลังดำเนินการ/เสร็จแล้ว/ทั้งหมด) + a **เฉพาะของฉัน** toggle (replaces the old ทั้งหมด/ของฉัน 2-chip; each preserves the other axis via `reqHref`, defaults omitted from the URL); the list renders as **band sections** (heading + count + cards), 3-way empty state (no requests / none in this view / bands). No new query (bands computed over the already-fetched pending+decided rows). **Procurement view unchanged** (keeps its spec-104 pipeline + spec-110 filters). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; the band helper carries the tests). Spec doc `137-site-worklist-bands.md`. Acceptance owed: site sees grouped bands, default hides received, view chips + เฉพาะของฉัน work. **Consistency refinement (2026-06-17, operator: "check UI, not the same as the other side; think hard what users want to know"):** the site bands now match the procurement pipeline's section styling — `hot` band (amber heading + amber count chip) where the site's attention band = **กำลังจัดส่ง** (incoming = what site receives, the analogue of procurement's buy-now band). And the thing site most wants to know — **is anything late** — surfaces as a `เลยกำหนด N` danger chip on a band with overdue arrivals (`groupRequestsByBand(rows, view, today)` counts items whose `eta` < today; +3 unit tests, 998 total). The shared `PurchaseRequestCard` already carries ETA/tracker/status per row.

## Spec 95 (hardening) - iOS keyboard-freeze: scroll-independent repaint for short forms (2026-06-17)

Status: code COMPLETE, **NOT device-verified** (desktop preview cannot reproduce the WebKit/iOS-PWA quirk — operator must confirm on a real iOS PWA, especially a SHORT form). **App-only, no migration.** lint · typecheck · **999 unit** · green. **Operator: "fix the frozen screen after closing the keyboard for good."** Recurrence #2 of the spec-95 blank-after-keyboard glitch (recurrence #1 = the still-focused-field case, fixed 436ee6f). **Root cause:** every `ViewportScrollGuard.repaintScroller` path forced the repaint via `scroller.scrollBy(0,1)` — which only repaints when `<main>` can actually scroll. A form that fits the viewport (no overflow) can't scroll → scrollBy moves nothing → the locked scroller (spec 64: body overflow:hidden, `<main>` the only scroller) stays blank. The 85% of long forms recovered; short ones froze "for good". **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now ALSO does a scroll-INDEPENDENT repaint — a 1px `transform: translateY(1px)` nudge on `<main>`, restored on the next rAF (preserves any existing transform; the 1px shift is invisible for one frame) — re-rasterizing the layer regardless of content height. Belt-and-suspenders with the existing scroll nudge; both the focusout (force=false, keeps the mid-edit field-to-field skip) and visual-viewport-resize (force=true, the still-focused recurrence) paths get it. TDD: 3rd test in `viewport-scroll-guard.test.tsx` asserts the transform is set inside the repaint and cleared next frame. **Verified-by-checklist** (jsdom has no layout/WebKit; the unit test pins the DOM contract, the real proof is the operator's device test). If it recurs again: next levers are lowering the 80px keyboard-down threshold, nudging on `geometrychange`, or firing across more frames of the ~250ms keyboard slide.

**Recurrence #3 — "hidden, not frozen" (2026-06-17):** operator reported the prior fix didn't take and reframed the symptom — the screen feels **hidden** (pushed off), not blank/frozen. That's a DIFFERENT bug than the missing-repaint one. The layout comment (`app/layout.tsx`) says the guard's job is to "snap the locked document back to top … (otherwise the header is pushed off)", but `git log -S scrollTo` proves the guard **never** snapped to top — only the net-zero nudge ever existed, so the header-pushed-off case was unhandled the whole time. Mechanism: body is locked (spec 64 overflow:hidden) so `window`/document scroll should always be 0, but iOS pans the document up to reveal a focused field near the bottom and doesn't always undo that pan on keyboard-close → the sticky header sits ABOVE the visible viewport ("hidden"). The net-zero nudge can't fix a real offset. **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now also `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` on keyboard-close (always safe — a locked body cannot legitimately scroll; does NOT touch `<main>`'s own scroll = the reading position); gated by the same force/mid-edit logic so it never yanks the viewport while the keyboard is still up. TDD: +2 tests (snaps to top on close; does NOT snap while another field is being edited). **1001 unit** · lint · typecheck green. **Still NOT device-verified** — operator must confirm on a real iOS PWA. If still seen: the pan may be a visual-viewport offset rather than a layout scroll → next lever is also zeroing `document.documentElement.scrollTop` / handling `visualViewport.offsetTop`.

**Recurrence #4 — "BOTTOM BLANK GAP" — actual root cause (2026-06-17):** prior two fixes (repaint, snap-to-top) didn't take. Diagnostic questions cracked it: operator says a **blank gap at the BOTTOM** (not top, not frozen), recovered by **rotate / navigate / refresh but NOT by scrolling**. That discriminator rules out both earlier hypotheses — not a paint bug (scrolling would fix it), not a scroll offset (snap-to-top didn't fix it). It's a **stale viewport HEIGHT**: iOS standalone PWA leaves the locked body/`<main>` at the keyboard-REDUCED `100%`/`h-full` height after the keyboard closes, so the vacated bottom strip stays blank; rotate/navigate/refresh fix it because they all force a fresh relayout at the restored height. **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now also pins `document.body.style.height` to the live `window.innerHeight` for one frame, then releases it back to the CSS `h-full` lock on the next rAF — replicating the rotate relayout so `100%` re-resolves against the correct viewport (own rAF, restores even with no `<main>`). TDD: +1 test (pins to innerHeight, clears next frame). **1002 unit** · lint · typecheck green. The guard now does FOUR things on keyboard-close: height relayout (bottom gap) · window snap-to-top (hidden header) · scroll nudge (blank scrollable) · transform nudge (blank short form). **Still NOT device-verified** — if the bottom gap persists, escalate from the one-frame toggle to PERMANENT height tracking (set body height = innerHeight on every resize/orientationchange, the `--app-height` pattern) rather than relying on iOS to recompute `100%`.

**Recurrence #5 — STOP guessing, instrument (2026-06-17):** the height-relayout toggle (#4) also didn't take on device — operator: "problem still there". Three blind fixes missed because the surface is auth-gated + iOS-PWA-only (no desktop repro), so the actual stale dimension is still unknown. Switched from guessing to MEASURING: new **`ViewportDebug`** flag-gated diagnostic overlay (`src/components/features/chrome/viewport-debug.tsx`, mounted in `app/layout.tsx`) — renders null unless `?vpdebug=1` (or `localStorage.vpdebug="1"`), otherwise a fixed top-left overlay printing live `innerHeight / visualViewport.height/offsetTop/pageTop / documentElement.clientHeight / body.rectH / main.rectH+clientH+scrollH+scrollTop / window.scrollY / doc.scrollTop / activeElement`, updated on resize/scroll/orientationchange/focusin/focusout (+vv resize/scroll). Operator opens the broken screen with `?vpdebug=1`, closes the keyboard, screenshots the numbers in the blank-gap state → tells us which dimension is wrong → one definitive fix instead of a 4th guess. TDD: `viewport-debug.test.tsx` (renders null without flag; overlay with flag). **App-only, flag-gated, zero impact on normal use.** lint · typecheck · test green. **TEMPORARY — remove once the real fix lands.** The viewport-scroll-guard fixes (#2–#4) stay in (each is harmless and may help one of the sub-cases); the next fix targets whatever the device numbers reveal.

**REGRESSION + REVERT (2026-06-17):** fixes #2–#4 were NOT harmless — operator: the orange ถ่ายรูป hero bar (phase-uploader, `position: fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0`, a descendant of PageShell `<main>`) was "hovering all over the screen". Cause: the #4 `transform: translateY(1px)` nudge on `<main>` — a CSS transform makes the element the **containing block for its `position:fixed` descendants**, so the fixed capture bar (and any fixed chrome: tab bar, modals, toasts) detaches from the viewport and tracks `<main>`. Worse, the guard's resize path fires `repaintScroller(true)` whenever `innerHeight - vv.height < 80`, which is TRUE even with no keyboard (vv.height ≈ innerHeight), so incidental iOS visualViewport resize/scroll events re-applied the transform constantly → the bar wandered. **Action:** reverted `viewport-scroll-guard.tsx` + its test to the last-good pre-regression commit `4b90a3a` (436ee6f baseline = net-zero `scrollBy` nudge only); dropped the transform nudge, the `window.scrollTo(0,0)`, and the body-height pin (all three unverified, all three fired on the always-true gate). **The keyboard bug itself is still UNFIXED** — but the guard is back to a known-good state and the regression is gone. The `ViewportDebug` overlay (af25bc5/6253d14) stays — it's a fixed top-left `pointer-events:none` box that transforms no ancestor, so it's safe — and it's now the path to a real diagnosis. **Lesson:** never put a `transform` on the scroll container — it reparents all fixed chrome; and the `< 80` resize gate is too loose (fires without a keyboard), so anything behind it must be a true no-op when no keyboard is present.

**Recurrence #7 — THE FIX (browser-verified) (2026-06-17):** operator sent the actual screenshots. They settled it: post-revert the orange bar is correctly bottom-fixed (regression gone), and the `/requests` create form is **physically clipped at the focused field (จำนวน/หน่วย) with a giant blank gap below** — the scroller is collapsed to the keyboard-reduced height, not merely unpainted. Confirms the stale-height mechanism: the locked `html → body → main` chain is all `h-full` (=100%); iOS standalone shrinks the web view for the keyboard and never recomputes `100%` on close, so the root stays short → `<main>` clipped → blank gap. The #4 one-frame toggle failed because it released back to the broken `100%`. **Fix = new `AppHeightTracker`** (`src/components/features/chrome/app-height-tracker.tsx`, mounted in `app/layout.tsx`): pins `documentElement` + `body` height to the live `window.innerHeight` and re-applies on `resize` / `visualViewport resize` / `orientationchange` / `pageshow` / `focusout`(+100ms settle), so the keyboard-close resize restores the full height for real instead of relying on iOS to recompute `100%`. `<main>`'s `h-full` follows the corrected root. **No transform** (the regression lesson) — only an explicit root height. **Browser-VERIFIED in the preview** (the first fix in this arc confirmed before shipping): at 812 the tracker pins html/body/main = 812px; shrink the viewport to 500 + fire resize → all track to 500; restore to 812 + fire resize → all restore to 812 (the keyboard open→close cycle); zero console errors. TDD: `app-height-tracker.test.tsx` (pins on mount; re-applies the restored height on resize). lint · typecheck · **1002 unit** (147 files) green. **Still owed: operator device confirmation** on the real iOS PWA (the in-app keyboard fires the resize/visualViewport events the preview can't), but the mechanism is now proven, not guessed. `ViewportDebug` overlay stays until confirmed, then both it and any vestigial guard nudge can be removed.

**Recurrence #8 — wrong API found by research; corrected to visualViewport (2026-06-17):** #7 didn't work either, and web research (martijnhols.nl OSK article, htmhell viewport guide, quirksmode) explained why: **on iOS the soft keyboard is a SEPARATE LAYER — the layout viewport never changes, so `window.innerHeight` stays constant and the window `resize` event does NOT fire when the keyboard opens/closes.** #7's `AppHeightTracker` was built on innerHeight + window resize, so it literally could not respond to the keyboard. Also confirmed: `interactive-widget=resizes-content` is NOT implemented in iOS Safari, so the declarative fix is out. **The only reliable signal is `window.visualViewport`** (`.height` shrinks; the `resize`/`scroll` events fire). **Rewrote `AppHeightTracker`** to publish `Math.round(visualViewport.height)` as the **`--app-vh`** CSS variable on `documentElement`, driven by `visualViewport.resize`/`scroll` (+ window resize/orientationchange/pageshow + focusout fallbacks + a 300ms delayed re-read for iOS's close-lag where visualViewport.height doesn't settle immediately). **PageShell's `<main>`** is now `style={{ height: "var(--app-vh, 100%)" }}` (h-full class stays as the pre-JS/non-iOS fallback) — so the scroller tracks the actually-visible area: it shrinks above the keyboard and restores to full on close. CSS variable only — **NO transform** (the reverted regression) and no scroll change, so fixed chrome is untouched. **Browser-VERIFIED in the preview:** at 812 → `--app-vh` 812px, main computed 812px; shrink the visual viewport to 500 → main 500 (the `visualViewport.resize` listener fires automatically, unlike window resize); restore to 812 → main 812; zero console errors. TDD: `app-height-tracker.test.tsx` rewritten with a mocked `window.visualViewport` (publishes --app-vh on mount; restores on visualViewport resize). lint · typecheck · test green. **Device pass still owed** — the preview proves the wiring + the right API, but only the real iOS keyboard fires the genuine visualViewport events. If it STILL fails, enable `ViewportDebug` (corner×5 / `?vpdebug=1`) and screenshot — the live vv.height vs innerHeight numbers will confirm the model.

**Recurrence #9 — THE ACTUAL CAUSE, from on-device numbers (2026-06-17).** Made `ViewportDebug` ON by default (#8.5) so the next screenshot carried the metrics. It did, and it ended six rounds of guessing. **Broken state, keyboard CLOSED (`active=body`): `window.scrollY = 389`, `document.documentElement.scrollTop = 389`** (also `vv.height=744`, `vv.offsetTop=49`, `doc.clientH=793`). The body is LOCKED (spec 64 overflow:hidden) so the document must always be at scroll 0 — it was at **389**. iOS scrolls the documentElement to reveal the focused input when the keyboard opens and **does not reset it on close**, leaving the whole page shifted up ~389px = exactly the bottom blank gap. **Not paint, not main-height, not a stale 100% — a stuck DOCUMENT scroll.** Every prior fix was the wrong axis; the `--app-vh`/visualViewport-height experiment (#8) even made `<main>` 49px short (744 vs the 793 layout) and is now REVERTED. **Fix:** `ViewportScrollGuard` rewritten to force `documentElement.scrollTop` / `body.scrollTop` / `window.scrollTo(0,0)` back to 0 when the keyboard is DOWN (`innerHeight - visualViewport.height < 100`, so it never fights iOS revealing the input mid-edit), fired on focusout + visualViewport resize/scroll + orientationchange/pageshow, with 300ms/600ms re-reads for iOS's late settle. Only the document scroll is touched — `<main>`'s reading scroll is left alone; no transform, no height changes. Reverted: deleted `AppHeightTracker` + its test, removed it from layout, PageShell `<main>` back to plain `h-full`. TDD: guard test rewritten (resets doc scroll to 0 when keyboard down; does NOT reset when keyboard up). lint · typecheck · test green; preview confirms no regression (main full height, no console errors). `ViewportDebug` stays default-on for the confirmation screenshot — expectation: in the broken-flow re-test, `window.scrollY`/`doc.scrollTop` now read 0. Remove the overlay once the operator confirms.

**Recurrence #10 — THE REAL ROOT: iOS auto-zoom on input focus (2026-06-17).** After the doc-scroll-reset, operator: **"zooming out to 100% solves the blank portion."** That reframes it — the page was ZOOMED IN, and the `scrollY=389` pan was the zoom-pan, not an independent document scroll. Cause confirmed in code: the form fields are NOT the `ui/input` primitive (which is `text-base`=16px on mobile) — they're hand-rolled `<input>`/`<select>`/`type=date`/`<textarea>` at **`text-sm` = 14px** (`purchase-request-form.tsx` lines 201/222/241/297/308/358, and the same pattern across other forms). **iOS auto-zooms into any focused input whose font-size < 16px**, and a standalone PWA leaves the page zoomed + panned after blur → the blank portion; manual zoom-to-100% clears it. **Fix (design-preserving, global):** viewport meta now `width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no` (Next `viewport` export: `maximumScale:1, userScalable:false`) — a standalone PWA honors it and iOS no longer auto-zooms on focus, so the field-first `text-sm` sizes stay unchanged. **Trade-off:** disables pinch-zoom app-wide (acceptable for a native-feel ops PWA; operator framed zoom as the bug). Alternative if pinch-zoom must stay: bump every form input to 16px (enlarges field text, many files) — offered to operator. Verified in preview: the rendered `<meta name=viewport>` content = `…maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no`; no console errors. Added `vv.scale` to `ViewportDebug` so the operator can confirm scale stays 1.00. The doc-scroll-reset (#9) stays as defense. lint · typecheck · test green.

## Spec 95 — RESOLVED + cleanup (2026-06-18)

Operator confirmed on device: **no more zoom, no blank portion.** Root cause = iOS auto-zoom on `<16px` form inputs; fix = `maximum-scale=1` in the root `viewport` export (global — the only `viewport` export in the app, so it covers every form on every screen). **Cleanup refactor:** removed the temporary `ViewportDebug` overlay (component + test + layout mount + corner-tap gesture); confirmed no dangling `--app-vh` / `AppHeightTracker` / `vpdebug` references remain. **Kept** `ViewportScrollGuard`, reframed as defense-in-depth for the spec-64 body-lock invariant (document scroll stays 0 if iOS scrolls the documentElement to reveal a focused input) — a cheap no-op in the normal case; its header comment now points at `maximum-scale=1` as the primary fix. Browser-verified: overlay gone, viewport meta still `…maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no`, no `--app-vh` leftover, no console errors. lint · typecheck · test green. The spec-95 saga (#1–#10) is closed; memory `ios-keyboard-repaint-freeze` rewritten to lead with the auto-zoom root cause + the process lesson (for device-only bugs: ship a metrics overlay first, get one screenshot, then fix the named dimension). **Possible follow-up (not done, scope):** the 14px hand-rolled inputs duplicate styling across forms — a shared form-field input component would centralize the size decision and document the <16px-zoom trap.

## Spec 138 U1 — ต้องติดตามด่วน urgent-follow-up panel (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **1003 unit** (+4 new TDD) · green. **Source:** a Claude Design (claude.ai/design) handoff bundle ("Mobile-first redesign for tablets", primary file `PRC Ops Worklist Mobile.dc.html`) — the operator mocked the desktop→mobile worklist UX and asked to implement the relevant aspects. Audit found most of the mock already shipped on the procurement `/requests` view (navy `AppHeader`, the 2×2 `BuyerStat` KPIs, the spec-104 bands, `PoGroupCard`/`PhonePoBasket`, `BottomSheet`, `BottomTabBar`); the net-new affordances become spec-138 units. **U1 = the mock's headline addition:** a "ต้องติดตามด่วน" panel that lists the ACTUAL overdue deliveries (the items behind the เกินกำหนด count, which until now was only a number/toggle), each row tapping into the request, with a footer link to the full overdue chase filter. **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/overdue-attention.ts` `selectOverdueFollowUp(rows, todayIso, limit=4)` → in-transit rows with `eta < today`, most-overdue first (earliest ETA, ties by PR number), capped, mapped to `{id, prNumber, itemDescription, supplier, eta, amount, overdueDays}`; "overdue" reuses `procurementBand(status)==='in_transit'` + `eta<today` so the set is exactly the spec-105 `procurementSummary.overdue` KPI's (4 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `OverdueFollowUpPanel` (server-safe, presentational, no `'use client'`): `bg-card`/`border-edge`/`rounded-card` panel, danger-toned header + count, each item a `<Link>` to `/requests/{id}` (danger dot · PR-#### + item name [truncate, spec-47 card parity] · supplier · `เกิน N วัน` · amount font-mono), footer `<Link>` "ดูทั้งหมดที่เกินกำหนด" → the overdue filter. Field-First tokens only (danger trio + ink/edge/card) — passes design-doctrine (no green-\*, no min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): computes `attentionItems` for procurement after the existing `amountById` admin read (the panel shows amount — already back-office for procurement; no new query, no new money exposure), renders the panel in the procurement column right after the KPI grid, before the filters, only when non-empty; the footer href reuses `buildWorklistQuery({ ...filter, overdue: true })` (same target as the เกินกำหนด tile). Named distinct from the existing `AttentionCard` callout primitive (different thing). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page → no preview; the pure helper carries correctness). Spec doc `138-mobile-worklist-redesign.md` (index updated; also backfilled the missing 136/137 index rows). **Next units (not started, one-per-session):** U2 KPI hero restyle (amber-gradient รอสั่งซื้อ tile + icons + tap-to-filter); U3 scrollable status-chip filter with live counts (replace the status `<select>`). **Refinement (2026-06-18, operator: "Remove ต้องติดตามด่วน section from phone view"):** wrapped the render in `hidden lg:block` so the panel now shows only on tablet/desktop (lg+) — its origin in the design (the desktop `.dc.html` added it first, mobile mirrored). On the phone the เกินกำหนด KPI tile (its filter toggle) stays the chase entry point; the panel read as redundant there. className-only, no logic/helper/test change; lint · typecheck · test green.

## Spec 138 U2 — desktop KPI hero row (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **1007 unit** (+4 new TDD) · green. **Source:** the SECOND file of the same Claude Design handoff — the DESKTOP `PRC Ops Worklist.dc.html` (operator sent it after the mobile one). Audit: the desktop procurement view already has the dense grid (spec 108/109), filters (spec 110), KPIs, and the U1 attention panel on lg+; the desktop mock's net-new is the KPI HERO styling + a side-by-side KPI/attention layout (plus status chips + sortable columns, deferred). **U2 = the KPI hero row.** **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/worklist-kpis.ts` `buildWorklistKpis({summary, outstanding, overdueHref, overdueActive})` → the four tile descriptors `{key,label,value,caption,tone,icon,href,active}` in order (รอสั่งซื้อ hot · กำลังจัดส่ง shipping · เกินกำหนด danger-when-overdue/active · ค้างจ่าย neutral); only the เกินกำหนด tile carries href/active (the spec-110 chase toggle) (4 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `WorklistKpiTile` (server-safe, presentational): icon chip (lucide Clock/Truck/AlertTriangle/Wallet keyed by `icon`) + big value + label + caption, toned per tile (hot = amber fill `bg-attn`; shipping = white card + `text-action` value + `bg-action-soft` chip; danger = `bg-danger-soft`; neutral = white); a tile with href renders as a `<Link>` with a pressed ring when active. **Field-First, flat amber — NOT the mock's raw gradient** (doctrine: no raw palette; passes no-green-\*/no-min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): builds the tiles + maps to `WorklistKpiTile`; wraps the 2×2 KPI grid + the U1 attention panel in `grid lg:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_332px] lg:items-start` so they sit SIDE BY SIDE on lg+ and stack on phone (panel stays `hidden lg:block`); **deleted the now-dead `BuyerStat` local**. Click behaviour preserved — only เกินกำหนด is a filter toggle (the mock's clickable รอสั่งซื้อ/กำลังจัดส่ง tiles need the band→status filter mapping = U3, deferred). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page → no preview; the pure helper carries correctness; the side-by-side layout + tile styling are the operator's device/desktop look-feedback acceptance). Spec doc updated (U2 Change section). **Next (not started):** U3 status-chip filter with live counts (introduces the band→status mapping, then the รอสั่งซื้อ/กำลังจัดส่ง tiles can become filters too); sortable grid columns are a possible later unit (care: it overrides the priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Cleanup — remove temporary BootTiming overlay (2026-06-18)

Removed the `BootTiming` boot-profiling overlay (commit 9d1008c) — it was a TEMPORARY, default-on, top-left Navigation-Timing readout added to measure the operator-reported ~2s cold launch ("remove after measuring", same measure-first pattern as the spec-95 `ViewportDebug`). Operator asked "do we still need it?" → no: it had served its measuring purpose and was visible to every user in prod. Deleted the component + its test + the `layout.tsx` mount/import; grep confirms no `BootTiming`/`boot-timing` refs left. **App-only, no schema.** lint · typecheck · **1006 unit** (−1, the boot-timing test) · green. Re-adding to profile boot again is one `git revert 9d1008c`. The boot perf itself was NOT changed here (measurement tool only); if the ~2s persists, the fix targets whatever the captured Navigation-Timing split named (server auth round-trips vs redirect chain vs hydrate).

## Spec 138 U3 — scrollable status-chip filter with live counts (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **1015 unit** (+8 new TDD: 5 chip helper + 3 band axis) · green. **U3 = the desktop/mobile mock's status chips.** Replaces the procurement worklist status `<select>` with a horizontally-scrollable row of BAND pills (ทั้งหมด / อนุมัติแล้ว=`to_order` / กำลังจัดส่ง=`in_transit` / เกินกำหนด=overdue), each with a LIVE count. **Introduces the band filter axis** the U2 seam flagged — a single raw `status` enum can't express the in_transit band's purchased-OR-on_route set, so a later unit can now make the รอสั่งซื้อ/กำลังจัดส่ง KPI tiles tap-to-filter (reuse this axis). **Filter axis** (`src/lib/purchasing/worklist-filter.ts`): `ProcurementFilter` += `band: ProcurementBand | null`; `matchesProcurementFilter` AND-composes it (`procurementBand(row.status) === filter.band`); `buildWorklistQuery` serializes `?band=to_order|in_transit` (between project + status). The raw `status` axis STAYS (no UI now, but `?status=rejected` still surfaces the banded-out rejected/cancelled history — the only escape, kept to avoid a regression). **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/worklist-status-chips.ts` `buildWorklistStatusChips({rows, filter, todayIso})` → 4 chip descriptors `{key,label,count,href,active}` in order (all·to_order·in_transit·overdue); counts reuse the spec-105 `procurementSummary` (so they agree with the KPI hero) + `all` = rows with a non-null band; `rows` is the supplier/project-narrowed set → counts are LIVE to those axes; each `href` reuses `buildWorklistQuery` preserving supplier/project/status while setting band+overdue per chip; `active` = overdue wins, else band, else all (5 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `WorklistStatusChips` (server-safe, presentational, no `'use client'`): `overflow-x-auto` `<Link>` pill row, each label + count badge, active pill `bg-fill/text-on-fill` — mirrors the spec-137 `worklistChipClass`, min-h-11 tap floor, Field-First tokens only (passes design-doctrine: no green-\*/no-min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): parses `?band` (validated against `PROCUREMENT_BANDS`), builds the chips over supplier/project-narrowed rows, renders `<WorklistStatusChips>` between the KPI grid and the supplier/project pickers; `filterActive` += `filter.band`. Band filtering flows through `matchesProcurementFilter` + the existing `groupByProcurementBand` (an active band yields that one group); the `status` flat-group branch is untouched. **`ProcurementFilters`**: removed the status `<select>` (+ its now-dead `STATUS_OPTIONS` / label import / `PurchaseRequestStatus` type); supplier/project pickers stay; the ล้างตัวกรอง clear-all now also resets `band`. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page → no preview; pure helpers carry correctness; operator device/desktop look-feedback is acceptance). Spec doc updated (U3 Change section). **Open question (decided):** removing the `<select>` drops the only VISIBLE path to filter rejected/cancelled — kept reachable via the `?status=` URL but no longer a control (spec-sanctioned; bands already drop those from the pipeline). **Spec 138 = U1·U2·U3 done.** Next possible unit: make the รอสั่งซื้อ/กำลังจัดส่ง KPI tiles tap-to-filter (the band axis is now in place); sortable grid columns (care: overrides priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Spec 138 U4 — KPI hero tiles tap-to-filter (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **1019 unit** (+4 net: the `worklist-kpis` test rewrote from 4 → 8 cases) · green. U4 = the final mock affordance: the รอสั่งซื้อ (`to_order`) and กำลังจัดส่ง (`in_transit`) KPI hero tiles are now clickable band toggles (deferred from U2 "needs the band→status mapping", which U3 then built), reusing the U3 band axis. **Pure helper** (`src/lib/purchasing/worklist-kpis.ts`): `buildWorklistKpis` now takes `{ summary, outstanding, filter }` (dropped the pre-built `overdueHref`/`overdueActive` pair) and derives every tile's `href`/`active` internally via `buildWorklistQuery` — mirroring the U3 `buildWorklistStatusChips` so the toggle logic lives in one place. A `bandTile(band)` helper builds the รอสั่งซื้อ/กำลังจัดส่ง tiles as band toggles: `href` sets that band while clearing `overdue` (`band === key ? null : key` — re-tapping the active tile clears it back to ทั้งหมด); `active = !filter.overdue && filter.band === key` (so neither band tile reads active while the overdue chase is on). The เกินกำหนด tile is UNCHANGED — exactly the existing chase toggle `buildWorklistQuery({ ...filter, overdue: !filter.overdue })` / `active = filter.overdue` / danger-when-`overdue > 0 || filter.overdue`; ค้างจ่าย stays static (`href: null`). **`WorklistKpiTile`: no change** — its `href`→`<Link>`+pressed-ring branch (U2 built it for the เกินกำหนด tile) already covers the two new band tiles; the hot/shipping tones flow through it unchanged. **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): passes `filter` to `buildWorklistKpis` (dropped the inline `overdueHref`/`overdueActive` args; `buildWorklistQuery` import stays — the U1 panel footer still uses it); updated the stale "only the เกินกำหนด tile is a filter toggle" comment to note all three pipeline tiles toggle. 8 unit tests (TDD): order/captions, static tones, band-tile href+idle, active band toggles off, band hrefs preserve supplier/project + clear overdue, overdue chase toggle preserved, outstanding static, overdue tone. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page → no preview; the pure helper carries correctness; operator device/desktop look-feedback is acceptance). Spec doc updated (U4 Change section + Units/Out-of-scope). **Spec 138 = U1·U2·U3·U4 done — the full mock is shipped.** Possible later unit: sortable grid columns (care: overrides priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Spec 139 U1 — App-feel slice 3: optimistic worker active-toggle (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **1021 unit** (+2 new TDD) · green. App-feel slice 3 (optimistic UI) was the last deferred slice — done CAREFULLY per-surface, payroll-safe only. U1 = the worker active-toggle on `/workers` (`WorkerRow` in `src/components/features/labor/worker-roster-manager.tsx`), the clean safe pick: already a client component, the toggle reads `worker.active` only for presentation (name colour, `(ปิดใช้งาน)` suffix, button label), nothing server-computed beside it. **Introduces React 19 `useOptimistic`** (was zero usage in the codebase). **Change:** replaced the toggle's `busy` + success-only `router.refresh()` with `committedActive = useState(worker.active)` (post-mount truth, advanced only by a successful flip) + `optimisticActive = useOptimistic(committedActive, (_, next) => next)` + `useTransition` (`isToggling`). `toggleActive` is now sync: inside `startToggle(async …)` it `setOptimisticActive(next)` (instant flip), awaits `updateWorker({ id, active: next })`, and on ok `setCommittedActive(next)` (commit — the optimistic falls through to it when the transition ends, **no `router.refresh`** → the flicker is gone), on `!ok` `toast.error(result.error)` (the optimistic auto-reverts to `committedActive` = the rollback, now explained via the slice-1 toast channel — the toggle previously failed **silently**). The row renders name colour / `(ปิดใช้งาน)` suffix / button label from `optimisticActive`; button `disabled={isToggling}` (no double-fire race). **Untouched** (out of scope): the add-worker form + per-row edit-save stay pessimistic (they surface server validation + need the new/edited row to appear → keep `router.refresh`; `useRouter` import stays); the action + RPC + page unchanged; no styling/class change (design-doctrine green). **Tests** (`tests/unit/worker-roster-manager.test.tsx`, +2): optimistic flip-before-server + commit (deferred-promise: label flips to เปิดใช้งาน + `(ปิดใช้งาน)` suffix appears while pending, `updateWorker({id:"w1",active:false})`, **no `router.refresh`**; resolve ok → stays flipped); rollback + toast (`{ok:false}` → reverts to ปิดใช้งาน, suffix gone, `toast.error` fired, no refresh). The 4 existing notes/picker tests unchanged. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pm/super `/workers` → no preview; the component tests carry correctness; operator on-device eyeball = acceptance, the slice-76/77/78 convention). Spec doc `139-app-feel-optimistic-ui.md` written (index row added; spec-138 index row relabelled to the full mock). **Next:** more payroll-safe optimistic surfaces (later units, per-surface); slice 5 motion (CSS list-enter via `@starting-style`; route View Transitions = guarded spike only). **Care recorded:** money/payment + labor-day logging surfaces are explicitly NOT optimistic candidates (server-computed / auditable).

## Spec 140 U1 — App-feel slice 5: staggered list-enter motion (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint · typecheck · **1025 unit** (+4 new TDD) · **build green** (CSS compiles — ran `pnpm build` since CI/eslint don't cover globals.css). Pivoted to slice 5 (motion) after an investigation showed slice-3's remaining optimistic surfaces all carry desync (WP hold-status is a central field read across the page) or sensitivity (bank-change approval, consent) risk — the worker toggle was the one uniquely-clean surface (operator chose the pivot). Slice 5 U1 = the **safe half** only: a CSS-driven, reduced-motion-gated staggered list-enter on the WP worklist (the most-opened screen). Route View Transitions stay a guarded spike (out of scope). **Pure helper** `src/lib/ui/list-enter.ts` `listEnterProps(index)` → `{ className:"list-enter", style:{"--enter-index": step} }` where `step = clamp(trunc(index), 0, LIST_ENTER_STAGGER_CAP=8)` — the cap is load-bearing (a long list's tail must not wait seconds; rows past the cap share the last step), the testable seam (4 tests: index, clamp-0/negative, cap-tail, trunc-fraction, TDD). **`globals.css`** `@keyframes list-enter` (opacity 0→1, translateY 6px→0) + `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference){ .list-enter{ animation: list-enter 240ms ease-out both; animation-delay: calc(var(--enter-index,0) * 35ms) } }` — matches the spec-76 `toast-in` / spec-78 `sheet-up` pattern; `both` holds the row invisible through its stagger then settles visible; reduced-motion users get static fully-visible rows (the `from{opacity:0}` only applies while the animation is attached, which only happens inside the no-preference query → no FOUC). **`WorklistRow`** gained optional `enterIndex?: number` (merged into the root `<Link>` — appends the class + sets the style var; omitted → every existing caller untouched). **`work-package-list.tsx`** passes `enterIndex={i}` from the map index at the 3 WorklistRow render sites (grouped-by-band / flat action-state / deliverable-group `<li>`) so the WP list glides in uniformly in every group mode. Server-safe (no `'use client'`; class+style are plain markup), zero data risk. **Verified-by-checklist + build** (auth-gated project page → no preview; the helper test carries the cap logic; CSS animation isn't unit-testable → operator on-device eyeball = acceptance, the slice-77 visual-slice precedent; design-doctrine green — "list-enter" trips no green-\*/min-h-9 rule). Spec doc `140-app-feel-motion.md` written (index row added). **Next:** more lists (procurement worklist cards, contacts, suppliers, requests — fast-follows reusing `listEnterProps`); route / View Transitions later (guarded spike, never the foundation); slice 3 optimistic continues per-surface (payroll-safe only).

## Spec 141 U1 — equipment registry data layer (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-18.** lint · typecheck · **1036 unit** (+11 new TDD validator) · green; **pgTAP file 65 green post-push (22/22; full suite 65 files / 1363 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied migrations 20260723000000/000100/000200; `db:types` reconciled; `db push --dry-run` = remote up to date. First unit of the equipment tracking → intercompany rental system (ADR 0055; design + spec written and indexed earlier this session). **Owner-host decision (ADR 0055 open Q, operator-confirmed):** the equipment owner (sister company) lives in its OWN master `equipment_owners` — not suppliers/service_providers — so the future owner portal can bind to it the way the DC portal binds to contractors (ADR 0051); cost = a 3rd table this unit. **Migrations (3; ts 20260723000000/000100/000200; renumbered from a stale 20260713\* guess that collided with the already-applied PO-delivery migrations — caught by db:push, nothing mis-applied):** `equipment_owners` (id/name/phone/created_by; masters posture — staff SELECT, pm/procurement/super INSERT+UPDATE, no delete, created_by pin); `equipment_categories` (extensible lookup TABLE not enum per ADR 0055 §2, optional parent_id self-FK, same posture); `equipment_items` — the serialized-first registry: `equipment_tracking` enum (unit|bulk) + `equipment_status` enum (available/on_site/in_use/maintenance/returned/lost), owner_id→equipment_owners + category_id→equipment_categories (both NOT NULL), unique partial index on asset_tag (where not null), CHECKs enforcing unit⇔no-qty / bulk⇒qty≥1 / bulk⇒no-tag / name-nonblank / acq-cost≥0. **MONEY POSTURE (ADR 0055 §6, workers.day_rate pattern):** `acquisition_cost` + `acquired_at` get NO authenticated grant — the column-scoped SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE grants OMIT them (admin-client-only read behind requireRole pm/super/procurement); both documented via `comment on column` (money-doc doctrine, file 38). Non-money columns field-visible (site_admin SELECT). **Policies eval-once-WRAPPED** `(select public.current_user_role())`/`(select auth.uid())` from day one (file-40 scanner). **No write RPC this unit** — masters-pattern column-scoped grants + back-office policies + DB CHECKs carry the writes; money columns are simply unwritable by authenticated (capture lands later/P2). **Pure helper** `src/lib/equipment/validate-equipment-item.ts` `validateEquipmentItem` (decide-pattern union, Thai errors; 11 tests, TDD — written FIRST, RED→GREEN). **pgTAP file 65** (plan 22): catalog (3 tables + 2 enums + enum labels), id PK, RLS on all three, the 4 CHECK invariants (23514), money anti-grant (authenticated can't read acquisition_cost/acquired_at → 42501), staff read of non-money cols, site_admin INSERT denied (42501), pm happy INSERT, created_by-pin enforced (42501), no-delete (42501), anon denied (42501). **Types:** NOT hand-extended — no U1 consumer; `db:types` regenerates authoritatively at the gate (avoids drift on a generated file). **Verified-by-checklist** (no UI this unit; the validator carries TS correctness, pgTAP carries DB correctness post-push). Docs: ADR 0055 + spec 141 + both indexes written earlier; memory `spec141-equipment-rental` saved. **Operator gate (DONE 2026-06-18):** branch → ff-merge → push (commits 303eebc feat + 2ec5cdc renumber + 6cac689 types); `db:push` applied; `db:test` green; `db:types` reconciled (app + worker copy); drift check clean. **Lesson:** check the true max migration timestamp before authoring — the first `db:push` failed because 20260713\* (a stale-glob guess) collided with the already-applied PO-delivery run (20260713–20260722); db:push caught it, nothing mis-applied, renumbered to 20260723\*. **Next units (P1 tracking, one-per-session):** U2 equipment list/detail UI; U3 `equipment_movements` append-only + current-location anti-join. Then P2 (money): U4 rental batches, U5 WP allocation + `freeze_wp_equipment_cost` (mirrors wp_labor_costs), U6 owner portal + WP-owner billing.

## Spec 141 U2 — equipment management UI (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE — 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration** (U1's grants/policies carry the writes). lint · typecheck · **1043 unit** (+7 new TDD: equipment-manager) · green; pushed to main, Vercel auto-deploys. Makes the shipped registry visible/usable on-device. **Route** `/equipment` (`requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)` = pm/super/procurement, the U1 INSERT/UPDATE audience), mirrors `/workers` scaffold (PageShell + BottomTabBar + DetailHeader back→/settings). Reads via the **RLS server client only — NO admin client** (no money on this surface: `acquisition_cost`/`acquired_at` stay admin-only, untouched). **`EquipmentManager`** (`src/components/features/equipment/`, new domain folder — registered in feature-components-structure test): quick-add category + owner cards (bootstrap the masters), an add-item form, and per-item inline edit; the unit|bulk RadioChip swaps the asset-tag/quantity field; the U1 `validateEquipmentItem` gives Thai client-side errors before the action re-checks. No optimistic toggle (status = explicit edit, not a flip). **Actions** (`src/app/equipment/actions.ts`): `createEquipment` · `updateEquipment` · `createEquipmentCategory` · `createEquipmentOwner` — each `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)` (defense-in-depth + gives the caller id for the `created_by` pin), validates shape (`validateEquipmentItem` + UUID/enum), writes via the RLS client, decide-pattern returns, `revalidatePath`. **NO RPC** (unlike workers — equipment's non-money writes have direct column-scoped grants). **Nav:** `SettingsLink` (อุปกรณ์, Wrench icon) in the settings master-data section (pm/super) + `/equipment` added to `SETTINGS_TAB.match`. **Tests:** `tests/unit/equipment-manager.test.tsx` (7, TDD — written FIRST: row render, add unit w/ tag, bulk-switch hides tag + passes qty, client-side reject before action, inline edit, quick-add category, quick-add owner); registered `/equipment` in the nav-back-affordance (STATIC_DETAIL) + feature-components-structure (ALLOWED_DOMAINS) anti-drift guards. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page → preview only renders /login; the component test + U1 validator/pgTAP carry correctness; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 141 doc gained a U2 section. **Seams / OUT (deferred):** money (acquisition_cost entry/display — admin-only); movements/deployment (U3); site_admin read-only view + its nav (until U3 gives the field a reason); procurement nav entry (page reachable by URL today; settings master-data section is pm/super-gated); category/owner edit/delete (no-delete by design); a standalone detail page. **Next:** U3 `equipment_movements` (append-only, deploy-to-project per the Case-A model) + current-location anti-join; then P2 money units.

## Spec 141 U3 — equipment movements + current location (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-18** (commit/push/apply same as U1; commits a446dfe feat + bdd4e66 types/plan-fix). lint · typecheck · **1049 unit** (+5 new TDD: current-location helper) · green; **pgTAP file 66 green post-push (16/16; full suite 66 files / 1379 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260724000000`; `db:types` reconciled; `db push --dry-run` = remote up to date. Data layer only — the move-equipment UI is U4. **Migration** `20260724000000_create_equipment_movements.sql`: `equipment_movement_kind` enum (received/deployed/returned/maintenance/lost); `equipment_movements` (item_id→equipment_items, kind, project_id→projects [null], quantity≥1, occurred_at, note, created_by) — **CHECK** `project_id is not null ⇔ kind='deployed'` (a set deploys to a PROJECT, every other kind has no project; the Case-A attach-to-project model), **append-only** (SELECT+INSERT grants only, NO update/delete — corrections are compensating movements), staff SELECT+INSERT (site_admin/pm/super/procurement — the field moves gear; tracking not money) with created_by pin, eval-once-WRAPPED policies. **SECURITY DEFINER AFTER INSERT trigger** `equipment_movement_derive_status` denormalizes `equipment_items.status` from the new movement (received→available, deployed→on_site, returned→returned, maintenance→maintenance, lost→lost) — definer because site_admin records movements but can't UPDATE equipment_items under RLS (purchasing-derive-trigger posture). **Pure helper** `src/lib/equipment/current-location.ts` `currentEquipmentLocation` → latest movement per item by occurred_at → {kind, projectId} (the display source of truth; local enum union so it compiles pre-db:types; 5 tests, TDD first). **pgTAP file 66** (plan 15): catalog+enum, kind↔project CHECK, qty≥1, append-only (no update/delete→42501), staff INSERT + created_by pin, visitor denied, the status-derive trigger (deployed→on_site, returned→returned), anon denied, staff-sees-2-movements. **Docs:** ADR 0055 §4 reworded (event-log latest-OCCURRED wins, not a supersede anti-join; status-derive trigger noted) + spec 141 U3 section. **Money posture:** unchanged (movements carry no money; fully field-visible). **Seams:** status-derive trigger is last-recorded-wins (backdated movement sets status to its own kind); the helper is latest-OCCURRED + is the display SoT — they agree when movements are recorded in order. Bulk current-on-project quantity (signed-sum) = allocations/P2. No audit_log row (the append-only log is the trail; no new audit_action enum). **Next:** U4 move-equipment UI (deploy/return actions + where-is-it display reusing the helper); then P2 money (U5 daily usage logs + freeze, U6 owner portal).

## Spec 141 U4 — move equipment + where-is-it display (2026-06-18)

Status: **COMPLETE (local) — 2026-06-18; awaiting operator merge/push.** App-only, **no migration** (U3's `equipment_movements` grants + status-derive trigger + `currentEquipmentLocation` helper carry the write/derivation). lint · typecheck · **1060 unit** (+11 new TDD: 7 equipment-location-label + 4 equipment-manager move) · **153 files green**. The payoff unit: the deploy/return action + current-location badge now live on the back-office `/equipment` screen. Audience stays `BACK_OFFICE_ROLES` (the site_admin field move view + widened gate is a later unit). **Shipped:** (1) SSOT `EQUIPMENT_MOVEMENT_KIND_LABEL` in `src/lib/i18n/labels.ts` (received/deployed/returned/maintenance/lost → Thai; consumed by the move-form kind picker + the badge); (2) pure `src/lib/equipment/equipment-location-label.ts` `equipmentLocationLabel(location, projectName)` → "หน้างาน: <project>" for deployed (bare label if name missing), placeholder "—" when no movements (7 tests, TDD first, RED→GREEN); (3) action `recordEquipmentMovement` in `src/app/equipment/actions.ts` — `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)`, validates itemId UUID + kind enum + **project-IFF-deployed** + qty≥1 + note≤2000 (mirrors the U3 DB CHECKs so the UI fails friendly), inserts via the **RLS client** with `created_by` pin, `occurred_at` omitted (DB now()), `revalidatePath`; NO RPC (U3 grants + CHECKs + derive trigger are the guard); (4) page `/equipment` adds two RLS-client fetches (`projects` id/name + `equipment_movements` item_id/kind/project_id/occurred_at desc), maps movements to the helper record shape; (5) `EquipmentManager` gains `projects` + `movements` props, builds the location map with `currentEquipmentLocation`, renders a 📍 where-is-it badge per row, and a per-row "ย้าย" toggle opening `MoveEquipmentForm` (kind select default deployed → conditional project select [required when deployed] / bulk-only "จำนวนที่ย้าย" qty / optional note → `recordEquipmentMovement`). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page → preview renders /login only; the component test covers the wiring through the mocked action; the U3 RLS + CHECKs + derive trigger carry DB correctness, already green in pgTAP file 66 on prod; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 141 doc gained a U4 section. **Seams:** latest-event only (a bulk item split across projects shows its most recent deploy — per-project on-hand tally is the P2 allocation concern); badge reads latest-OCCURRED while the status chip reads the trigger-derived last-RECORDED status (agree for chronological entry — the U3 seam). **OUT (deferred):** site_admin field move view + nav + widened gate (next unit); backdating UI; movement edit/void (append-only — correction = compensating movement); `in_use`; money; audit_log rows. **Next:** site_admin field move view (widen the gate, give the field its screen), then P2 money (U5 daily usage logs + freeze, U6 owner portal).

## Spec 141 U5 — site_admin field move view (2026-06-18)

Status: **COMPLETE (local) — 2026-06-18; awaiting operator merge/push.** App-only, **no migration** (U3's `equipment_movements` RLS already admits site_admin INSERTs; U4 recorded moves only from the back-office screen). lint · typecheck · **1062 unit** (+2 new TDD: reduced-view hides management / move still works) · **153 files green**.
