inserts the receipt + **additively** upserts on-hand (`on conflict … do update set qty+=, value+=`) — a pure stock-IN needs no weighted-avg recompute (that's issue-OUT, Phase 4). **READ:** both tables SELECT = `can_see_project OR procurement` arm (procurement must read what it writes); no write policy — the RPC is the sole writer. **Migration `20260809000000`, pgTAP `181`** (structure + anon-deny, procurement cross-project + PM-member happy paths, moving-avg maths across two receipts at different unit costs → value 600/qty 20 = avg 30, generated total_cost, unit snapshot, every deny/validation branch). **THREE traps caught + fixed forward:** (1) **bare `current_user_role()` in an RLS policy** → the "no public policy calls current_user_role() bare" guard pgTAP went RED — Postgres-RLS perf pattern requires `(select public.current_user_role())` (I'd wrapped `can_see_project` but not the role call); fixed both policies (edited the migration file AND applied the wrap live via `supabase db query --file` so file==DB, no noise migration). (2) **`suppliers.created_by` is NOT NULL with no default** — my fixture inserted only (id, name) → the whole 181 file errored at setup ("not ok - 181"); added created_by. (3) **plan() undercount** — `plan(22)` but 24 asserts ran (miscounted the read-posture section) → `plan(24)`. **Lesson:** when a fixture row errors, the runner reports the whole file `not ok` with the diagnostic hidden if you grep-filter the output — re-run unfiltered to a file to see the `# ERROR:` line. **Out of scope (flagged):** /store UI (read on-hand + record form) = U2; เบิก/Issue + two-party custody + weighted-avg-cost decrement = Phase 4; GL posting of stock-in (Dr Inventory/Cr AP into the spec-149 outbox) = later; PO→store linkage (needs catalog_item_id on PR/PO lines first); receipt reversal/correction; required supplier; stock count/variance; SA self-receive (self-governance). **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U2 = the `/store` surface (read on-hand per project + the record-stock-in form, back-office tier).

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U2: the /store surface (read on-hand + record stock-in) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 214 files / 1409 (+7: store-manager)** · **db:test 130 files / 2445 / 0** (no new pgTAP file; 181 re-runs green after the function REPLACE) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000100`; db:types regenerated. The screen that drives the U1 engine: a back-office user picks a project, sees its on-hand stock, and records a รับเข้า at cost. **Surface (`/store`, single route, BACK_OFFICE_ROLES, settings drill-down — DetailHeader back→/settings, no HubNav, mirrors /catalog):** a project `<select>` (RLS-scoped picker — procurement sees all, PM sees member projects; `onChange` → `router.push('/store?project=<id>')`); the chosen project's on-hand list (item · ต้นทุนเฉลี่ย avg = value/qty · qty+unit · value) + a `รับเข้าสต๊อก` BottomSheet form (catalog item `<optgroup>` by category · จำนวน · ราคาต้นทุน/หน่วย · optional ผู้ขาย · หมายเหตุ) → `recordStockIn` action. The record UI is gated on a selected project (no project = pick-a-project hint, no form). **`StoreManager`** ('use client': selector navigation, sheet state, submit transition + refresh) in the new `src/components/features/store/` folder. **`recordStockIn` server action** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) validates uuids/qty>0/unit_cost≥0, maps the RPC's 42501/22023, `revalidatePath('/store')`. **Labels SSOT:** `STORE_LABEL` (สโตร์หน้างาน) + `STORE_RECEIVE_LABEL` (รับเข้าสต๊อก) in `labels.ts`. **Nav:** สโตร์หน้างาน door (Warehouse icon) in /settings procurement + manager blocks (next to ทะเบียนวัสดุ). **Migration `20260809000100`** = CREATE OR REPLACE `record_stock_in` adding `default null` to the two trailing nullable args (p_supplier_id, p_note) — same signature, grants preserved, pgTAP-181 positional calls unaffected. **Updated 3 anti-drift guards (the spec-175 settings-drill-down trio):** nav-back-affordance `STATIC_DETAIL += "store"`, feature-components-structure `ALLOWED_DOMAINS += "store"`, bottom-tab `SETTINGS_TAB.match += "/store"` (no per-role `[label,href]` pin touched — only the match array). **TWO typegen traps caught by typecheck + fixed forward → [[prc-ops-db-migration-lessons]]:** (1) **nullable RPC arg needs `default null`** (the spec-155 lesson, re-learned) — `p_supplier_id` had no default → generated Args typed it required `string`, so passing a null supplier wouldn't compile; added `default null` (+ trailing `p_note` per Postgres trailing-default rule) via the U2 migration; reconstructed the body from LIVE `pg_get_functiondef` (the U4 rule). (2) **`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` forbids explicit `undefined`** for an optional prop → can't pass `p_supplier_id: supplierId ?? undefined`; must OMIT the key — conditional spread `...(supplierId !== null ? { p_supplier_id: supplierId } : {})`. **Test (written first, RED proven):** `store-manager.test.tsx` (on-hand rows + derived avg-cost display, empty state, project-selector navigation, submit-gating until item+qty+cost, the recordStockIn call shape, record control hidden until a project is chosen). Self-inflicted test-helper bug fixed: `opts.selectedProjectId ?? "p1"` coerced an explicit `null` to "p1" → use `=== undefined ?` to distinguish omitted from null. **Browser pass = operator on-device** (the page is behind requireRole/LINE auth; no real creds on the cloud PC — spike-02). **Out of scope (unchanged from U1):** เบิก/Issue + custody (Phase 4), GL posting of stock-in, PO→store linkage, receipt reversal, stock count/variance, SA self-receive, the margin layer. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U3 — เบิก/Issue (issue-out to a WP, weighted-avg-cost decrement, two-party custody handshake), the first OUT flow.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U3: เบิก/Issue (the first OUT flow), at weighted-avg cost (2026-06-22)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 214 files / 1409** (DB-only — no unit-test delta) · **db:test 131 files / 2465 / 0** (file 182, 20 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000200`; db:types regenerated. The first OUT flow: a work package draws stock from its project's store; the store issues it OUT at the **moving-average cost** (the COST basis — the sell/margin layer stays locked out). **Design (spec 177 §U3, DB-only — mirrors the U1 cadence; the เบิก UI is U4):** `stock_issues` (append-only เบิก events: project_id, catalog_item_id, **work_package_id NOT NULL** = the WP drawn for, qty>0, **unit snapshot**, **unit_cost = moving-avg at issue**, **`total_cost` GENERATED**, note, issued_by, issued_at — no UPDATE/DELETE policy; corrections = a later reversal). **RPC `issue_stock(project, item, wp, qty, note default null)`** SECURITY DEFINER: role gate = **SITE_STAFF_ROLES** (site_admin draws at the WP + the PM tier — **NOT procurement**, which does รับเข้า IN not เบิก OUT — the gate that differs from U1/U2) + `can_see_project`; validates qty>0 / WP-in-project / active-catalog-item / **sufficient on-hand** (all 22023), non-member→42501; **locks the on-hand row (`for update`)**, computes `avg = round(total_value/qty_on_hand, 2)`, decrements `qty_on_hand -= qty` and `total_value -= qty*avg` — **fully depleting forces total_value to 0** so rounding dust never lingers — and writes the issue at the snapshot avg cost. **READ:** `stock_issues` SELECT = `can_see_project OR procurement` (consistent with receipts/on-hand; policy uses the wrapped `(select current_user_role())` — the U1 guard lesson applied up front). **Migration `20260809000200`, pgTAP `182`** (structure + anon-deny; site_admin issues 5 of 20@avg30 → on-hand 15 / value 450, issue unit_cost 30 / total_cost 150; issue the remaining 15 → qty 0 / value forced 0; insufficient-stock 22023; PM-member issues a 2nd item; qty≤0 + WP-from-another-project 22023; non-member + visitor 42501; member reads 3 issues, outsider 0). **Clean first push — no traps** (the U1/U2 lessons preempted them: policy wrapped, fixtures complete, plan count correct). **Out of scope (flagged):** the เบิก UI (a button on the WP detail and/or /store) = **U4**; **two-party custody handshake** (store-keeper hands / receiver attests, reuse polymorphic consents) = later; **wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`** (the WP material cost = at-issue cost) = the cost-attribution/margin layer, deliberately deferred; equipment round-trip return; receipt/issue reversal; stock count/variance. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U4 — the เบิก UI (issue-out from the WP detail / store), then the two-party custody handshake.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U4: the เบิก UI on /store (issue-out + recent issues) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 214 files / 1414 (+5: store-manager เบิก cases)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (the `issue_stock` RPC + `stock_issues` table shipped in U3; types already regenerated) → no migration, no db:test delta. The first UI for the OUT flow: the U3 `issue_stock` engine now has a button. **Surface choice:** put เบิก on the **existing `/store` console** (reuses U2's per-project on-hand) — /store becomes a complete store console (see on-hand · รับเข้า · เบิก) with the least new code. **Gate:** the เบิก control shows only for the **manager tier** (`isManagerRole` = PM/super/director) — the intersection of `/store` access (BACK_OFFICE) and the `issue_stock` gate (SITE_STAFF); **procurement reaches /store but cannot issue** (button hidden), and **site_admin can issue per the RPC but isn't on /store** → its field-draw UI at the WP detail is **U5** (flagged). **Design:** each on-hand row gets a `เบิก` button → a BottomSheet pre-scoped to THAT item (shows "มีในมือ N unit") with a WP `<select>` (the project's WPs) + qty + note → `issueStock` action → `issue_stock` RPC → refresh. Below on-hand, a read-only **เบิกล่าสุด** list (last 10 issues: item · WP · at-issue cost · qty). **`StoreManager`** gains `canIssue` / `workPackages` / `issues` props + the issue-sheet state (a second `useTransition`); the page computes `canIssue`, fetches the project's WPs + the recent `stock_issues` (joined to catalog_items + work_packages). **`issueStock` server action** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) validates the 3 uuids + qty>0, maps the RPC's 42501 → no-permission and 22023 → "สต๊อกไม่พอ หรือข้อมูลไม่ถูกต้อง" (covers insufficient stock / wrong-project WP / inactive item), `revalidatePath('/store')`. **Label:** `STORE_ISSUE_LABEL` (เบิกออก) in `labels.ts`. **No new anti-drift guards** (the `/store` route + the `store` component folder were already registered in U2; no migration, no nav change). **Test (written first, RED proven — 4 failed/7 passed before, 11/11 after):** `store-manager.test.tsx` gains the เบิก describe (no control when `!canIssue`, a control per row when `canIssue`, issuing the row's item to the chosen WP+qty calls `issueStock`, submit gating on WP+qty, the recent-issues list renders). The two sheets share a `จำนวน` label but never collide — `BottomSheet` returns null when closed (verified), so only the open sheet's labels are in the DOM. **Browser pass = operator on-device** (page behind requireRole/LINE auth). **Out of scope (flagged):** **U5 = the site_admin field-draw UI at the WP detail** (the doctrine's primary drawer); the two-party custody handshake; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`; equipment round-trip; reversals; stock count/variance. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U5 — เบิก at the WP detail for site_admin (field self-service), then the custody handshake.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U5: เบิก at the WP detail (site_admin field-draw) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U5 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 215 files / 1419 (+5: wp-issue-stock)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U3 `issue_stock` RPC + the U4 `issueStock` action) → no migration, no db:test delta. The doctrine's PRIMARY drawer: a site_admin draws stock from the project store TO the WP they're standing in — at the WP detail screen, not /store (site_admin has no /store access). **Surface:** a เบิก block in the **คำขอซื้อ tab** of the WP detail (`src/app/projects/[projectId]/work-packages/[workPackageId]/page.tsx`), rendered in a CARD right after the บันทึกการซื้อหน้างาน (site-purchase) block — both gated `!readOnly` (= SITE_STAFF, which is exactly the `issue_stock` gate; **procurement is the read-only viewer → no เบิก**, mirroring site-purchase). **New component `WpIssueStock`** (`src/components/features/store/wp-issue-stock.tsx`, 'use client'): a `เบิกวัสดุจากสโตร์` button → BottomSheet (item `<select>` from the project's on-hand showing "มี N unit", qty, note) → `issueStock({project, item, **this WP**, qty, note})` → `router.refresh()`; empty store → "ยังไม่มีสต๊อกในสโตร์" + no button; below it, this WP's recent เบิก list. **Page wiring:** the project's `stock_on_hand` (qty>0, joined to catalog_items) + this WP's `stock_issues` (last 10) ride the existing `loadWorkPackageDetail` Promise.all (no waterfall). **Simplification (typecheck-driven):** dropped a `readOnly`-conditional on the FETCH (the typed-empty `Promise.resolve` placeholders fought the PostgREST embed union) — procurement may READ on-hand via RLS anyway (no leak), so always fetch and gate only the RENDER by `!readOnly`. **Consistency:** the WP เบิก reuses the U4 button→sheet UX + the same `STORE_ISSUE_LABEL` / `issueStock` action — one เบิก mechanism, two entry points (/store for managers, WP detail for the field). **No new guards/migration/nav** (the `store` component folder + the WP route already registered; no new route). **Test (written first, RED proven):** `wp-issue-stock.test.tsx` (offers a control when the store has stock, empty-state + no control when empty, issuing the chosen item to THIS WP calls `issueStock`, submit gating on item+qty, the recent-issues list renders). **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **เบิก/Issue now has BOTH surfaces** — the manager console (/store, U4) and the field self-service (WP detail, U5). **Out of scope (flagged):** the **two-party custody handshake** (store-keeper hands / receiver attests, reuse polymorphic consents) = next; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit` (cost-attribution, deferred with the margin layer); equipment round-trip; reversals; stock count/variance. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** the two-party custody handshake on เบิก (who holds the item), then stock count/variance.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U6: two-party custody handshake on เบิก (2026-06-22)

Status: **U6 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 215 files / 1419** (DB-only — no unit delta) · **db:test 132 files / 2483 / 0** (file 183, 18 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000300`; db:types regenerated. The self-governance custody layer: every issued item gets exactly one custodian so "who lost it" is always answerable; shrinkage = a store P&L hit. **Operator decisions (AskUserQuestion, both recommended):** (1) **issue now, receiver confirms after** — keep เบิก as it is (stock decrements immediately, recorded by the keeper) + name a RECEIVER who later attests receipt; smallest change to the shipped issue flow. (2) **the receiver is a WORKER** (the team member who physically takes the material — the self-governance actor; confirms via the worker portal). **Design (spec 177 §U6, DB-only; the receiver-picker at เบิก + the portal confirm UI are U7):** `stock_issues` gains `receiver_worker_id` (FK workers, nullable — legacy + the /store-manager path have none) + `received_at` (null = pending receipt, the audit flag). **`issue_stock` widened** with a trailing `p_receiver_worker_id default null` (DROP the 5-arg + CREATE the 6-arg = ONE overload, body reconstructed verbatim from LIVE `pg_get_functiondef`; the 5-positional callers — pgTAP + the issueStock action — still resolve here, receiver defaulting null); validates a named receiver is an **active worker on this project (or unassigned)** (22023). **New `confirm_stock_issue(issue)`** SECURITY DEFINER: the NAMED receiver worker attests — `current_user_worker_id()` (the portal binding) must equal the issue's `receiver_worker_id`; sets `received_at`; non-worker→42501, unknown/no-receiver/already-confirmed→22023, wrong-worker→42501. **RLS:** `stock_issues` SELECT gains a `receiver_worker_id = (select current_user_worker_id())` arm so the worker portal can list their issues. **Migration `20260809000300`, pgTAP `183`.** **TWO traps caught by db:test + fixed forward → [[prc-ops-db-migration-lessons]]:** (1) **the issue_stock signature change broke pgTAP 182's structure pin** — 182 asserted the 5-arg `issue_stock(uuid,uuid,uuid,numeric,text)` exists (`to_regprocedure` + `has_function_privilege`), which after the DROP returns null / errors on a non-existent function; updated 182's two pins to the 6-arg signature (the behavioral 5-positional calls still resolve, untouched). The meta-lesson: **widening an RPC's arity = grep every pgTAP file pinning the old signature.** (2) **a non-receiver worker can't READ the target issue via RLS**, so my first test passed the issue id through an RLS-scoped subquery → it resolved to NULL → `confirm_stock_issue(null)` raised 22023 (unknown), not the intended 42501 (receiver-mismatch); fixed by inserting the issue with a FIXED id and passing that literal (the definer RPC sees it and enforces the receiver-only rule). Plus an infra note: the remote pooler threw a transient `ECIRCUITBREAKER` on one file mid-suite (heavy DB traffic) — re-ran clean. **Out of scope (flagged):** **U7 = the custody UI** (pick a receiver worker when เบิก'ing at the WP detail + the worker-portal "ได้รับแล้ว" confirm + a pending-receipt indicator); a manager confirm-on-behalf override; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`; equipment round-trip; reversals; stock count/variance. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U7 — the custody UI (receiver picker + portal confirm), then stock count/variance.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U7: custody UI — receiver picker + pending/received badge (2026-06-22)

Status: **U7 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 215 files / 1423 (+4: wp-issue-stock receiver/badge + store-manager badge)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U6 `issue_stock` 6-arg + `stock_issues.receiver_worker_id`/`received_at`) → no migration, no db:test delta. The ISSUE side of the custody handshake gets a UI: name a receiver when เบิก'ing, and see pending-vs-received status. **Scope:** the receiver picker lives on the **WP-detail เบิก** (`WpIssueStock`, the field surface where hand-over to a worker physically happens); the pending/received **badge** shows on BOTH recent-issues lists (/store + WP detail). The **/store-manager receiver picker is deferred** (managers allocate stock to a WP; the on-site receiver is the field's job) and the **worker-portal "ได้รับแล้ว" confirm is U8**. **Design:** `WpIssueStock` gains a `workers` prop + a `ผู้รับ (ถ้ามี)` `<select>` (the project's active workers) in the เบิก sheet → `issueStock` passes `receiverWorkerId` (conditional spread — omit when none, the supplier/exactOptionalPropertyTypes pattern); `WpIssueRow`/`IssueRow` gain `receiverName`/`receivedAt` (+ `receiverWorkerId` on the /store row) and each list renders a badge — `รอรับ` (text-ink-muted) vs `รับแล้ว` (text-action), shown only when a receiver was named (the manager path leaves it null = no badge). **Page wiring:** the WP-detail page fetches the project's active workers (`workers` where project_id + active — site_admin reads them via the "workers readable by staff" RLS policy; the labour fetch already uses the RLS client) on the existing Promise.all, builds an id→name map, and resolves `receiverName` from it (no fragile PostgREST embed); both pages add `receiver_worker_id, received_at` to the `stock_issues` select. **`issueStock` action** gains an optional `receiverWorkerId` → `p_receiver_worker_id` (omit-key conditional spread). **No DB / migration / guards / nav** (everything rides the U6 schema + existing routes). **Tests (written first, RED proven — 4 failed/16 passed before, 20/20 after):** `wp-issue-stock.test.tsx` (the receiver select → `issueStock` called with `receiverWorkerId`; the pending/received badges + receiver name) + `store-manager.test.tsx` (the /store list badges pending vs received). **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** **U8 = the worker-portal confirm** (`/portal` lists a worker's pending issues + a `ได้รับแล้ว` button → a `confirmStockIssue` action calling the U6 `confirm_stock_issue` RPC); the /store-manager receiver picker; a manager confirm-on-behalf override; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`; stock count/variance; reversals. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U8 — the worker-portal receipt confirm (closes the custody loop), then stock count/variance.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U8: worker-portal receipt confirm (closes the custody loop) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U8 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1426 (+3: portal-receipts)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U6 `confirm_stock_issue` RPC + the receiver-read RLS arm) → no migration, no db:test delta. **The custody loop is now closed:** the receiver worker attests receipt from their own portal, flipping the issue `รอรับ → รับแล้ว`. **Design:** the `/portal` worker branch (when `get_my_worker_profile` returns a bound worker) gains a **รายการรอรับ** section, placed FIRST in the body (the actionable surface). It fetches the worker's pending issues — `stock_issues` where `received_at is null`, joined to catalog_items + work_packages — on the existing Promise.all; **the U6 receiver-read RLS arm scopes this to the bound worker's own issues** (a worker is not a project member, so the policy returns only rows where `receiver_worker_id = current_user_worker_id()`), so no explicit worker filter is needed. **New `PortalReceipts`** client component (`src/components/features/portal/portal-receipts.tsx`): each pending item (name · spec · WP · qty) with a `ได้รับแล้ว` button → `confirmStockIssue` → `router.refresh()`; empty → `ไม่มีรายการรอรับ`; a per-row submitting state + an inline error. **New `confirmStockIssue(issueId)` server action** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) calls the `confirm_stock_issue` RPC, maps 42501 (not the named receiver) → "ไม่มีสิทธิ์ยืนยันรายการนี้" and 22023 (already confirmed / unknown) → a friendly message, `revalidatePath('/portal')`. **No DB / migration / guards / nav** (the `portal` component folder + the route already registered). **Test (written first, RED proven):** `portal-receipts.test.tsx` (lists pending items, empty state, the `confirmStockIssue` call shape on ได้รับแล้ว). **Browser pass = operator on-device** (the worker portal needs a portal-bound worker login). **CUSTODY HANDSHAKE COMPLETE end-to-end:** U6 engine → U7 issuer names a receiver + status badge → U8 receiver confirms on the portal. An unconfirmed issue stays `รอรับ` = the audit flag ("who holds it"). **Out of scope (flagged):** the /store-manager receiver picker; a manager confirm-on-behalf override (when a worker has no portal login); wiring issue cost into `wp_profit` (cost-attribution, deferred with the margin layer); equipment round-trip; reversals; stock count/variance. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** stock count / variance (physical count vs on-hand → shrinkage adjustment), then reversals.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U9: stock count / variance (shrinkage) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U9 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1426** (DB-only — no unit delta) · **db:test 133 files / 2502 / 0** (file 184, 19 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000400`; db:types regenerated. The self-governance shrinkage mechanism: a physical count reconciles on-hand to reality, and the variance valued at cost is the store-BU P&L hit. **Design (spec 177 §U9, DB-only; the count UI is U10):** `stock_counts` (append-only physical-count events: project_id, catalog_item_id, **system_qty snapshot**, counted_qty≥0, **unit + unit_cost snapshot** (the moving-avg at count), **GENERATED `variance` = counted − system** and **`variance_value` = (counted − system) × unit_cost** — both generated from plain columns only, no generated-from-generated chain; note, counted_by — no UPDATE/DELETE policy; a wrong count is corrected by a fresh count). **RPC `record_stock_count(project, item, counted_qty, note default null)`** SECURITY DEFINER: role gate = **SITE_STAFF_ROLES** (site_admin keeps the physical store + the PM tier — NOT procurement) + `can_see_project`; `counted_qty >= 0` (22023); **locks the on-hand row — counting is limited to items the store already tracks** (no on-hand row → 22023 "not stocked"; cataloguing found-untracked stock is a รับเข้า, not a count); snapshots the unit (even a DEACTIVATED-but-still-on-hand item counts), computes `avg = round(value/qty, 2)` (the unchanged cost basis — a count adjusts QUANTITY not unit cost), logs the count, and **reconciles on-hand to the counted truth** (`qty_on_hand = counted`, `total_value = round(counted × avg, 2)`). **READ:** `stock_counts` SELECT = `can_see_project OR procurement` (the wrapped `(select current_user_role())` from the start). **Migration `20260809000400`, pgTAP `184`** (structure + anon-deny; count DOWN 100→90 → on-hand 90/value 900, variance −10 / variance_value −100 / system_qty 100; count UP 90→95; count to ZERO → value 0; unstocked-item + negative-qty 22023; non-member + visitor 42501; member reads the count history). **Clean first push — no traps** (the prior units' lessons held: policy wrapped, fixtures complete, plan count correct). **Out of scope (flagged):** **U10 = the count UI** (a ตรวจนับ action per on-hand row on /store → counted-qty form → `record_stock_count`, showing the variance); reversals of receipt/issue; found-untracked-stock costing; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`; the late margin layer. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U10 — the count UI (ตรวจนับ on /store), then reversals.

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U10: the count UI (ตรวจนับ on /store) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U10 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1430 (+4: store-manager count)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U9 `record_stock_count` RPC) → no migration, no db:test delta. The U9 count engine gets its UI: count an item, see the variance, reconcile on-hand. **Surface:** each on-hand row on `/store` gets a `ตรวจนับ` button (alongside `เบิก`), gated to the **manager tier** (`canIssue` = isManagerRole = PM/super/director — the /store-access ∩ `record_stock_count`-gate set; procurement on /store can't count, site_admin keeps the physical store but has no /store access → a **site_admin store-count surface is flagged** like the U5 field-เบิก gap, but counting is store-wide with no per-WP analog) → a BottomSheet pre-scoped to that item: shows "ระบบมี N unit", a **จำนวนที่นับได้** input with a **live variance preview** (`ส่วนต่าง ±N (ขาด/เกิน/ตรงกัน)`, colored text-danger/text-action/text-ink-muted), + note → `recordStockCount` → refresh. **`StoreManager`** gains the count-sheet state (a third `useTransition`) + the per-row button; **`recordStockCount` server action** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) validates uuids + `countedQty >= 0`, maps the RPC's 42501 → no-permission and 22023 → "ตรวจนับได้เฉพาะวัสดุที่มีในสโตร์ หรือข้อมูลไม่ถูกต้อง", `revalidatePath('/store')`. **No DB / migration / guards / nav** (rides the U9 RPC + existing /store route). **Test (written first, RED proven — 3 failed/13 passed before, 16/16 after):** `store-manager.test.tsx` count describe (the ตรวจนับ control per row + gating, the live variance preview, the `recordStockCount` call shape). Self-inflicted test fix: `getByText(/-2/)` also matched the `PRC-2026-002` project option → tightened to `/ส่วนต่าง -2/`. **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** a site_admin store-count surface; a count-history view; reversals of receipt/issue; found-untracked-stock costing; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`; the late margin layer; the deferred /store-manager receiver picker + manager confirm-on-behalf. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** reversals (append-only negative receipt/issue correction), then the late margin layer (sell-to-WP per-level, flip `wp_profit` to at-issue-sell-price — the cost-first dial is still locked).

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U11: reversals (undo a wrong รับเข้า / เบิก) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U11 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1430** (DB-only — no unit delta) · **db:test 134 files / 2520 / 0** (file 185, 18 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000500`; db:types regenerated. The correction mechanism: a wrong stock movement is undone by an append-only reversal that reverses its on-hand effect — never by editing the original. **Design (spec 177 §U11, DB-only; the reversal UI is U12):** `stock_reversals` (append-only: project_id, catalog_item_id, **a TYPED FK to the reversed `receipt_id` OR `issue_id` — exactly one** per CLAUDE.md's no-mixed-content-reference rule, enforced by a `(receipt_id is not null) <> (issue_id is not null)` CHECK; `qty` + `value_delta` (the on-hand value change applied — negative for a receipt reversal, positive for an issue reversal); note, reversed_by — no UPDATE/DELETE policy). **A partial unique index per FK (`where … is not null`) blocks reversing the same movement twice (→ 23505).** **RPC `reverse_stock_receipt(receipt_id, note default null)`** (BACK_OFFICE gate — the รับเข้า audience; membership = `can_see_project OR procurement`): loads the receipt, **locks on-hand and guards `qty_on_hand >= receipt.qty`** (you cannot un-receive stock that has since been issued out → 22023), inserts the reversal (unique check first), then subtracts `qty` + `total_cost` (depletion → value 0). **RPC `reverse_stock_issue(issue_id, note default null)`** (SITE_STAFF gate + `can_see_project`): inserts the reversal, then **adds the issue's `qty` + `total_cost` back** to on-hand (no negative risk). **READ:** `stock_reversals` SELECT = `can_see_project OR procurement` (wrapped role call). **Migration `20260809000500`, pgTAP `185`** (structure + anon-deny; procurement reverses a 30-receipt → on-hand 50→20 / value 500→200 / value_delta −300; double-reverse → 23505; a receipt whose qty > on-hand → 22023; site_admin reverses a 10-issue → on-hand 20→30 / value_delta +100; double-reverse issue → 23505; visitor + non-member denies 42501; member reads the reversal history). **One trap caught by db:test + fixed forward → [[prc-ops-db-migration-lessons]]:** the receipt reversal checked the on-hand guard (`qty >= receipt.qty`) BEFORE the insert, so a double-reverse — where the first reversal already dropped on-hand below the receipt qty — raised 22023 (guard) instead of the intended 23505 (unique). Fix: **do the unique insert FIRST**, then the on-hand guard (a failed guard still rolls the insert back since the whole RPC call aborts atomically); applied live via `CREATE OR REPLACE` + edited the migration file (file==live). Lesson: **when an RPC has both a uniqueness constraint and a state guard, attempt the unique write before the state check** so the "already done" error wins over the "state changed" error. **Out of scope (flagged):** **U12 = the reversal UI** (a กลับรายการ button on the recent-receipts/issues lists → `reverse_stock_receipt`/`reverse_stock_issue`, with a confirm); the custody implication of reversing a CONFIRMED issue (the material returns after the worker attested — allowed as a correction, surfaced later); intervening-activity makes a receipt reversal's avg approximate (exact for the immediate-mistake case); the late margin layer. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U12 — the reversal UI, then the late margin layer (needs an operator decision — it rewires `wp_profit`).

## Spec 177 — Store + Stock-In, U12: the reversal UI (กลับรายการ on /store) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U12 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1435 (+5: store-manager reversal)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U11 `reverse_stock_receipt` / `reverse_stock_issue` RPCs) → no migration, no db:test delta. The U11 reversal engine gets its UI: undo a wrong รับเข้า/เบิก from /store with a confirm. **Surface:** `/store` gains a **รับเข้าล่าสุด** (recent receipts, last 10) list — each row a `กลับรายการ` control; and the existing **เบิกล่าสุด** list gains a `กลับรายการ` control per issue. Both use the **shared `ConfirmActionButton`** (spec 67 — button → themed ConfirmDialog → action in a transition → refresh; replaces window.confirm) with a per-row confirm message ("…ของจะถูกตัดออกจากสโตร์" / "…ของจะถูกคืนเข้าสโตร์"). **Gates (the key asymmetry):** receipt reversal = `reverse_stock_receipt` gate = **BACK_OFFICE = exactly the /store audience**, so the receipt กลับรายการ shows for **every /store user (incl. procurement — it records รับเข้า)**; issue reversal = `reverse_stock_issue` gate = SITE_STAFF → on /store that is the **manager tier**, so the issue กลับรายการ is gated on `canIssue` (procurement can't reverse an issue). **`StoreManager`** gains a `receipts` prop + the two `ConfirmActionButton`s; **`reverseStockReceipt` / `reverseStockIssue` server actions** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) map the RPCs' 42501 / 23505 ("รายการนี้ถูกกลับไปแล้ว") / 22023 ("ของถูกเบิกออกไปแล้ว กลับรายการรับเข้าไม่ได้"). **Page:** /store fetches the project's recent `stock_receipts` (last 10, joined catalog_items). **No DB / migration / guards / nav.** **Test (written first, RED proven — 4 failed/17 passed before, 21/21 after):** `store-manager.test.tsx` reversal describe (the recent-รับเข้า list + its กลับรายการ control for any user; the receipt reversal after confirm → `reverseStockReceipt`; the issue กลับรายการ only when `canIssue`; the issue reversal after confirm → `reverseStockIssue`). **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** reversal controls on the WP-detail issues list (only /store for now); the custody implication of reversing a confirmed issue; the late margin layer. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** the LATE MARGIN LAYER — sell-to-WP at a per-level price, flip `wp_profit` to at-issue-sell-price. **This is a FORK that needs an operator decision** (it rewires `wp_profit` and unlocks the cost-first dial); bring the design choice via AskUserQuestion before building.

## Spec 178 — Store margin layer (transfer pricing), U1: sell-rate data + setter (2026-06-22)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1435** · **db:test 135 files / 2534 / 0** (+1 file 186, +14 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000600`; db:types regenerated (app + worker). The BIG FORK resolved: operator AskUserQuestion locked all four recommended dials — (1) **flat per-item sell rate** (global, not markup, not per-project), (2) sell **captured at issue**, (3) `wp_profit` materials = **ADD store-sell beside GL purchases** (disjoint source, no double-count), (4) external/3rd-party sales **deferred**. U1 = the data foundation (DB-only, mirrors `set_sell_rate` from spec 161 U1): `item_sell_rates` (PK `catalog_item_id`, `sell_rate numeric(12,2) >= 0`, **zero authenticated grant** = the margin-sensitive money posture) + `set_item_sell_rate(p_catalog_item_id, p_sell_rate)` definer (super_admin gate, upsert, audit row, anon EXECUTE revoked). mig `20260809000600`, pgTAP file 186. **Trap caught by db:test (1 file failed, 0 of its asserts ran): the test read the zero-grant `item_sell_rates` while `set local role authenticated` → permission-denied aborts the WHOLE transactional file (the \_tap_buf inserts roll back too → "not ok - 186" with 0 assertions). Fix = read the zero-grant table only as OWNER (before `set role` / after `reset role`); only the setter CALLS run under `authenticated` (the spec-161 pgTAP-98 pattern).** Convention note: the store family uses `22023` for value/existence validations (vs the labour family's `P0001`) — kept `22023` here for store consistency.

## Spec 178 — Store margin layer, U2: issue snapshots the sell price (2026-06-22)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 216 files / 1435** · **db:test 136 files / 2546 / 0** (+1 file 187, +12 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000700`; db:types regenerated (+sell_price/total_sell on stock_issues, app + worker). The WP "buys" the stock at issue: `stock_issues` += `sell_price numeric(12,2)` (nullable — legacy/cost-only rows have none) + `total_sell numeric(16,2)` generated `qty * sell_price`. `issue_stock` (**same 6-arg signature → CREATE OR REPLACE, body-only, grants + pgTAP 182/183 sig pins preserved, no DROP**) computes `v_sell = coalesce((select sell_rate from item_sell_rates where catalog_item_id=…), v_avg)` — the **costing rule**: an unpriced item sells AT COST (zero store margin), never null, so wp_profit (U4) is never understated. The snapshot is immutable (append-only) — changing a rate only affects future issues (pgTAP proves: bump 50→80, the old issue keeps 50). issue_stock is SECURITY DEFINER so it reads the zero-grant `item_sell_rates` as owner. **Lesson re-applied:** miswrote `plan(10)` for 12 assertions (the bare `set_item_sell_rate` call isn't an assertion but the 3 post-bump checks are) → fixed to `plan(12)`. NEXT = U3 `store_pnl` read (per-item cost/sell/margin + shrinkage, reversed issues excluded).

## Spec 179 — Purchase request links a catalog item (2026-06-22)

Status: **U1+U2 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-22.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 217 files / 1441 (+6: validate-purchase-request catalogItemId · purchase-request-form-catalog)** · **db:test 137 files / 2554 / 0** (+1 file 188, +8 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000800`; db:types regenerated (app + worker). Procurement's request (spec 171): "items not selectable yet" → operator AskUserQuestion chose the **real FK link** (not a UI snapshot). `purchase_requests` gains a **nullable `catalog_item_id` FK → catalog_items** (spec 175 master); picking a catalog item links it AND prefills `item_description` (`baseItem` + ` specAttrs`) + `unit` (a human snapshot that survives a later catalog edit/deactivation); the off-catalog free-text path stays (link null). **Posture mirrors `reason_code` (spec 176 U4):** nullable, NO default (legacy rows null), link set ONCE at create → INSERT-granted, not UPDATE-granted. **THE GRANT TRAP (caught proactively, not by a red db:test):** `purchase_requests` carries **column-scoped INSERT grants** to `authenticated` (mig `20260616000400` revoked table-level insert + re-granted an explicit column list), so a new column is NOT covered → the session-client insert would be denied; the migration adds `grant insert (catalog_item_id) … to authenticated`. **SELECT is table-level (mig `20260608120000`, never revoked) → the new column is readable without a grant** (unlike the spec-174 column-grant-SELECT trap — different table, different posture). No RLS change (the INSERT policy pins requested_by/source/role only; the FK is unconstrained + nullable). **Validator** `validateCreatePurchaseRequest` += optional `catalogItemId` (uuid-or-null; blank/null/omitted → null; bad uuid → "รหัสวัสดุในแคตตาล็อกไม่ถูกต้อง"); the exact-shape `toEqual` test updated with `catalogItemId: null`. **Action** `createPurchaseRequest` writes `catalog_item_id`. **Form** (`purchase-request-form.tsx`) += optional `catalogItems` prop (omitted/empty → picker hidden = free-text-only, **so the existing form tests are unaffected**) + a `เลือกวัสดุจากแคตตาล็อก` `<select>` grouped by `item_category` (mirrors the supply-plan picker; option `baseItem · specAttrs (unit)`, a `นอกแคตตาล็อก (พิมพ์เอง)` value="" escape hatch); picking prefills the description + unit (a catalog unit outside COMMON_UNITS falls to the อื่น ๆ free-text unit input). **Both form callers feed the active catalog:** `projects/[id]/work-packages/[wpId]` (procurement + SA — added to the existing Promise.all) and `review/work-packages/[wpId]` (PM review — a new fetch). **Migration `20260809000800`, pgTAP `188`** (column nullable-uuid + no-default; FK to catalog_items; INSERT+SELECT granted, UPDATE not — set once). **Test-first proven:** 6 validator/form cases RED before impl. **Out of scope (flagged):** reconciling PR → stock-in → on-hand by `catalog_item_id` (the payoff the link unlocks — a later store unit); spend-by-item analytics; back-filling historical free-text requests; a typeahead/search picker (the grouped `<select>` matches supply-plan; a search box is a later UX unit if the catalog grows). **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests" (operator to explain).**

## Spec 180 — PR item is catalog-only + searchable (2026-06-23)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 217 files / 1444 (+3: catalog-search rewrite 3→6; reason/priority retargeted to search)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (form-layer behaviour over the spec-179 column) → no migration, no db:test delta. Two operator follow-ups to spec 179: (1) "anything outside catalog must be **registered first**" → the PR item is now **catalog-only** (the `นอกแคตตาล็อก` free-text escape hatch is GONE); (2) "typing in item selection **searches** the catalog" → the grouped `<select>` becomes a typeahead. **Operator AskUserQuestion on the register-flow → chose "must register on setting"** (not inline add, not widen-to-SA): registration stays a Settings activity at `ตั้งค่า → แคตตาล็อก` (`/catalog`, spec 175, BACK_OFFICE-gated). Of PR requesters only **site_admin** isn't a catalog curator (PM/super/**procurement** all are) — a missing item means asking a curator to register it; preserves the curated anti-drift catalog (the point of spec 175). **Form reshape (`purchase-request-form.tsx`, the ONLY code surface — no DB/RPC/route/nav):** removed the free-text `รายการวัสดุ` input, the `หน่วย` dropdown + `อื่น ๆ` unit-other, and the spec-179 grouped `<select>` (+ the `COMMON_UNITS`/`UNIT_OTHER_VALUE` machinery). New UX: a `ค้นหาวัสดุจากแคตตาล็อก` search input (dependency-free — no combobox primitive exists; a normalised `contains` over `baseItem+specAttrs+unit`) → a short clickable result list (item · spec (unit) + category subtitle); picking sets `catalogItemId` and **derives** `item_description` (`baseItem`+specAttrs) + `unit` as an immutable read-only chip with a `เปลี่ยน` action; หมายเหตุ still carries brand/model refinement. A **no-match** query shows `ไม่พบวัสดุที่ค้นหา — เพิ่มวัสดุที่ ตั้งค่า → แคตตาล็อก` linking `/catalog`. **`catalogItems` prop is now REQUIRED** (both callers already pass it). **Submit gated on a chosen item** (`catalogItemId !== ""`); the shared **validator stays lenient** (catalogItemId optional uuid-or-null) so the non-form create paths (AppSheet, `record_site_purchase`, legacy) — `catalog_item_id` correctly NULL — are unaffected; the catalog-only rule lives at the form (the needed_by/priority UX-only posture, ADR 0026). **Tests (RED-first):** `purchase-request-form-catalog.test.tsx` rewritten (no free-text item input; typing filters; select → chip + เปลี่ยน + search hidden; submit gating; submit sends linked id + derived desc/unit; no-match → /catalog link) + `-priority`/`-reason` retargeted to search+pick. **Two self-inflicted test fixes:** (a) a dynamic `จำนวน (unit)` label broke `getByLabelText("จำนวน")` → reverted to plain (unit shows in the chip); (b) a typed-spread mock wrapper → TS2556 → `vi.mocked(createPurchaseRequest)`. **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** catalog-only on `record_site_purchase` (ad-hoc on-site cash buy stays free-text unless extended); inline add-to-catalog (operator chose Settings-only); arrow-key result navigation (revisit if the catalog grows large); making `catalog_item_id` NOT NULL. **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests".**

## Spec 180 — PR item picker, U2: pro-max bottom-sheet (2026-06-23)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 217 files / 1445 (+1: catalog picker now 8 cases — adds the category-chip filter)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (`catalog_items.image_path` already exists since spec 175 U4 — the WP pages just select + sign it) → no migration, no db:test delta. Operator on the U1 inline search: **"UX is weird, any better ones? Uxui pro max."** The U1 picker dumped all ~71 catalog items in an always-open inline `<ul>` under the field — a wall that shoved the form down. AskUserQuestion → **full pro-max** (bottom sheet + chips + thumbnails). **New component `CatalogItemPicker` (`src/components/features/purchasing/catalog-item-picker.tsx`):** the app's `BottomSheet` idiom (same primitive as เบิก/stock/supply-plan) — a trigger button `เลือกวัสดุจากแคตตาล็อก` opens a sheet with a **pinned search** (`ค้นหาวัสดุ`, autofocus via rAF after the sheet's own focus-on-open), **category filter chips** (`RadioChip` — the `/catalog` "select category first" pattern, spec 175 U6; ทั้งหมด + each present category), and **thumbnail result rows** (40px image or placeholder, base+spec, unit, category subtitle) with the **matched substring highlighted** (`highlight()` wraps the case-insensitive match in `text-action font-semibold`). Picking closes the sheet → a selected **chip** (thumbnail + name + `category · unit` + `เปลี่ยน`); `เปลี่ยน` clears + reopens. No-match → a dashed register-CTA card → `/catalog`. **Thumbnails plumbed:** `PurchaseRequestCatalogItem` gains `thumbnailUrl: string | null`; both WP pages (`projects/[id]/work-packages/[wpId]` + `review/...`) now select `image_path` and `mintSignedUrls(CATALOG_IMAGES_BUCKET, …)` (private bucket → service-role signed URLs; rows already read under the user's RLS) — same pattern as `/catalog`. **The form (`purchase-request-form.tsx`) shrank:** its inline search JSX + the `catalogQuery`/`catalogMatches` state + the `Link`/`ITEM_CATEGORY_LABEL` imports moved into the picker; the form keeps `catalogItemId` + derives `item_description`/`unit` and passes `selectCatalogItem`/`clearCatalogItem` to the picker. **Tests (RED-first):** `purchase-request-form-catalog.test.tsx` rewritten for the sheet flow (trigger → search → category chip → result → chip; submit gating; submit payload; no-match → /catalog); `-reason` retargeted to open the sheet first. BottomSheet portals to `document.body` so testing-library `screen` still queries it. **Self-inflicted:** left a `setCatalogQuery("")` in the submit-reset after deleting that state (TS2304 + a test ReferenceError) → removed. **Browser pass = operator on-device** (form behind LINE auth + project/WP context). **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests".**

## Spec 180 — PR item picker, U3: category tap no longer ghost-selects an item (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest unchanged (217 / 1445)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change, single-file fix.** Operator: "tapping item categories sometimes turns into item selection." Root cause: the category chips are `RadioChip`s (an sr-only native radio). Tapping one focuses the radio → `BottomSheet`'s panel `onFocus={handleFocusCapture}` runs `scrollIntoView({ block: "center" })` (rAF) on the focused control — intended to lift a text input above the keyboard, but on a chip it **scrolls the result list up under the finger**, so the synthesized touch `click` lands on a result row. The rAF timing is why it's intermittent ("sometimes"); it only misfires when the chip wasn't already centered. **Fix (`catalog-item-picker.tsx`):** `onFocusCapture={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` on the category chip-row `<div>` — a capture-phase stop halts the focus event before it bubbles to the panel's `onFocus`, so the sheet doesn't re-center on a chip (the radio still focuses; selection/a11y intact). The search input is untouched → still centers for the keyboard. **Not unit-testable** (the bug is touch + scrollIntoView layout timing, absent in jsdom — no real scroll/touch); existing category-chip filter test still green; verified on-device by the operator. **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests".**

## Spec 181 — Supply Plan bulk PR, U1: procurement plans in PM's stead (2026-06-23)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 217 files / 1448 (+3: SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES role-set pins)** · **db:test 138 files / 2565 / 0** (+1 file 189, +11 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000900`; **no db:types delta** (RLS + RPC-gate only, no signature/column change). The "bulk PR (planned purchases)" arc opens. Operator's canonical flow: **PM plans → procurement compares prices → PD approves → procurement purchases**; for the moment procurement ALSO does the planning (PM's stead), PD approval stays required. **Locked decisions (AskUserQuestion + free-text):** (1) plan must be **PD-approved** before procurement generates PRs; generated PRs are **born `approved`** (inherit the plan's PD approval, no per-PR re-approval) → ready for the existing price-compare/PO flow [U3]; (2) procurement does create/add/remove/submit + generate; **approve/reject stay PD/super**; (3) entry = **inline grid** [U2], no paste-import v1; (4) **accuracy-correctness catch** — a plan-generated PR is PLANNED not reactive, so it links to its plan line and is **excluded from `unplanned_miss`** [U3], else generating PRs would corrupt the very metric spec 176 exists to produce. **U1 = procurement ACCESS (this unit):** new `SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES = [...PM_ROLES, "procurement"]` (role-home; members coincide with WORKER_ROSTER_ROLES, meaning differs = "who plans supply", kept separate per role-doctrine). **Migration `20260809000900`** (RLS + RPC widening, **`alter policy` + `CREATE OR REPLACE`, bodies sourced from LIVE = spec-176 U1/U3, edited only at role-list + membership lines**): the `supply_plans`/`supply_plan_lines` SELECT policies gain a `current_user_role()='procurement'` arm beside `can_see_project` (procurement is cross-project → its arm carries NO membership gate, spec 171/173 pattern); `create_supply_plan`/`add_supply_plan_line`/`remove_supply_plan_line`/`submit_supply_plan` role-list += procurement + membership skipped for it (`role <> 'procurement' AND NOT can_see_project`); `approve_supply_plan`/`reject_supply_plan` **untouched** (PD/super). **Page** (`/projects/[id]/supply-plan`): `requireRole(SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES)`; the `SupplyPlanAccuracy` card is rendered ONLY for the planner tier (procurement skips it — `supply_plan_accuracy` stays PM-gated + the measure is the PM's, so the page would 42501/empty otherwise); `canApprove` stays PD/super → procurement sees add/submit, no approve. **pgTAP `189`:** cross-project procurement (non-member) create+read+add+read-line+remove+submit + status=submitted; **cannot approve (42501)**; a non-member PM stays denied (procurement arm didn't widen PM); anon still can't EXECUTE (CREATE OR REPLACE preserved the revoke — no DROP+CREATE anon re-grant). **Clean first push — no traps** (CREATE OR REPLACE preserved grants; pgTAP 176 had no procurement pins to break; `alter policy` kept the policy names so any name pins hold). **NEXT = U2** (bulk-add RPC + the inline grid editor), then U3 (generation engine + accuracy exclusion), U4 (convert mode UI).

## Spec 181 — Supply Plan bulk PR, U2: bulk-add + the inline grid editor (2026-06-23)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 217 files / 1450 (+2: supply-plan-manager grid 6→8)** · **db:test 139 files / 2574 / 0** (+1 file 190) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001000`; db:types regenerated (app + worker, +`add_supply_plan_lines`). The "table / multiple items at once" the operator asked for. **DB — `add_supply_plan_lines(p_plan_id uuid, p_lines jsonb)`** (mig `20260809001000`): loops `jsonb_array_elements`, validates + inserts every line with the SAME per-line checks as the single `add_supply_plan_line` (qty>0, active catalog item, WP-in-project), **ATOMIC** — any bad line `raise`s → the whole batch rolls back (no partial saves). Same gate as the single add (PM/super/director/**procurement**, membership skipped for procurement); plan must be draft/rejected; returns the count inserted. The unique (item, WP) index still bites (dup vs the batch or vs existing → 23505, rolls back). **Action `bulkAddPlanLines`** (`supply-plan/actions.ts`): validates each row client-side (uuid + qty>0), get-or-creates the plan (`create_supply_plan`), then one `add_supply_plan_lines` call; maps 23505/42501/22023. **UI — `SupplyPlanManager` rewritten** to an **inline grid** replacing the spec-176 one-at-a-time BottomSheet: N draft rows (item grouped-`<select>` + WP `<select>` (ทั้งโครงการ = null) + qty number + note + เอาแถวออก), `＋ เพิ่มแถว`, and `บันทึก N รายการ` that bulk-saves only the COMPLETE rows (item + qty>0; blank/partial trailing rows ignored — forgiving fast entry) → resets to one blank row + refresh. The saved-lines list + the lifecycle buttons (ส่งอนุมัติ / อนุมัติ / ตีกลับ) are unchanged. **Used the existing grouped-`<select>` per row (NOT the spec-180 searchable picker)** to keep U2 decoupled (no cross-feature coupling, no thumbnail plumbing, no page change) — **swapping the grid's item cell to the searchable CatalogItemPicker is a flagged follow-up** if the 71-item select feels heavy. **Test rewrite (`supply-plan-manager.test.tsx`):** the two add cases → grid (disable-save-until-item+qty, bulk-save shape `{projectId, lines:[{catalogItemId, workPackageId, qty, note}]}`, whole-project null-WP, ＋เพิ่มแถว adds a row); remove/submit/approve/reject unchanged; read-only now asserts the grid is absent. **Draft-row remove uses aria-label `เอาแถวออก`** (NOT `ลบ`) so it doesn't collide with the saved-line `ลบ` button in `getByRole`. **Self-inflicted:** wrote `plan(10)` for 9 assertions in pgTAP 190 → the custom runner does NOT fail on plan-count mismatch (only counts not-ok), so it slipped past the first db:test green; caught by an explicit assert-count grep, fixed to `plan(9)`. **Lesson re-logged: the pgTAP runner is lenient on plan mismatches — grep `plan(N)` vs the real assertion count, don't trust a green db:test alone.** (Spawned a chip to reconcile a few PRE-EXISTING plan mismatches in other files, incl. one "planned 16 ran 15" = a silently-skipped assertion.) **NEXT = U3** (the generation engine: `purchase_requests.supply_plan_line_id` + `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan` born-approved + accuracy exclusion), then U4 (convert UI).

## Spec 181 — Supply Plan bulk PR, U3: the generation engine (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **db:test 140 files / 2584 / 0** (+1 file 191, +10 asserts) · **vitest unchanged** (DB + action only, no UI) · `pnpm build` (pending the U3+U4 ship — built green at U2) ; db:push applied `20260809001100`; db:types regenerated (+`supply_plan_line_id` column + `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan` RPC). The "bulk PR" engine: an approved plan's selected lines → purchase_requests in one call. **Schema:** `purchase_requests.supply_plan_line_id` (nullable FK → supply_plan_lines) + a **PARTIAL unique index** `where supply_plan_line_id is not null` (a line converts at most once = idempotency; NULL — the vast majority — unconstrained). No authenticated INSERT grant needed for the new column — the definer RPC owns the insert. **RPC `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan(p_plan_id, p_line_ids uuid[])`** (mig `20260809001100`): gate PM/super/director/**procurement** (membership skipped for procurement); plan must be **`approved`** (else 22023); loops the selected lines, **born-`approved` PRs** (status='approved', `approved_by = the plan's approver`, decided_at=now, reason_code NULL, source 'app', requested_by=auth.uid(), supply_plan_line_id=line) — **inherits the plan's PD approval, no per-PR re-approval**; **skips already-linked lines** (idempotent, returns the count); **whole-project lines (null WP) → 22023** (a PR is raised against a WP). **Born-approved is SAFE — verified the triggers:** the only AFTER-INSERT trigger `notify_pr_created` fires `when (new.status = 'requested')` so a born-approved PR sends NO false "needs approval" notification; every other purchase_requests trigger (audit_decision/cancellation/appsheet, derive, set_updated_at, notify_status_change) is AFTER/BEFORE **UPDATE** → none fire on insert. **`auth.uid()` inside SECURITY DEFINER returns the CALLING user** (procurement), not the owner — JWT claims are session context, role is what DEFINER swaps (same as approve_supply_plan). **Accuracy fix (the design catch):** `supply_plan_accuracy` (CREATE OR REPLACE, grants preserved) gains `and r.supply_plan_line_id is null` in the prs CTE — **plan-generated PRs (reason NULL but PLANNED) are excluded from the reactive/untagged counts**, else every planned order would read as a planning miss. **Action `generatePlanPurchaseRequests`** relays the RPC (maps 42501/22023; revalidates /supply-plan + /requests). pgTAP `191`: procurement generates 2 → born-approved + approved_by=PD; idempotent re-gen → 0; null-WP → 22023; non-approved plan → 22023; **accuracy: the 2 generated PRs excluded from untagged (0), the 1 reactive PR still counts as unplanned_miss (1)**. Generated PRs flow into the existing create_purchase_order (operates on approved PRs) = procurement's price-compare/PO step. **NEXT = U4** — the convert-mode UI (checkboxes on an approved plan's lines + สร้างคำขอซื้อ, per-row PR-แล้ว badge), per the mockup.

## Spec 181 — Supply Plan bulk PR, U4: the convert-mode UI (ARC COMPLETE) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23. SPEC 181 ARC COMPLETE (U1–U4).** lint · typecheck · **vitest 217 files / 1453 (+3: SupplyPlanManager convert mode)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U3 `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan` RPC + a page-side converted-set read) → no migration, no db:test delta. The user-facing payoff: on an **approved** plan, select lines and fire the PRs. **`SupplyPlanManager` convert mode** (`planStatus === 'approved'`): the saved-lines list gains a leading **checkbox** for each convertible line (WP-bound + not yet converted), a trailing **`สร้าง PR แล้ว`** badge for already-converted lines, and a **`ต้องระบุงานก่อน`** note for whole-project (null-WP) lines; below, a **`สร้างคำขอซื้อจากแผน`** bar with **เลือกทั้งหมด** + **`สร้างคำขอซื้อ (N)`** → `generatePlanPurchaseRequests({projectId, planId, lineIds})` → clears the selection + a `สร้างคำขอซื้อแล้ว N รายการ` status + refresh. Everyone on the page can generate (the page gate = SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES = exactly the RPC's generate gate). **Page** (`/projects/[id]/supply-plan`): `PlanLine` gains `converted: boolean` — after loading the lines it reads `purchase_requests.supply_plan_line_id IN (lineIds)` (procurement/PM via the privileged PR SELECT) → marks each line converted (idempotent badge + excluded from selection). **Tests:** the convert describe (badge on a converted line, no checkbox for converted/whole-project, select → `generatePlanPurchaseRequests` shape `{projectId, planId, lineIds:["lc"]}`, select-all, no convert UI on a draft plan). **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **THE BULK-PR ARC IS DONE end-to-end:** procurement (PM's stead) plans in the inline grid (U2) → PD approves → procurement checks the approved lines and fires born-approved PRs (U3 engine, U4 UI) → they flow into the existing price-compare/PO step; plan-linked PRs stay out of the PM-accuracy metric. **Flagged follow-ups:** swap the grid's grouped-`<select>` item cell to the spec-180 searchable picker; paste-from-spreadsheet import; whole-project (null-WP) line conversion; the convert UI as a proper desktop table (mockup) vs the current responsive list.

## Spec 181 — Supply Plan bulk PR, U5: the missing nav door for procurement (2026-06-23)

Status: **U5 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change, single-file nav fix.** Operator: "procurement said they cannot find the menu." Root cause = the spec-171-U4 "access without a door" lesson, again: U1 admitted procurement to the `/projects/[id]/supply-plan` page + RPCs, but the **แผนจัดหา chip on the project page was inside `{isManagerRole(ctx.role)}`** (PM/super/director) — and **procurement is NOT a manager role**, so it never saw the link. The whole path otherwise already existed: procurement hub → `โครงการ` (`/projects`, PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES) → a project page (PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES, spec 173) → … but the supply-plan chip was the dead end. **Fix (`projects/[projectId]/page.tsx`):** `const canPlanSupply = SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES.includes(ctx.role)` (mirrors `canOpenSchedule`) + the แผนจัดหา `<Link>` MOVED OUT of the manager-only block into its own `{canPlanSupply ? … : null}` door (placed right after the schedule chip). The reports/settings chips stay manager-only. No project-page test pins these chips (none exists), so no test delta. **LESSON re-logged (3rd time — spec 171 U4, this): admitting a role to a PAGE + its RPCs is HALF the job — also surface the NAV chip/link that reaches it, and check whether it's bundled inside an `isManagerRole`/PM-only block that excludes the new role.** **SPEC 181 NOW REACHABLE end-to-end for procurement.**

## Spec 182 — Procurement price comparison, U1: quote capture + compare table (2026-06-23)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 218 files / 1457 (+4: price-comparison)** · **db:test 141 files / 2597 / 0** (+1 file 192, +13 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001200`; db:types regenerated (+`purchase_quotes` table + 2 RPCs). Procurement's price comparison was 100% offline (pick one supplier, type the amount into the PO; losing quotes vanish). U1 = structured quote capture + a ranked compare table. **DB — `purchase_quotes`** (mig `20260809001200`): PR + supplier + `unit_price numeric(12,2) >= 0` + note; **UNIQUE (purchase_request_id, supplier_id)** (one quote per supplier; re-quote = remove+re-add). **Money posture (unit_price is sensitive — more than PO existence):** RLS SELECT to **back-office only (PM/procurement/super_admin/project_director), NOT site_admin**; no write policy. RPCs `add_purchase_quote` (back-office gate; PR must be **`approved`** = the pre-PO sourcing window; dup supplier → 23505; unknown PR/supplier or non-approved → 22023) + `remove_purchase_quote`. **THE project_director RIDE-ALONG TRAP — caught by db:test file 91 (3rd time this session): a policy/RPC naming `project_manager` MUST also name `project_director`.** My first cut listed `('project_manager','procurement','super_admin')` → file 91 RED (2 fails). Fixed forward: added `project_director` to the policy + both RPCs, **re-applied to live via `supabase db query --linked --file`** (ALTER POLICY + CREATE OR REPLACE the 2 funcs) since the migration was already pushed, AND edited the migration file so a fresh setup is correct. **Action** `addPurchaseQuote`/`removePurchaseQuote` (maps 23505/42501/22023; trap: the RPC `p_note` param is typed `string` non-null → pass `?? ""` not `?? null`). **UI — `PriceComparison`** (`price-comparison.tsx`): ranks quotes cheapest-first, cheapest row highlighted + `ถูกสุด`, total = unit_price × qty, `+N%` over the cheapest; an add form (supplier `<select>` of NOT-yet-quoted suppliers + ราคาต่อหน่วย) + per-row remove. **Wired into `/requests/[requestId]`** in the `isBackOffice && status==='approved'` branch, ABOVE `CreatePoFromRequestButton` (compare → then order); the page fetches the PR's quotes (supplier-name embed, ordered by price) only for that branch. **Tests:** pgTAP 192 (back-office add/read/remove, dup→23505, non-approved→22023, **site_admin can neither add (42501) nor READ (RLS count 0)**); `price-comparison.test.tsx` (rank+total+% , add offers only un-quoted suppliers, remove, empty state). **NEXT = U2** (pick a quote → prefill `create_purchase_order` with that supplier + amount), then U3 (price-history benchmark via the spec-179 catalog link), U4 (per-quote doc attachment).

## Spec 182 — Procurement price comparison, U2: pick winner → PO (2026-06-23)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 218 files / 1459 (+2: pick→PO)** · `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses `create_purchase_order` + the U1 quotes) → no migration, no db:test delta. The winning quote now flows into the PO. **`CreatePurchaseOrderSheet`** gained `defaultSupplierId` + `defaultAmounts` props (seeded into the initial `supplierId`/`amounts` state; the caller **remounts via `key`** on a new pick — avoids the setState-in-effect React-Compiler lint). **`PriceComparison`** now owns the create-PO action: each quote row gets a **pick radio** (defaults to the cheapest; the picked row gets `border-action bg-action-soft`), and a **`สร้างใบสั่งซื้อจาก {supplier}`** button opens the sheet **prefilled** with the picked supplier + `defaultAmounts = { [line.id]: net total }` (net = unit_price × qty; the sheet's default VAT mode is already `exclusive` = net+7%, matching Thai net quotes). With no quotes the button opens the sheet blank (same as before). **`/requests/[requestId]`** now renders `PriceComparison` with the PR-as-`line` and **drops the standalone `CreatePoFromRequestButton`** (PriceComparison owns it) — the approved-state action is unified. **exactOptionalPropertyTypes trap:** passing `defaultSupplierId={picked?.supplierId}` (string|undefined) into a `?: string` prop → TS2375; fixed by typing the prop `?: string | undefined`. **Test:** the PO sheet is mocked to a stub exposing the prefill props; default-pick = cheapest (s1, ฿4,600), re-pick the dearer quote → that supplier + total (s2, ฿4,900). **NEXT = U3** (price-history benchmark via the spec-179 catalog link), then U4 (per-quote doc attachment).

## Spec 182 — Procurement price comparison, U3: last-paid benchmark (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 218 files / 1461 (+2: price-history benchmark)** · **db:test 142 files / 2607 / 0** (+1 file 193, +10 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001300`; db:types regenerated (+`item_price_history` RPC). The "what did we pay last time" reference above the quote table. **DB — `item_price_history(p_catalog_item_id uuid)`** (mig `20260809001300`): SECURITY DEFINER table-returning read → recent PURCHASED `purchase_requests` for a catalog item (the spec-179 link) → `supplier_name` (coalesce suppliers.name, the text snapshot), **NET unit price** (`round(amount/(1+vat_rate/100)/nullif(qty,0), 2)` — amount is the line GROSS per spec 119, so net = apples-to-apples with the net quotes), qty, purchased_at; `where amount is not null and quantity > 0`, **order by `coalesce(purchased_at, requested_at) desc` limit 5**. **Money posture (ADR 0038, same as purchase_quotes):** back-office role gate (PM/procurement/super/**project_director** — the file-91 ride-along, named correctly first try this time) → site_admin + anon get nothing; DEFINER also lets the cross-project price reference see purchases beyond the caller's membership (a price benchmark is org-wide). **UI — `PriceComparison`** gains an optional `history: ItemPriceHistory[]` prop (default `[]` → U1/U2 tests untouched); when non-empty it renders **`เคยซื้อล่าสุด ฿X/{unit} จาก Y · N ครั้ง`** above the เปรียบเทียบราคา heading (X/Y from `history[0]` = newest; N = `history.length`). **Page** (`/requests/[requestId]`): the select gains `catalog_item_id`; the history is fetched (one `rpc("item_price_history")`) only in the `isBackOffice && status==='approved' && catalog_item_id` case (off-catalog PRs have no axis → no line) and passed to `PriceComparison`. **Tests:** pgTAP 193 (shape + SECURITY DEFINER + anon-no-exec; **site_admin → 42501**; count excludes the amount-null PR + the other-item PR; net with VAT 0 = 98, with VAT 7% = 100, a null-supplier_id row coalesces to the text snapshot = 100; no null nets; `results_eq` on `purchased_at` proves newest-first); component test (benchmark line shows newest price/supplier/`N ครั้ง`; no line when history empty). **NET decision (no operator ask needed):** quotes are NET so last-paid is computed NET for a fair compare; the line uses the actual `unit` (matching the quote rows) not the literal "หน่วย". **NEXT = U4** (per-quote doc attachment: `quote` attachment purpose + `quote_id` FK).

## Spec 182 — Procurement price comparison, U4: per-quote source document (ARC COMPLETE) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23. SPEC 182 ARC COMPLETE (U1–U4).** lint · typecheck · **vitest 218 files / 1463 (+2: quote-doc render)** · **db:test 143 files / 2619 / 0** (+1 file 194, +12 asserts; +pin updates in file 20) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001400`+`20260809001500`; db:types regenerated (+`quote_id` column on attachments + the view). Each supplier quote (U1) now carries its source quotation doc (the 📎). **DB — mig `…001400`** adds the `quote` value to `purchase_request_attachment_purpose` (own migration — ADD VALUE can't be referenced same-txn, the spec-66 invoice precedent). **mig `…001500`:** `purchase_request_attachments.quote_id` (nullable FK → `purchase_quotes`, **ON DELETE CASCADE** — matches the sibling PR FK; both dormant outside the admin hard-delete) + `pra_quote_id_shape` CHECK (quote_id only on quote rows) + column-scoped `grant insert(quote_id)` (the spec-179 new-column lesson); the INSERT policy gains a **quote arm** (back-office only — NOT site_admin; PR `approved`; quote on THIS PR); a **RESTRICTIVE select** gates quote-purpose rows to back-office (money — a quotation shows prices); the storage upload policy widened (+procurement/+director, +`approved` status arm); the `_current` view appends `quote_id`. **Action `addQuoteAttachment`** mirrors `addInvoiceAttachment` (idempotent 23505 replay; purpose='quote'+quote_id; server rebuilds the path). **UI:** new `QuoteDocAttach` (per-row uploader, the InvoiceUploader flow) — a doc-less quote row shows the attach control, a doc'd row shows a `เอกสาร` link; **remove is disabled on a doc'd quote** (append-only + FK → not hard-removable; kept for audit, aligned with "losing quotes are kept"). The page fetches quote docs (`purpose='quote'` from `_current`, signed via the pr-attachments bucket) → `quoteId → url` map passed to `PriceComparison`. **Per-row attach (not mid-add)** chosen for robustness + it matches the spec doc's "📎 per quote row"; the task's "in the add form" phrasing is an impl hint. **TRAPS caught by db:test (all forward-fixed; the corrected INSERT policy re-applied to live via `supabase db query --linked --file` since `…001500` was already pushed):** (1) **name-capture** — `q.purchase_request_id = purchase_request_id` bound the unqualified RHS to `q` (purchase_quotes has that column!) → always true → a cross-PR quote could be linked; qualified to `purchase_request_attachments.purchase_request_id` (the hazard every attachments-policy header warns about). (2) **bare eval-once** — wrote `current_user_role()`/`auth.uid()` bare → pgTAP file 40 RED; wrapped as `(select …)` (pg_get_expr drops the `public.` so `(select public.current_user_role())` still satisfies the lint — file 40 stayed the model). (3) **two file-20 pins** my change legitimately invalidates — the purpose-enum label set (+quote) + `policies_are` (the 4th, restrictive, policy); updated both. (4) **spec-143 membership** — the pgTAP fixture's site_admin needed a `project_members` row to read the parent PR (procurement keeps its cross-project read). **Verified:** procurement attaches on an approved PR + reads; site_admin can neither attach (42501) nor read the quote doc (RESTRICTIVE, incl. via the view) but still sees the parent's reference row; a cross-PR quote_id is rejected. **THE PRICE-COMPARISON ARC IS DONE end-to-end:** capture N supplier quotes (U1) → rank + pick → prefill the PO (U2) → last-paid benchmark from history (U3) → each quote carries its source doc (U4). **Flagged follow-ups:** removing/replacing an attached quote doc (the doc-removal path is out of U4 scope — a doc'd quote is currently immutable); a desktop table layout; quote expiry/validity dates.

## Spec 178 — Store margin layer, U3: Store P&L read (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **db:test 144 files / 2633 / 0** (+1 file 195, +14 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001600`; db:types regenerated (+`store_pnl`, app + worker). **Renumbered around the concurrent spec-179/181/182 session** (it took migs `…000800`–`…001500` + pgTAP 188–194, all merged to main while I was paused; U1+U2 stayed ancestors of HEAD `b20f3554`) → my mig = `…001600`, pgTAP = 195. The margin layer becomes observable: `store_pnl(p_project_id)` returns **per-item** rows — `qty_issued`, `cost_total` (Σ total_cost), `sell_total` (Σ **`coalesce(total_sell, total_cost)`** so unpriced/legacy issues count at cost), `margin` (sell−cost), `shrinkage_value` (Σ `stock_counts.variance_value`). **Reversed issues excluded** (anti-join `stock_reversals` on `issue_id` — a reversed เบิก never charged the WP). FULL OUTER JOIN of per-item issue aggregates + per-item count aggregates → an item that was only counted (pure shrinkage) still appears. **Money gate super_admin / project_director** (mirrors `wp_profit` — NOT procurement, NOT site_admin/PM); SECURITY DEFINER, called on the user session (admin client has no JWT → 42501). Unknown project → 22023. pgTAP 195 (14): structure + anon-deny + per-item cost/sell/margin/shrinkage + unpriced-at-cost + reversed-excluded + director-allowed + PM/procurement-denied + unknown-project. Clean first push. NEXT = U4 flip `wp_profit` (fold Σ total_sell of non-reversed issues into the materials line).

## Spec 178 — Store margin layer, U4: flip wp_profit (the keystone) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 1463** · **db:test 145 files / 2637 / 0** (+1 file 196, +4 asserts) · `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001700`; **db:types ZERO drift** (same return signature). The transfer-pricing payoff lands: `wp_profit`'s `materials_cost` now = the GL acct-1400 purchase cost (unchanged) **PLUS** the per-WP `Σ coalesce(total_sell, total_cost)` of **non-reversed** `stock_issues` — the store charges the WP its SELL price for material drawn from the store. **Disjoint sources** (store issues are never posted to the GL) → additive, no double-count; a WP pays sell price for store stock AND purchase price for direct-to-WP buys. Reversed issues excluded (anti-join `stock_reversals` on `issue_id`). **Body sourced from the LIVE `pg_get_functiondef`** (confirmed unchanged by the concurrent session), not an old migration — the recurring overload/dropped-gate trap; **SAME return signature → CREATE OR REPLACE (body-only)**, so grants + the pgTAP **102** structure pins + the 90/91 gate invariants (no PM ref) all hold, and `settle_project` banks the new profit automatically (it reads `wp_profit.profit`). pgTAP 196 (4): WP-with-store-issues materials = store-sell 520 (priced 500 + unpriced-at-cost 20; reversed 5000 excluded) · profit = budget 1000 − 520 · a WP with no store draws unchanged (per-WP, no leak) · labor_sell still 0 (only the materials line folds). **102/104/105 stayed green** (their fixtures have no `stock_issues` on the tested WPs → the new term = 0). Clean first push. **wp_profit now reflects the store as a transfer-pricing BU. The cost-first dial is unlocked end-to-end (engine).** NEXT = U5 sell-rate setter UI (super-only) · U6 store-margin UI on /store · then Stream B mop-up.

## Spec 178 — Store margin layer, U5: the sell-rate setter UI (super-only) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U5 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 219 files / 1466 (+3: set-sell-rate)** · `pnpm build` green. **Code-only — no DB / migration / db:test delta** (reuses the U1 `set_item_sell_rate` RPC). The first margin-layer UI: the operator can finally set the per-item sell rate. **Home = `/catalog`** (the item registry — sell rate is a global per-item attribute). **Super_admin ONLY, money-locked end to end:** the rate is read on the page **via the admin client** and **only when `ctx.role === "super_admin"`** (the nova money pattern — `item_sell_rates` is zero authenticated grant, so the rate never reaches the client for procurement/PM/director); written via the new **`setItemSellRate`** action (`requireRole(["super_admin"])` + `set_item_sell_rate` on the RLS server client so the definer super gate resolves; maps 42501/22023). New **`SetSellRate`** client control (per row, gated on a `canSetSellRate` prop) — shows `฿{rate}` when set or `ตั้งราคาขาย` when unset → a BottomSheet number input → `setItemSellRate` → refresh. `CatalogList` += `canSetSellRate` prop + `sellRate?` on `CatalogItem` (omitted for non-super via conditional spread — the rate doesn't leave the server). Labels `ITEM_SELL_RATE_LABEL`/`SET_ITEM_SELL_RATE_LABEL` in labels.ts. **2 traps:** (1) `getByLabelText(/ราคาขาย/)` matched BOTH the input AND the BottomSheet dialog (its `aria-labelledby` → title `ตั้งราคาขาย` contains `ราคาขาย`) → query the number input by `getByRole("spinbutton")`. (2) `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` rejected the explicit `undefined` on optional `sellRate` → omit the key via conditional spread (the spec-177-U2 lesson). Browser pass = operator on-device (auth-gated). NEXT = U6 store-margin UI on /store · then Stream B mop-up.

## Spec 178 — Store margin layer, U6: the Store P&L view on /store (ARC COMPLETE) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U6 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23. SPEC 178 STREAM A (margin layer) ARC COMPLETE (U1–U6).** lint · typecheck · **vitest 220 files / 1468 (+2: store-pnl-view)** · `pnpm build` green. **Code-only — no DB / migration / db:test delta** (reads the U3 `store_pnl` RPC). The margin layer is now visible: /store shows a **กำไร-ขาดทุนสโตร์** section — per item (เบิก qty · ต้นทุน · ยอดขาย · margin, + ของขาด-เกิน when a count varies) and the **project net = Σ margin + Σ shrinkage** (กำไรสุทธิ). **super_admin / project_director ONLY** (`canSeePnl`, mirrors the `store_pnl` RPC gate — procurement/PM/site_admin never see margins); rendered only when a project is selected. **Page** calls `store_pnl(project)` on the user session (definer gate resolves via JWT) and resolves item names separately (covers since-deactivated items). New presentational **`StorePnlView`** (pure server component, no `'use client'`) + `STORE_PNL_LABEL`. Browser pass = operator on-device (auth-gated). **STREAM A DONE: U1 sell-rate data → U2 issue snapshots sell → U3 store_pnl read → U4 wp_profit fold → U5 sell-rate setter UI → U6 store P&L view. The store is a transfer-pricing BU end to end (engine + screens).** NEXT = **Stream B** cost-side mop-up (deferred items): WP-detail reversal controls, site_admin count surface, count-history view, /store-manager receiver picker, manager confirm-on-behalf, GL posting of stock movements.

## Spec 178 — Stream B (cost-side mop-up), B1: WP-detail reversal controls (2026-06-23)

Status: **B1 SHIPPED to prod — 2026-06-23.** lint · typecheck · **vitest 220 files / 1470 (+2: wp-issue-stock reversal)** · `pnpm build` green. **Code-only — no DB** (reuses the spec-177-U11 `reverse_stock_issue` RPC + the U12 `reverseStockIssue` action). Closes a real gap: a site_admin who drew stock from the WP detail (`WpIssueStock`) could not undo it there — only managers could, and only from /store.
